<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227148264 space=MD2024xR3 version=15 -->
## PAGE 00013: 2024x Refresh3 Version News

- page_id: `227148264`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148264/2024x+Refresh3+Version+News
- version_number: 15
- version_date: `2025-07-04T13:20:37.222+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Released on: July 11, 2025

The 2024x Refresh3 release introduces key enhancements to collaborative modeling on both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Teamwork Cloud now supports partial project usages as a technology preview, enabling more flexible reuse of specific project parts, along with improved SSO support across command-line tools. On the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform, standard profile migration has been simplified, and project opening performance with local cache has been significantly improved. Both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform will benefit from a new option to detect changes in derived properties during project merging or comparison.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024xR3']. For further information, check the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space=''].

****

****

- 
  - 
  - 
- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
- 

### miModeling and Infrastructure

- You can now disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing Usage Analytics information' space='']>>]]>
- This release introduces the ability to install custom browser extensions in JxBrowser.

### Collaboration

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages (Technology Preview)

We’re excited to introduce a significant enhancement to project usages, now available as a **technology preview**. Previously, you could only use an entire project (full usage), but with this release, you can define a usage scope and use only a specific part of a project. Additionally, you can seamlessly switch between full and partial usage at any time and adjust the usage scope as needed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting the scope of a partial project usage.

##### Improvements to Single Sign-On Authentication

For improved security, the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard now only accepts access tokens generated by your user when using single sign-on (SSO) authentication. Additionally, SSO is now supported in other command-line tools, like Report Wizard and Cameo Collaborator.

#### **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

##### Easier Standard Profile Migration

The standard profile migration has been simplified allowing you to migrate all standard profiles in one go. Now, you can use the project migration dialogto select the profiles from both the server and your modeling tool installation. During migration:

- Standard profiles stored in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform are migrated.
- Standard profiles existing in the modeling tool installation but not yet added to the the server can be selected and added to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
- Any dependent profiles are also migrated or added.

##### Performance Improvements

In version 2024x Refresh3, project opening performance has been significantly improved when a local cache is already present on your machine, as demonstrated in the chart below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparison of project load times with local cache.

##### Other Improvements

- The following predefined templates have been introduced for publishing Cameo Collaborator documents:
  - UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator
- You can now specify the project branch, revision, and iteration when logged in via the command line interface.

#### General Collaboration Improvements

- The new Detect Changes in Derived Properties environment option allows you to specify which derived properties to compare when detecting changes during project merge or compare.

**Version news of servers and plugins**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh3 Version News' space='SYSMLP2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh3 Version News' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh3 Version News' space='CDH2024xR3']

**resourcesMagicDraw Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='']

**News of earlier versions**

- Product News Archive

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86c1e37a-6f77-4e5e-aa46-ea5b738c8c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mi</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Modeling and Infrastructure</h1><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sharing Usage Analytics information" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="a57a59c9-bea4-45bb-a3c4-8f324aa06907"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This release introduces the ability to install custom browser extensions in JxBrowser.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1><span>Collaboration</span></h1><h2><span>Teamwork Cloud</span></h2><h3>Partial Project Usages (Technology Preview)</h3><p>We’re excited to introduce a significant enhancement to project usages, now available as a <strong>technology preview</strong>. Previously, you could only use an entire project (full usage), but with this release, you can define a usage scope and use only a specific part of a project. Additionally, you can seamlessly switch between full and partial usage at any time and adjust the usage scope as needed.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="partial_project_usages.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting the scope of a partial project usage.</h6><h3>Improvements to Single Sign-On Authentication</h3><p>For improved security, the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard now only accepts access tokens generated by your user when using single sign-on (SSO) authentication. Additionally, SSO is now supported in other command-line tools, like Report Wizard and Cameo Collaborator.</p><h2><strong>3D</strong><span>EXPERIENCE Platform</span></h2><h3>Easier Standard Profile Migration</h3><p>The standard profile migration has been simplified allowing you to migrate all standard profiles in one go. Now, you can use the project migration <span>dialog </span>to select the profiles from both the server and your modeling tool installation. During migration:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ea592f23-9f7c-4e00-8805-a2f2fa6e27b0">Standard profiles stored in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform are migrated.</li><li data-uuid="d9e30fd1-ed77-4cb2-affc-71a0b3ff23bd">Standard profiles existing in the modeling tool installation but not yet added to the the server can be selected and added to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</li><li data-uuid="91b20f41-ba64-4b8b-b473-d125863b86b2">Any dependent profiles are also migrated or added.</li></ul><h3>Performance Improvements</h3><p>I<span>n version 2024x Refresh3, project opening performance has been significantly improved when a local cache is already present on your machine, as demonstrated in the chart below.<br /><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_opening_performance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Comparison of project load times with local cache.</h6><h3>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="487bc44b-0634-4d29-aaf4-0e63f90e43a3">The following predefined templates have been introduced for publishing Cameo Collaborator documents:<ul><li>UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator</li><li>UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator</li><li>BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="0f39613b-095e-4c4c-b29f-1157d4bfc2fc">You can now specify the project branch, revision, and iteration when logged in via the command line interface.</li></ul><h2>General Collaboration Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="2a516fc5-22a0-490c-9293-8c3ddd45fabe">The new <strong>Detect Changes in Derived Properties </strong>environment option allows you to specify which derived properties to compare when detecting changes during project merge or compare.</li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.01em;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Version news of servers and plugins</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR3" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh3 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud 2024x Refresh3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh3 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2024x Refresh3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh3 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2024x Refresh3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh3 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF 1.2 Plugin 2024x Refresh3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2024xR3" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh3 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo DataHub 2024x Refresh3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb63ce8f-8c35-4c05-ab0e-c34c56a1bf70"><ac:parameter ac:name="">resources</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>MagicDraw Documentation</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="MagicDraw Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw 2024x Refresh3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>News of earlier versions</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw 2024x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw 2024x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="2024x Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw 2024x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/NMDOC/MagicDraw">Product News Archive</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00014: 2024x Version News

- page_id: `227148329`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148329/2024x+Version+News
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:22.416+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

648293284

INLINE

100.00002%

648293298

INLINE

648293285

INLINE

top

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Released on: November 10, 2023

The 2024x release introduces general modeling and infrastructure, as well as collaborative modeling enhancements.

For general modeling and infrastructure, this release presents the DSLS licensing option, the ability to zoom the Modeling Browser using keyboard shortcuts, and additional Legend adornment properties in Tables. Additionally, OpenJDK is now updated to version 17.0.8+7, CentOS Linux 7 is now replaced with Oracle Linux 8.8 support, and the JavaScript Rhino engine is now upgraded from 1.7R4 to the 1.7.13 version.

Several features have been discontinued with the 2024x version, such as the JavaScript Nashorn language usage, Record Macro functionality, and the SPEM plugin.

We have exciting news for collaborative modeling, as well. The new file exchange format .szip is now out of technology preview and was improved with a UI dialog for ease of use. Also, we introduce a major new feature: the Model Patch mechanism, a significantly lighter solution to move project changes. There are enhancements for 3DEXPERIENCE projects, too; you can now manage used projects more easily and open certain projects in a read-only mode.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024x']. For further information, check the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2024x'].

1994146009

###### Discontinued FeaturesDiscontinued Features

The following features have been discontinued with the 2024x version release.

##### JavaScript Nashorn Scripting

The JavaScriptNashorn language usage has been removed from the modeling tool. Please use JavaScript*Rhino* instead*.*This affects expression-related functionality, e.g., custom validation rules, derived properties, smart packages, tables, and macros.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn' space='']>]]>

##### Record Macro Functionality

The Record Macro functionality has been discontinued. However, any previously recorded macros can still be used in the modeling tool.

##### SPEM Plugin

The SPEM 2.0 Plugin (16.8 and 16.9 versions) has been discontinued.

649041710

INLINE

###### miModeling and Infrastructure

##### DSLS LicensingDSLS Licensing

Starting with the 2024x release, we are introducing a new licensing option - DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server). DSLS is a licensing system developed by Dassault Systèmes that provides these benefits:

- License usage statistics, debugging, and simple UI to administer and view statistics.
- Licenses are bound to their expiration date rather than the product version.
- Nodelock (seat), floating, and managed licensing options.

Thus, now you can choose to use either FlexNet or DSLS for product licensing.

The DSLS version must be compatible with the modeling tool version you use (currently, DSLS version R2024x with modeling tool version 2024x).

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DSLS installation and licensing' space='IL2024xR3']>]]>

##### Zoom in Model BrowserZoom in Model Browser

From now on, you can easily increase the size of the displayed data in the Model Browser tabs. Zoom in and out using keyboard shortcuts to modify the view to your liking.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Zooming in the Model Browser using keyboard shortcuts.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the Model Browser' space='']>]]>

##### Advanced Legend Adornments in TablesAdvanced Legend Adornments in Tables

In addition to using Legend Items' Fill Color property to color table cells, now you can also add and color cell borders via the Pen Color and Line Width properties, as well as change the color of the text via the Text Color property, allowing you more options to emphasize specific data rows in tables.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Text Color, Pen Color, and Line Width adornment properties are specified for the Legend Item *In Progress*.

##### Other ImprovementsOther Improvements

- OpenJDK version update. Desktop applications (MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect) and Teamwork Cloud are now only run using OpenJDK 17.0.8+7 version.
- Supported operating system changes for Linux. CentOS Linux 7 has reached End of Life; thus, it is no longer supported. It is now replaced with Oracle Linux 8.8 support.
- JavaScript Rhino version update. The JavaScript Rhino engine is now upgraded from 1.7R4 to the 1.7.13 version.
- It is no longer necessary to display the pin's type when displaying its multiplicity due to the newly introduced Show Multiplicity symbol property.

2139947249

INLINE

###### CollaborationCollaboration

##### TWCTeamwork Cloud

###### New File Exchange Format for Server Projects

In version 2022x Refresh2, we introduced **.szip** – a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using server project file format' space='']. It addresses the issues of slow performance and project structural changes associated with the .mdzip format. Using .szip, you can export and import projects more quickly without altering the project structure.

In version 2024x, we further improved the .szip format by introducing a new UI dialog that allows you to select which projects you want to import to the server and which to skip. This gives you greater control over the file exchange process.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The charts below depict how the .szip project format increases the performance of project export and import operations when compared to .mdzip. Depending on the project size element and used project count-wise, the performance gain for export can vary between 2-20 times, while the project import speed has been increased by roughly 40%.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparing the import and export performance of Project #1 (1M elements, 57 Used Projects), Project #2 (2M elements, 7 Used Projects), and Project #3 (2M+ elements, 3 Used Projects).

###### Model Patch Mechanism (Technology Preview)

We introduce the technology preview of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Patch' space=''], a more lightweight way to move model changes. Model Patch can help you:

- Move changes from one branch to another without the need to perform a full Merge operation, which can be slow and cumbersome;
- Transfer only a specific scope of model changes between disconnected teams instead of the entire model;
- Reuse only the necessary subset of an external model as an alternative to bringing in the whole model as a Used Project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### PowerPower’By

###### New Features to Manage Used Projects

We added new features that provide you with better capabilities to manage used projects in **3D**EXPERIENCE:

- Export packages as a new server project;
- Move elements to/from used projects;
- Import used projects.

###### Opening Projects in Read-Only Mode

Beginning with version 2024x, you can open **3D**EXPERIENCE projects in Read-Only mode. Projects open in this mode with a balloon notification if you do not have the necessary access rights or if the project is in Read-Only mode because of its maturity state.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Notification informing the user that the **3D**EXPERIENCE project is read-only.

Progress</em>.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><h3 style="text-align: left;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="672ef41c-9f3f-4bde-add8-61dd2944ee47"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5a26372f-78b5-4ce6-bf52-87d848d0d122">OpenJDK version update.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong>Desktop applications (MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect) and Teamwork Cloud are now only run using OpenJDK 17.0.8+7 version. </li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Supported operating system changes for Linux. </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">CentOS Linux 7 has reached End of Life; thus, it is no longer supported. It is now replaced with Oracle Linux 8.8 support. </span></li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong>JavaScript Rhino version update.</strong> The JavaScript Rhino engine is now upgraded from 1.7R4 to the 1.7.13 version.</li><li>It is no longer necessary to display the pin's type when displaying its multiplicity due to the newly introduced Show Multiplicity symbol property.</li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3bfd317b-5a37-4665-8e30-10b1fed74474"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2139947249</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h5><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2a3e277-67ff-49eb-8666-39ed7c6d17c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Collaboration</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h5><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82578d71-581b-42b4-bfdc-d5dcd49ff7cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Teamwork Cloud</h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c2ca0cb-2ea5-4912-bec7-86429a10563a">New File Exchange Format for Server Projects</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p>In version 2022x Refresh2, we introduced <strong>.szip</strong> – a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using server project file format" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[server project exchange format]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It addresses the issues of slow performance and project structural changes associated with the .mdzip format. Using .szip, you can export and import projects more quickly without altering the project structure.</p><p>In version 2024x, we further improved the .szip format by introducing a new UI dialog that allows you to select which projects you want to import to the server and which to skip. This gives you greater control over the file exchange process.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_exchange_options.png" /></ac:image></h6><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The charts below depict how the .szip project format increases the performance of project export and import operations when compared to .mdzip. Depending on the project size element and used project count-wise, the performance gain for export can vary between 2-20 times, while the project import speed has been increased by roughly 40%.</span></p><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;"><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 0.0in;text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="szip_project_import_performance.png" /></ac:image><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="szip_project_export_performance.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Comparing the import and export performance of Project #1 (1M elements, 57 Used Projects), Project #2 (2M elements, 7 Used Projects), and Project #3 (2M+ elements, 3 Used Projects).</h6><p><br /></p><h4>Model Patch Mechanism (Technology Preview)</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">We introduce the technology preview of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Patch" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Patch mechanism]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, a more lightweight way to move model changes. Model Patch can help you:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Move changes from one branch to another without the need to perform a full Merge operation, which can be slow and cumbersome;</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Transfer only a specific scope of model changes between disconnected teams instead of the entire model;</span></li><li>Reuse only the necessary subset of an external model as an alternative to bringing in the whole model as a Used Project.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_patch_mechanism.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfdd0694-20ca-468f-858e-b8118a0d2206"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Power</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Power’By</h3><h4>New Features to Manage Used Projects</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">We added new features that provide you with better capabilities to manage used projects in <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Export packages as a new server project;</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Move elements to/from used projects;</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Import used projects.</span></li></ul><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Opening Projects in Read-Only Mode</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Beginning with version 2024x, you can open <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c7319731-fdab-4bb0-85c6-0548eef6b250">3D</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c7319731-fdab-4bb0-85c6-0548eef6b250">EXPERIENCE projects</ac:inline-comment-marker> in Read-Only mode. Projects open in this mode with a balloon notification if you do not have the necessary access rights or if the project is in Read-Only mode because of its maturity state.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:height="96"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DEXPERIENCE_project_editing_disabled.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Notification informing the user that the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE project is read-only.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58d3ee8c-ddb5-419a-a63b-ded702d1fbf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">649599800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Version news of servers and plugins</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="2024x Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud 2024x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="2024x Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF 1.2 Plugin 2024x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page 2024x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6a30458-91b6-46f2-89c2-ca01ea5a0a10"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">649619380</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227153055 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00015: 3DEXPERIENCE platform troubleshooting

- page_id: `227153055`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227153055/3DEXPERIENCE+platform+troubleshooting
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-06-26T13:45:02.153+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

4

##### Connectivity issues when the certificate is not trusted

If you cannot connect to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform because the certificate is not trusted, you need to export the certificate from the platform and then import it to the JRE trusted certificates keystore used to run your modeling tool.

To download the certificate file using Microsoft Edge

1. Open the **3D**EXPERIENCE p latform you want to download the certificate from.
2. On the left side of the address bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='ms_edge_icon.png'] .
3. Select Connection is secure and click [ATTACHMENT filename='certificate_icon.png'] .
4. In the Certificate Viewer window, select the Details tab.
5. Select the certificate and click the Export button. [ATTACHMENT filename='microsoft_edge_certificate_viewer.png']

To download the certificate file using Google Chrome

1. Open the **3D**EXPERIENCE p latform you want to download the certificate from.
2. On the left side of the address bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='chrome_icon.png'] and select Connection is secure > Certificate is valid .
3. In the Certificate Viewer window, select the Details tab.
4. Select the certificate and click the Export button. [ATTACHMENT filename='chrome_certificate_viewer.png']

To download the certificate file using sing Mozilla Firefox

1. Open the **3D**EXPERIENCE p latform you want to download the certificate from.
2. On the left side of the address bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='firefox_icon.png'] and select Connection secure > More information.
3. In the Security tab of the Page Info window, click the View Certificate button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Scroll down to the **Miscellaneous**section and click **PEM (cert)** to download the certificate. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To install the certificate to JRE

1. Open the command prompt as an administrator.
2. Go to the JRE security directory located in <install_root>\jre\lib\security .
3. Copy the certificate file to the directory.
4. Run the following keytool command: For more information, see the following:[https://www.grim.se/guide/jre-cert](https://www.grim.se/guide/jre-cert)[https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25951602/adding-an-ssl-certificate-to-jre-in-order-to-access-https-sites](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25951602/adding-an-ssl-certificate-to-jre-in-order-to-access-https-sites)
  - On Windows shell]]>
  - On Mac shell.pem]]>

##### A used project cannot be found

If you try to open a project and get an error saying that a used project or projects are missing, this means that you either do not have sufficient permissions to access the used project or it was deleted.

You can still choose toopen the main project, but the used project or projects will be removed with all references to them:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In cases where the used project is not missing but cannot be opened because of insufficient permissions, we do not recommend cutting references as it may lead to data loss!

##### Project editing is disabled

When you open a project, you may see a balloon notification that project editing is disabled and the project is in read-only mode:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In such cases, check the following:

- if you have access to the collaborative space,
- if the project is shared with you; if so, is it shared with writing access?
- if you have access to write to set the maturity state of the opened revision

<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE p</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">latform</span> you want to download the certificate from.</li><li>On the left side of the address bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="firefox_icon.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Connection secure &gt; More information.</strong></li><li>In the <strong>Security</strong> tab of the <strong>Page Info </strong>window, click the <strong>View Certificate</strong> button.<br /><strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="firefox_page_info_window.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></strong></li><li>Scroll down to the <span class="extension"><span class="info-group-title"><strong>Miscellaneous </strong>section and click <strong>PEM (cert)</strong> to download the certificate.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="firefox_certificate_download.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To install the certificate to JRE</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the command prompt as an administrator.</li><li>Go to the JRE security directory located in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;\jre\lib\security</em>.</li><li>Copy the certificate file to the directory.</li><li data-uuid="1856d31f-6bd6-4b9d-ac90-e8fdcbdfb6d1"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run the following keytool command:</p><ul><li>On Windows<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eface0dc-af08-4881-af6f-8fcb713a371a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[..\..\bin\keytool.exe -import -trustcacerts -keystore cacerts -storepass changeit -noprompt -alias 'DS_Platform' -file .\<cert_file_name>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>On Mac<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b63329e3-bae0-4229-949e-21b8f7a67656"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[../../bin/keytool -import -trustcacerts -keystore cacerts -storepass changeit -noprompt -alias 'DS_Platform' -file ./<cert_file_name>.pem]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87f6c50f-d06e-4faa-a189-91f18b140fc9"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information, see the following:<ul><li data-uuid="81ed5999-5ac3-4384-b474-413b62d129ab"><a class="external-link" style="text-decoration: none;" href="https://www.grim.se/guide/jre-cert" title="Follow link">https://www.grim.se/guide/jre-cert</a></li><li link">https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25951602/adding-an-ssl-certificate-to-jre-in-order-to-access-https-sites</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>A used project cannot be found</h3><p style="text-align: left;">If you try to open a project and get an error saying that a used project or projects are missing, this means that you either do not have sufficient permissions to access the used project or it was deleted.</p><p style="text-align: left;">You can still choose to<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> open the main project, but the used project or projects will be removed with all references to them:</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="199" ac:width="621"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_project_cannot_be_found.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3b99441-fa92-4822-b414-385fe07f0e65"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In cases where the used project is not missing but cannot be opened because of insufficient permissions, we do not recommend cutting references as it may lead to data loss!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Project editing is disabled</h3><p>When you open a project, you may see a balloon notification that project editing is disabled and the project is in read-only mode:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DEXPERIENCE_project_editing_disabled.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p>In such cases, check the following:</p><ul><li>if you have access to the collaborative space,</li><li>if the project is shared with you; if so, is it shared with writing access?</li><li>if you have access to write to set the maturity state of the opened revision</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154814 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00016: @see on documentation

- page_id: `227154814`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154814/see+on+documentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-12T16:55:29.262+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This feature only works in the import-code-only mode.

Documentation with doxygen @see tag is reversed with a link to the model. The documentation should use oneof the following special doxygen tag to describe an @see element.

- @see
- \see
- @sa
- \sa

The documentation must be in the form of “@see element”. Only one element follows @see will be reversed as an element link in model documentation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>This feature only works in the import-code-only mode.</span></p><p><span>Documentation with doxygen @see tag is reversed with a link to the model. The documentation should use one </span>of the following special doxygen tag to describe an @see element.</p><ul><li>@see</li><li><span>\see</span></li><li><span>@sa</span></li><li><span>\sa </span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span>The documentation must be in the form of “@see element”. Only one element follows @see will be reversed as an element link in model docu</span>mentation. </p><p><span><br /></span></p>
````

## PAGE 00020: A Use Case scenario and an Activity diagram mapping schema

- page_id: `227150766`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150766/A+Use+Case+scenario+and+an+Activity+diagram+mapping+schema
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-05-17T08:06:33.848+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879969665

879969667

879969666

To understand the Use Case scenario representation in the Activity diagram, see the following table.

| Use Case Scenario | Mapping in Activity diagram | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| One (the first) step | The Activity with the Activity diagram is created under the owning Use Case just after the first basic flow step is created.The name of the created Activity and Activity diagram is the same as the owning Use Case name.In the Activity diagram, the Call Behavior Action is created for the basic flow step.The Initial Node is created before the Call Behavior Action.The Final Node is created after the Call Behavior Action.The Control Flow relationships are created from the Initial Node to the Call Behavior Action and from the Call Behavior Action to the Final Node. |  |
| steps | The Call Behavior Action is created for each basic flow step.The Initial Node is created before the first Call Behavior Action.The Final Node is created after the last Call Behavior Action.The Control Flow relationships are created from the Initial Node to the first Call Behavior Action, between each Call Behavior Action, and from the last Behavior Action to the Final Node. |  |
|  | The Call Behavior Action is created for the basic flow step of the included Use Case.The Call Behavior Action is inserted to the activity basic flow according to the order it was inserted in the basic flow.The Call Behavior Action is connected with the Control Flow relationships. The Call Behavior Action is not named.The Call Behavior Action has the Activity behavior defined. This Activity name corresponds to the name of the included Use Case.The activity (the Behavior or the Call Behavior Action) is owned by the included Use Case.If the included Use Case has its own Use Case scenario, this scenario is represented in the activity; the Activity diagram is created inside the Activity and flows are represented.To represent the included Use Case from the Activity diagram to the Use Case scenario, you must follow all the rules. Additionally, you must connect the including Use Case with the included Use Case with the included relationship in your project. |  |
| (with the extension point) | The extending Use Case with the extension point is added as an alternative flow; the Decision and Merge nodes are created.The Decision node name corresponds the name of the alternative condition.The Call Behavior Action is created for the alternative flow step of the extending Use Case. The Call Behavior Action is not named.The Call Behavior Action has the Behavior defined: the Activity whose name corresponds to the name of the extending Use Case.The Activity (the Behavior or the Call Behavior Action) is owned by the extending Use Case.If the extending Use Case has its own Use Case scenario, this scenario is represented in the Activity diagram; the Activity diagram is created inside the Activity under the extending Use Case.The [else] Guard property is defined for the Control Flow created from the Decision node to the Merge node.To represent the extending Use Case from the Activity diagram to the Use Case scenario, you must follow all the rules described above. Additionally, you must connect the extended Use Case with the extending Use Case with the extend relationship in your project. |  |
| (without any extension point) | The Call Behavior Action is created for the basic flow step of the Use Case scenario.The Call Behavior Action is connected with the Control Flow relationships according to the extending Use Case order in the basic flow.The Call Behavior Action is not named.The Call Behavior Action has the Behavior defined - the Activity whose name corresponds to the name of the extending Use Case.The Activity (the Behavior or the Call Behavior Action) is owned by the extending Use Case.If the extending Use Case has its own Use Case scenario, this scenario is represented in the Activity; the Activity diagram is created inside the Activity under the extending Use Case.To represent the extending Use Case from the Activity diagram to the Use Case scenario, you must follow all the rules described above. Additionally, you must connect the extended Use Case with the extending Use Case with the extend relationship in your project. |  |
| : alternative condition, alternative flow step | The alternative flow is interrupted in the basic flow by using the Decision and Merge nodes.The Decision and Merge nodes are created after the Call Behavior Action. The Call Behavior Action in the Use Case scenario represents the basic flow step of this alternative flow. In other words, in an Activity diagram, the elements of the alternative flow are created after the basic flow step this alternative flow belongs to.The Decision node name corresponds to the name of the alternative condition.The Call Behavior Action is created for each alternative flow step.All these elements are connected with the Control Flow relationships.The [else] Guard property is defined for the Control Flow created from the Decision node to the Merge node. |  |
| : exception type, exceptional flow step | The Structured Activity Node with the Input Pin is created.The Call Behavior Action for which the exceptional flow was created is linked with the Input Pin using the Exception Handler relationship.The Class element is created under the Activity. The Class corresponds to the exception type in the Use Case scenario, that is, the Class name corresponds to the exception type name in the Use Case scenario.The Class is assigned to the Input Pin as a type property.In the Structured Activity Node the Call Behavior Action is created for each Exceptional Flow step.The Call Behavior Actions inside the Structured Activity Node are connected with the Control Flow relationships.In the Structured Activity Node the Initial Node is created before the first Call Behavior Action, and the Final Node is created after the last Call Behavior Action.In the Structured Activity Node, the Initial Node is connected with the first Call Behavior Action by using the Control Flow relationship and the last Call Behavior Action is connected with the Final Node by using the Control Flow relationship. |  |

879969664

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154393 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00021: Abbreviation

- page_id: `227154393`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154393/Abbreviation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.288+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Abbreviation** property enables creation of a DSL element with an abbreviated name. You can enter the default name while specifying the customization element.

To define an abbreviation

1. In the Customization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'], click the Customization Target property and then select a stereotype or metaclass for which the abbreviation will be used.
2. Click the Abbreviation property and then type the text.
3. Reopen the project.

**Case Study**

We will demonstrate how to add an abbreviation for the class attribute.

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] .
2. Create a customization element named PropertyAbbreviation .
3. In the PropertyAbbreviation customization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Specification Window' space='MD2024xR3'] , click the Customization Target property and then select the Property metaclass.
4. Under the Naming category, click the Abbreviation property and type a short name for the property, for example, prop .
5. Close the Specification window. Reopen the project.
6. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] in the class diagram.
7. Create an attribute in the class. The property with the predefined name (or abbreviation) is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating an attribute in a class.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150332 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00022: Abstraction

- page_id: `227150332`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150332/Abstraction
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:33.977+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Dependency
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

AnAbstractionis a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2024xR3'] relationship that relates two elements or sets of elements that represent the same concept at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An Abstraction can be specified by changing its property values in the Abstraction[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area in the Specification window.

You may see between which elements Abstraction is drawn in the Abstraction dialog, Source and Target boxes.

Set the Abstraction symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR3'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating Abstraction relationship

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Abstraction</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> is a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link> relationship that relates two elements or sets of elements that represent the same concept at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints. </span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Abstraction in diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Abstraction" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Abstraction</span> can be specified by changing its property values in the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Abstraction</span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area in the Specification window.</span></p><p>You may see between which elements Abstraction is drawn in the <span class="hcp1">Abstraction</span> dialog, <span class="hcp1">Source</span> and <span class="hcp1">Target</span> boxes.</p><p><br /></p><p>Set the Abstraction symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Abstraction_relationship.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Abstraction" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating Abstraction relationship</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152793 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00023: Accept Changes or Reject Changes

- page_id: `227152793`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152793/Accept+Changes+or+Reject+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-03-18T14:32:33.745+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Used Project Switch Impact Analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Accept****Changes** button switches the used project version to the selected one. You will be warned if the selected version will cause or leave unresolved references.

**Reject Changes** restores the project to the state before invoking the Impact analysis with all of the local changes you had as well. The same appliesfor**X** (closing the dialog).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <strong>Accept</strong><strong> Changes</strong> button switches the used project version to the selected one. You will be warned if the selected version will cause or leave unresolved references. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Reject Changes</strong> restores the project to the state before invoking the Impact analysis with all of the local changes you had as well. The same applies </span>for<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <strong>X</strong> (closing the dialog).</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150149 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00024: Accept Event Action

- page_id: `227150149`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150149/Accept+Event+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-11-04T11:43:02.034+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907000387

907000389

907036862

**On this page**

****

907000388

The Accept Event Action is an Action that waits for the occurrence of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MD2024xR3']that meets specified conditions. If an Accept Event Action has no incoming edges, then the Action starts when the containing activity or Structured Node starts, whichever most immediately contains the Action. In addition, an Accept Event Action with no incoming edges remains enabled after it accepts an Event. It does not terminate after accepting an Event and outputting a value, but continues to wait for other Events. An Accept Event Action that has no incoming edges and is contained by a Structured Node is terminated when its container is terminated.

| Notation | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Accept Event Action | The Cancel Order Request Accept Event Action is connected to Cancel Order with the Exception Handler relationship. |
| Rotated Accept Event Action | The symbol of Accept Event Action is rotated. |
| Accept Time Event Action | The Time Event is connected to the Call Behavior Action. |

Assigning signals

To assign a Signal using a drag-and-drop operation

To assign a Signal to an Accept Event Action, at least one Signal element should exist in the project.

1. Select the Signal in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR3'] .
2. Drag the Signal to the Accept Event Action as shown in the following picture. [ATTACHMENT filename='accept_event_action_drag_signal.png']

To assign a Signal using the Accept Event Action’s shortcut menu

1. Right-click the Accept Event Action to open the shortcut menu.
2. On the shortcut menu, click Signal. Click to select the Signal that you wish to assign to the element, or click the Create Signal button.

Once the Signal is assigned to the Accept Event Action, a Signal Event and a Trigger for this Action are created automatically. If you change the Signal, the Signal Event and the Trigger will change accordingly to a new Signal. The following figure shows the Signal Event and the Trigger positions in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR3'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Using Time Event Shape

To draw the Accept Event Action with the Time Event Shape

- In the Activity diagram panel, select the Time Event button, and then click the diagram pane.

To change the position of the Accept Event Action name

1. Select the Accept Event Action shape on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='accept_event_action_selected.png']
2. Click the **Rotate State** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the upper-right side of the shape. The name of the Accept Event Action will appear on the opposite side of the shape. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can specify for an Accept Event Action, whether there is a single Output Pin for the Event or multiple Output Pins for the attributes of the Event.

##### Specifying the Is Unmarshall property

To specify the **Is Unmarshall** property

1. Select the Accept Event Action, and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] .
2. Select or clear the Is Unmarshall check box. If the value is set to:

- 
  - false , then there is a single Output Pin for the Event, and a real-world instance of the Signal is placed on this Output Pin.
  - true , then there are multiple Output Pins for the attributes of the Event, and feature values of the Signal instance are placed on the corresponding Output Pins.

907000386

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151302 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00025: Accepting and rejecting changes using shortcut menu

- page_id: `227151302`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151302/Accepting+and+rejecting+changes+using+shortcut+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-03-04T05:31:27.570+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['accepting-changes', 'rejecting-changes', 'merged-result']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

851702777

851702779

851702778

You can accept and reject changes using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Merged** **Result** window or using the shortcut menu in all panels.

The following table lists the commands of the shortcut menu that can be opened when an element is selected in the **Merged Result** tree/list.

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Accept | Click to accept the selected change. If the change has equivalent changes, they are accepted too. |
| Reject | Click to reject the selected change. If the change has equivalent changes, they are rejected too. |
| Accept With Properties | Click to accept the selected change and its property changes. |
| Reject With Properties | Click to reject the selecting change and its property changes. |
| Accept Recursively | Click to accept the selected change, its property changes, and all owned element changes in the Merged Result tree view. |
| Reject Recursively | Click to reject the selected change, its property changes, and all owned element changes in the Merged Result tree view. |
| Mark as Resolved | Click to mark the conflicting change as resolved by the user, but not automatically. |
| Select Original Location(s) | If the selected instance of a moved element is in the new location, the Select Original Location(s) command appears on the shortcut menu. Click it to navigate to and select the original location(s) of the moved element in the Merged Result tree. |
| Select New Location(s) | If the selected instance of a moved element is in its original location, the Select New Location(s) command appears on the shortcut menu. Click it to navigate to and select a new location(s) of the moved element in the Merged Result tree. |

851709959

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150148 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00026: Action

- page_id: `227150148`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150148/Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-11-04T11:17:44.926+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

906958764

906958767

906958766

An Action is a named element that is the fundamental unit of an executable functionality. The execution of an Action represents some transformations or processing in the modeled system, be it a computer system or otherwise. An Action execution represents the run-time [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024xR3']of executing an Action within a specific Behavior execution. As the Action is an abstract [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR3'], all Action executions will be the executions of a specific kind of Action. When the Action will be executed and what its actual inputs will be is determined by the concrete Action and the Behaviors in which it is used.

To remove an action from a diagram by keeping incoming and outgoing control flows connected, do one of the following

- On a diagram, select the Action and press Delete. Then, in the opened message, click Yes .
- In the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button. Then, in the opened message, click Yes .

- Flows can be joined only if the removed Action has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.
- You can select more than one Action to remove only if the selected part has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR3']**Environment Options** dialog , in the Diagram options group, under the Editing category, you can select one of the Join Control Flows option values: Ask to join control flows , Yes, or No .

An Action can be specified by changing its property values in the Action [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

906958763

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actions' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="467f9188-37d1-471e-a735-6654c1a2440a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">906958764</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f49e91d-6149-4c1d-9259-f599fe7a4b91"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">906958767</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70fe9d67-4960-4815-9686-c2fa049a81d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">906958766</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An Action is a named element that is the fundamental unit of an executable functionality. The execution of an Action represents some transformations or processing in the modeled system, be it a computer system or otherwise. An Action execution represents the run-time <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Behavior ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of executing an Action within a specific Behavior execution. As the Action is an abstract <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>, all Action executions will be the executions of a specific kind of Action. When the Action will be executed and what its actual inputs will be is determined by the concrete Action and the Behaviors in which it is used.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove an action from a diagram by keeping incoming and outgoing control flows connected, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On a diagram, select the Action and press Delete. Then, in the opened message, click <strong>Yes</strong>.</li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <ac:image ac:alt="gt_delete.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9918725/gt_delete.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1444133483319&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image>Delete button. Then, in the opened message, click <strong>Yes</strong>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a26999b-3209-4341-838c-866cd0689dc7"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Flows can be joined only if the removed Action has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.</li><li>You can select more than one Action to remove only if the selected part has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, in the <strong>Diagram</strong> options group, under the <strong>Editing</strong> category, you can select one of the <strong>Join Control Flows</strong> option values: <em>Ask to join control flows</em>, <em>Yes,</em> or <em>No</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>An Action can be specified by changing its property values in the Action <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24be1668-9134-4ba6-ad90-29fed8e7f09c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">906958763</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Actions" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Formatting Symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Customizing Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150494 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00027: Activators and predecessors

- page_id: `227150494`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150494/Activators+and+predecessors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:40:24.899+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Message > Messages in Communication diagrams
- labels: ['change-activator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372605732

372605734

372605733

INLINE

The predecessor denotes the set of Messages. The completion of these Messages enables the execution of the current Message. The predecessor means that the execution of a Message is not enabled until all the communications with sequence numbers appearing in the list have occurred. Therefore, the list of predecessors represents a synchronization of threads. The Message corresponding to the numerically preceding sequence number is an implicit predecessor and does not need to be explicitly listed.

All of the sequence numbers with the same prefix form a sequence. The numerical predecessor is the one in which the final term is one less. That is, number 6.4 is the predecessor of 6.5, where the number “6” is an activator (see the example in the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To show predecessors beside the Message number

Make sure the **Use Advanced Numbering** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Messages in Communication diagrams' space='MD2024xR3'].

- From the Message shortcut menu, select Show Predecessors .
- In the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR3']**Symbol Properties** dialog , set Show Predecessors to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change an activator number of Messages

Make sure the **Use Advanced Numbering** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Messages in Communication diagrams' space='MD2024xR3'].

1. From the Message shortcut menu, select Activator .
2. Select the activator you want to assign to the selected Message. Numbering of the selected Message and subsequent Messages decreases by one level. The first level number is the number of an activator Message (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you change the activator number to a predecessor Message, this number will change for subsequent predecessors.

372605731

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227148559 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: Active validation period

- page_id: `227148559`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148559/Active+validation+period
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-21T07:26:30.475+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Improving performance
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1010211403

1010211405

1010211404

By default, an open model is periodically validated. An active validation period is the period of time between subsequent validation occurrences. When an active validation period is short, it may have a negative effect on the performance of your modeling tool, as it needs to constantly process model data.

If you notice that modeling tool performance has decreased, try setting a longer active validation period or opening your model without loading its diagrams.

To change an active validation period

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Environment . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR3']**Environment Options** dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the Active Validation category.
3. In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the Active Validation Period .
4. Restart your modeling tool.

1010211401

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Improving performance' space='MD2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space='MD2024xR3']

The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Expand the <strong>General</strong> options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the <strong>Active Validation</strong> category.</li><li>In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the <strong>Active Validation Period</strong>.</li><li>Restart your modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11503625-78e4-4ee4-aaeb-50d9558fc556"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1010211401</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Improving performance" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Background Task Manager" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory allocation" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150447 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00029: Active Validation Rules for Links

- page_id: `227150447`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150447/Active+Validation+Rules+for+Links
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T18:05:01.327+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Link
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Active Validation instantly checks the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model, displays errors in the model, and suggests solutions.

There are two active validation rules for links:

- [Instance Specification type mismatch](#ActiveValidationRulesforLinks-InstanceSpecificationtypemismatch)
- [Missing slots in links](#ActiveValidationRulesforLinks-Missingslotsinlinks)

###### Instance Specification type mismatch

The *Instance Specification type mismatch* validation rule allows for updating slot values when referenced association end types are incompatible with connected instance specification types.

###### Missing slots in links

The *Missing slots on links* validation rule is more useful than migrating projects from the modeling tool 17.0.2 version or earlier. The validation rule detects links that have related associations but do not have slots created in them. For each incorrect link, the warning is displayed. To resolve the warning, there is the **Create slots for this link** resolution action. After choosing that action, slots are created in the link.

The validation rule is available only if the **Automatically Create Slots in Link** option is set to *true* in the Environment Options dialog.

#### PANEL: On this Page

On this Page

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Links' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152152 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00030: Active validation suites

- page_id: `227152152`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152152/Active+validation+suites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:58.781+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Optimizing performance

498536743

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Optimizing performance

498536745

498536742

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Optimizing performance

498536744

##### Introduction

Active validation suites enable you to immediately see if a model is correct and complete, instantly displaying any errors in the model and suggesting appropriate solutions. The active validation suites have the «activeValidationSuite» stereotype applied.

Each profile contains its own active validation suites. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='MD2024xR3'].

You can see the following active validation suites in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='MD2024xR3']**Validation** dialog or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space='MD2024xR3']**Validation Suites** dialog:

- Numbering Validation contains constraints that check if the element number is unique.
- Parameters Synchronization contains constraints that keep your model valid and synchronized with your changes. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space='MD2024xR3']
- Path Overlapping contains constraints that check to ensure that paths do not overlap.
- Pins Displaying contains constraints that check to ensure that actions do not contain hidden Pins.
- Relations Ownership contains constraints that check if a relationship has the same owner as its end elements.
- Shape Ownership contains constraints that check the element and path owner: if the symbol owner on the diagram correctly reflects the element owner in the model and the element, and If the diagram on which the symbol is drawn belongs to the same owner.
- Text References Validation (Auto) contains constraints that check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.
- UML Correctness contains constraints that check the Ports compatibility, Pin types compatibility, Slot and Tags multiplicity correctness, etc.
- Diagram Merge contains constraints that check if the diagrams or symbols are merged correctly.

You can find active validation suites in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR3'] when the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option is enabled. Expand the *UML Standard Profile* Package > *Validation Profile* Package > *Active Validation* package.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MD2024xR3']true

##### Validating a project with active validation suites

To manually validate a project with active validation suites, you can select the specific validation suite (mentioned above) as the **Validation Suite** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='MD2024xR3']**Validation** dialog.

[How to start the validation >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR3/SysML+validation+suites#SysMLvalidationsuites-startStartingthevalidation)

#### EXCERPT: Optimizing performance

INLINE

##### Selecting active validation mode

Active validation automatically runs in the background according to the selected active validation mode.

To select the active validation mode

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Active Validation .
  - On the main menu, select Options > Environment. On the left of the Environment Options dialog, select Active Validation group. Select the Active Validation option.
2. Select one of the following modes:
  - All - enables all (user defined and system) active validation suites.
  - **System** - enables validation suites that are critical to the model. For example, all projects have the UML Standard Profile, which has the Composition Integrity system validation suite. This suite contains validation rules allowing for detecting shared project usage, recovered element, or illegal reference related problems. InfoYou can check these rules under the **Auxiliary Resources** > **UML Standard Profile** > **Validation Profile** > **Composition Integrity**.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MD2024xR3']true
3. Click OK . Selected validation suites are active for all projects, not just the current project.

##### Specifying the active validation period

You cancontrol how often, in seconds, a modeling tool will analyze the rules to be executed and how often the modeling tool will execute these rules. After opening a project, all required validation rules will be executed, including system or all (user and system), and next time these rules will be checked only after the specified period.

To specify the active validation period

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment.
2. On the left of the Environment Options dialog, select Active Validation group.
3. Select the Active Validation Period (seconds) option.
4. Type its value in seconds. Specify a higher time period to ensure better performance of the application.
5. Click **OK.**The specified period is used for all projects, not just the current project.

##### Customizing active validation options

You can customize the Active Validation options in the**Project Options** dialog.

To open the Active Validation options

1. Open the Project Options dialog in one of the following ways:
  - On the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Active Validation Options .
  - On the main menu, select Options > Project . In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR3']**Project Options** dialog, select General > Validation .
2. Specify the options you need in the Active Validation area. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR3']How to use the **Project Options** dialog >>
3. Click OK .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Active Validation options in the Project Options dialog.

#### TIP: Optimizing performance

Optimizing performance

- Set the **Validate Only Visible Diagrams** option value to *true*. This limits a validation scope to visible diagrams only.
- Set the **Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Project** option value to true to reduce the number of elements to check in your model.
- Set the **Exclude elements from additional content** option value to *true* to ignore the content of Smart packages while running the passive validation in the **Validation** (**Analyze** > **Validation** >**Validate**) dialog.

- Smart package content (separate elements) will be validated only if Smart package is set directly as a scope of the active validation.
- If smart package is set indirectly for the validation scope, modeling tool will automatically exclude it from the active validation scope, because elements in the smart package are owned by other elements.

##### syncTurning off the parameters synchronization

To turn off the parameters synchronization

1. On the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > General .
3. Find the Synchronize Parameters and Arguments option and set its value to false .

227174901

**Related pages**

- MagicDraw predefined validation suites
- SysML validation suites
- [SysML active validation suites](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR3/SysML+active+validation+suites)
- Requirements validation suites

The active validation suites have the «activeValidationSuite» stereotype applied.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66f99b95-6f83-4d42-a19c-262b473a3e29"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Each profile contains its own active validation suites. suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can see the following active validation suites in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation
