The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting element types" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Element Type</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog with the element and stereotype list opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="criteria_element_type.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding or removing element types" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li><p>To add the element types, select the appropriate check boxes in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="190523af-47bd-4ba9-855b-72006d24be89"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Properties corresponding to the selected types will be added to the list of available columns.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>To remove the element types, click to clear the appropriate check boxes in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3310dc5-b68f-4eb4-baa3-0b243a15fde5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Properties corresponding to the deselected types will be removed from the list of available columns.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_type.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding or removing element types" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb05b02-bf43-4df6-8bcc-bcb5fb812b3d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>Select the </span><strong>Include subtypes</strong><span> check box to display the subtypes of the selected element types.</span></li></ul><ul><li><span>Select the </span><strong>Include Custom Types</strong><span> check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To add an element type, you can also drag a selected element to the <strong>Element Type</strong> box directly from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p><ul><li>To select <em>adjacent</em> elements, select a single element and hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.</li><li>To select <em>nonadjacent</em> elements, select a single element and hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.</li></ul><p>The new value of the <strong>Element Type</strong> box replaces the old value.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_element_type_generic_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding or removing element types" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152852 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `227152852`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152852/Adding+projects+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-08-04T12:32:47.513+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you start a collaboration session, you can add new projects to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform in one of the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2024xR3'] .
- Add a local project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2024xR3'] .

To be able to add projects to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2024xR3'] first.

##### Creating projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

This section explains how to create a new project in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform from scratch.

To create a project in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > New Server Project .
  - In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > Projects and click the New button in the open dialog.
2. On the left side of the New Server Project dialog, select the kind of project you want to create.
3. In the Name box, type the project name and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_server_project_dialog.png']

##### Adding local projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

This section explains how to add a local project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

To add a local project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. Open the local project you want to add to the 3D EXPERIENCE planform.
2. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Add Project to Server .
3. In the Name box of the open dialog, optionally change the name of the project.
4. In the **Comment** box, optionally add an explanation or note about the project. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you want to prevent the automatic creation of[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2021xR2']in your project, click **More** in the **Add Project to the Server** dialog and clear the **Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects** check box. Have in mind that Mount relationships keep used projects in the same location as in the local project.
5. Click the Add button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you start a collaboration session, you can add new projects to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a new project from scratch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding local projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform"><ac:link-body>Add a local project to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97820265-56fc-4fa5-96fb-340b982db553"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To be able to add projects to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, you must <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[log in to the platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform</h3><p>This section explains how to create a new project in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform from scratch.</p><p>To create a project in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>New Server Project</strong>.</li><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> and click the <strong>New</strong> button in the open dialog.</li></ul></li><li>On the left side of the <strong>New Server Project</strong> dialog, select the kind of project you want to create.</li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> box, type the project name and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_server_project_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3><br />Adding local projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform</h3><p>This section explains how to add a local project to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a local project to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the local project you want to add to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE planform.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Add Project to Server</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> box of the open dialog, optionally change the name of the project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Comment</strong> box, optionally add an explanation or note about the project.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_local_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f1eaee-7d11-448a-add5-cf6767f75b99"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you want to prevent the automatic creation of </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Mount relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> in your project, click <strong>More</strong> in the <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog and clear the <strong>Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects</strong> check box. Have in mind that Mount relationships keep used projects in the same location as in the local project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152372 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00048: Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `227152372`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152372/Adding+projects+to+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-04-22T08:12:03.237+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

147331079

147331081

147331080

Projects can be added to Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) in one of the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024xR3']

createTo create a project in TWCloud from scratch

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. In the Online Projects tab, click the New button. The New Server Project dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_server_project.png']
3. In the Name box, type a new project name.
4. Next to the **Category**box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the category in which you want to store your project.
  - To create a new category, select the Create Category button in the Select Category dialog: [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category_button.png']
5. Click OK .

addTo add a local project to TWCloud

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting a collaboration session' space='MD2024xR3'] .
2. Open the local project you want to add to TWCloud.
3. From the Collaborate menu, choose Add Project to Server . The Add Project to the Server dialog opens.
4. If you want to give the project a new name, type the name directly in the Name box when it is added to the server.
5. Next to the**Category** box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the category in which you want to store your project.
6. If you have some explanations or notes about this project, provide them in the Comment box.
7. usages If the project has no used projects, or if it has some that you want to be added to the server, skip this step. If you don't want any of the used projects to be added, do the following:
  1. Click More in the Add Project to the Server dialog for more options.
  2. Click the Local Projects tab. The list of used local projects opens.
  3. In the **Action** column of each local project that you don't want to add to the server, select **Leave as Local Project**. If you exclude all or some of the used projects from the addition to TWCloud (see step #7), you can alwayslink them to the project later. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] You can disable the **Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects**option in the **Add Project to the Server** dialog to prevent the automatic creation of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2024xR3'] in your project.
8. Click Add . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. The project is now added to TWCloud.

When the project is added to the server:

- The title of the client application, e.g., MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, window displays #1 next to the project name. This is the initial project version number. [ATTACHMENT filename='ccs_solutions_in_modeling_tool.png']
- The contents of the Model Browser are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2024xR3'] . The model is unlocked.
- If the project is added to the used projects, you can see them in the Model Browser under the root package and under Project Usages . [ATTACHMENT filename='maintain_mount_points_example_.png']
- To view the project, select Collaborate > Recent Server Projects.

147331078

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c04597a-983e-41d4-8a27-a36db93998ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147331079</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04570af1-f5a6-41df-84e7-f422c902d3ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147331081</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4871a78e-8e35-4fa5-a3b2-5aa30407340c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147331080</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Projects can be added to Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By creating a project in TWCloud from scratch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="add"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By adding a local project to TWCloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21de6931-9a3c-4eaf-8183-07071f389f51"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a project in TWCloud from scratch</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Online Projects</strong> tab, click the <strong>New</strong> button. The <strong>New Server Project</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_server_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> box, type a new project name.</li><li><p>Next to the <strong>Category </strong>box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the category in which you want to store your project.</p><ul><li>To create a new category, select the<strong> Create Category</strong> button in the <strong>Select Category</strong> dialog:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1932dd24-509d-4a56-b8a6-5ad85677693b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">add</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add a local project to TWCloud</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting a collaboration session" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Log in to TWCloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Open the local project you want to add to TWCloud.</li><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Add Project to Server</strong>. The <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>If you want to give the project a new name, type the name directly in the <strong>Name</strong> box when it is added to the server.</li><li><p>Next to the<strong> Category</strong> box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the category in which you want to store your project.</p></li><li>If you have some explanations or notes about this project, provide them in the <strong>Comment</strong> box.</li><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3996e67-3bba-4b35-bf71-f6aca701dfb4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">usages</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>If the project has no used projects, or if it has some that you want to be added to the server, skip this step. If you don't want any of the used projects to be added, do the following:<br /><ol><li>Click <strong>More </strong>in the <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog for more options.</li><li>Click the <strong>Local Projects</strong> tab. The list of used local projects opens.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Action</strong> column of each local project that you don't want to add to the server, select <strong>Leave as Local Project</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02eebe5b-43a3-4ec7-8542-92c9f646e3b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you exclude all or some of the used projects from the addition to TWCloud (see step #7), you can always<span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> link them to the project</span></span> later.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36cc3aff-c113-4851-a765-d37b00a1e83d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can disable the <strong>Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects </strong>option in the <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog to prevent the automatic creation of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Mount relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>Add</strong>.<br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_project_to_the_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>The project is now added to TWCloud.</p></li></ol><p>When the project is added to the server:</p><ul><li>The title of the client application, e.g., MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, window displays<em>#1</em> next to the project name. This is the initial project version number.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ccs_solutions_in_modeling_tool.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>The contents of the Model Browser are <ac:link ac:anchor="represent"><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[gray]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The model is unlocked.</li><li>If the project is added to the used projects, you can see them in the Model Browser under the root package and under <em>Project Usages</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="maintain_mount_points_example_.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>To view the project, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Recent Server Projects.</strong> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="135979c6-91f1-456d-b192-3498bd4ea86b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147331078</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152856 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00049: Adding standard profiles to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `227152856`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152856/Adding+standard+profiles+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-06T13:54:21.654+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard profiles should be stored in a public collaborative space everyone can access. If you try to add a project that requires a standard profile that does not exist on the server, the standard profile is automatically imported to the server. We highly recommend moving such standard profiles to a collaborative space dedicated to standard profiles.

##### Creating public collaborative spaces

We recommend that administrators responsible for managing MBSE on the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform create a public collaborative space for standard profiles. All users should have viewing permission for this space, but the editing permission should be reserved for the administrator.

To create a public collaborative space

1. On the 3D EXPERIENCE platform, click [ATTACHMENT filename='compass_icon.png'] and find Collaborative Spaces Control Center under My Apps .
2. Click Create Collaborative Space .
3. In the open dialog, enter the owner’s name (if it is not the current user).
4. In the Title box, the space title.
5. In the Description box, enter an optional space description.
6. In the Family drop-down box, select Design - Collaborative storage to manage any kind of content .
7. In the Visibility drop-down box, select Public - Content visible to all users .
8. Click the Create button. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_collaborative_space_dialog.png']

##### Adding standard profiles to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform

Once the collaborative space is created, users can add standard profiles to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

- Before starting work with the 3D EXPERIENCE platform, it is recommended to add all standard profiles provided in the modeling tool installation.
- Members of the same team should use a modeling tool with the same plugins.

To add standard profiles to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']Log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform where the collaborative space for standard profiles was created.
2. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Switch Platform/Credentials .
3. In the Credentials drop-down box of the open dialog, select the collaborative space. [ATTACHMENT filename='switching_to_public_space.png']
4. Click OK.
5. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Migrate Projects to Version <version number> .
6. In the **Show** drop-down menu of the open dialog, select **Standard Profiles**. It will display all standard profiles on the server and your modeling tool installation.****
7. Select the standard profiles you want to add or click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_all_button.png'] on the left side of the dialog to select all standard profiles. To ensure the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform is properly configured, we recommend selecting all available standard profiles. [ATTACHMENT filename='migrating_standard_profiles.png']
8. Click OK .

Following these steps will add the new standard profiles from your modeling tool installation to the platform and update any existing profiles to the version of your modeling tool. Once standard profiles are added to a public collaborative space, all newly created and added projects will use standard profiles from that space. If a profile is not added automatically, you can add it as a standalone project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Standard profiles should be stored in a public collaborative space everyone can access. If you try to add a project that requires a standard profile that does not exist on the server, the standard profile is automatically imported to the server. We highly recommend moving such standard profiles to a collaborative space dedicated to standard profiles.</p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Creating public collaborative spaces</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">We recommend that administrators responsible for managing MBSE on the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform create a public collaborative space for standard profiles. All users should have viewing permission for this space, but the editing permission should be reserved for the administrator.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To create a public collaborative space</span></p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compass_icon.png" the owner’s name (if it is not the current user).</li><li>In the <strong>Title</strong> box, the space title.</li><li>In the <strong>Description</strong> box, enter an optional space description.</li><li>In the <strong>Family</strong> drop-down box, select <strong>Design - Collaborative storage to manage any kind of content</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Visibility</strong> drop-down box, select <strong>Public - Content visible to all users</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_collaborative_space_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Adding standard profiles to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform</h3><p>Once the collaborative space is created, users can add standard profiles to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cf8b2e8-9b58-46ce-9678-fb880a837d08"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Before starting work with the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, it is recommended to add all standard profiles provided in the modeling tool installation.</li><li>Members of the same team should use a modeling tool with the same plugins.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To add standard profiles to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>Log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link> where the collaborative space for standard profiles was created.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>3DEXPERIENCE</strong> &gt; <strong>Switch Platform/Credentials</strong>.</li><li>In the Credentials drop-down box of the open dialog, select the collaborative space.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="switching_to_public_space.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK.</strong></li><li>In the main menu, select <strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Collaborate</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> &gt; </span><strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Migrate Projects to Version &lt;version number&gt;</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Show</strong> drop-down menu of the open dialog, select <strong>Standard Profiles</strong>. It will display all standard profiles on the server and your modeling tool installation.<strong><br /></strong></p></li><li>Select the standard profiles you want to add or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_all_button.png" /></ac:image> on the left side of the dialog to select all standard profiles.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51ed5a9e-6900-4255-a607-a3d9c05ce438"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To ensure the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform is properly configured, we recommend selecting all available standard profiles.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migrating_standard_profiles.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p style="text-align: left;"><br />Following these steps will add the new standard profiles from your modeling tool installation to the platform and update any existing profiles to the version of your modeling tool. Once standard profiles are added to a public collaborative space, all newly created and added projects will use standard profiles from that space. If a profile is not added automatically, you can add it as a standalone project.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227149655 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00050: Adding terms in the Glossary Table

- page_id: `227149655`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227149655/Adding+terms+in+the+Glossary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-07-11T05:42:35.488+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Glossary table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add terms in the Glossary Table in the following ways: 
43

##### Directly from the diagram pane

To add a word or phrase to a glossary directly from the diagram pane

1. Select a word or phrase you want to be a term. Even if you mark only a part of a word, the entire word will be added to the glossary as a term.
2. Right-click it and select Add To <Glossary Table name>. The word or phrase is converted to a term and added to the Glossary Table. [ATTACHMENT filename='glossary_add.png']

##### Using the Generic Table toolbar

To add a term in the Glossary Table using a toolbar

- Click the Add New button to create a new row for a term in the table.
- Click the Add Existing button and from the Select Element dialog. Choose the element you wish to be a term.

##### By dragging any element

To drag an element to the Glossary Table

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you wish to be a term.
2. Drag it to the Glossary Table.

##### By pasting from another resource

To create terms in the table by pasting data from another resource

1. Copy a table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Glossary Table.The data types of columns in the Glossary table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Glossary Table where you want to paste the copied data. The cells must be editable in the Glossary Table.
3. Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
Each row displays one term with its description. If the term already exists in the model, the information updates.If terms have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted terms in the Glossary Table. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024xR3']**Element Numbering** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Adding synonyms of the terms

You can add one or more synonyms for every term. After that, the synonyms are available in the suggestion list. When you use a synonym, the original term is displayed in the description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To add a synonym

- In the term's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3']**Specification** window , Synonym property specification cell, type the synonyms for the term. [ATTACHMENT filename='synonyms.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add terms in the Glossary Table in the following ways:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6684ea6d-ca59-4c0f-815c-f50bde5487b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Directly from the diagram pane</h3><p>To add a word or phrase to a glossary directly from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select a word or phrase you want to be a term.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcebc2ff-42e8-463b-b08f-c5d19c16e556"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Even if you mark only a part of a word, the entire word will be added to the glossary as a term.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Right-click it and select<strong> Add To &lt;Glossary Table name&gt;.</strong> <br />The word or phrase is converted to a term and added to the Glossary Table.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="glossary_add.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding terms in the Glossary Table" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Using the Generic Table toolbar</h3><p>To add a term in the Glossary Table using a toolbar</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to create a new row for a term in the table.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and from the<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog. Choose the element you wish to be a term.</li></ul><h3>By dragging any element</h3><p>To drag an element to the Glossary Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select an element you wish to be a term.</li><li>Drag it to the Glossary Table.</li></ol><h3>By pasting from another resource</h3><p>To create terms in the table by pasting data from another resource</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Copy a table from another resource.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5799968b-3b43-4cd1-8c33-87d572954aec"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Glossary Table.</li><li>The data types of columns in the Glossary table must be compatible with copied information.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open the Glossary Table where you want to paste the copied data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e77cc25f-6962-4a18-bd50-6ae2f1e0c262"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The cells must be editable in the Glossary Table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X).<br />Each row displays one term with its description.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aa3ecfe-f5e2-4189-8710-ee308ed4e62d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the term already exists in the model, the information updates.</li><li>If terms have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted terms in the Glossary Table. Manage ID prefixes in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="paste_table_in_glossary_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding terms in the Glossary Table" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Adding synonyms of the terms</h3><p>You can add one or more synonyms for every term. After that, the synonyms are available in the suggestion list. When you use a synonym, the original term is displayed in the description.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synonym_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding terms in the Glossary Table" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To add a synonym</p><hr /><ul><li>In the term's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Specification</strong> window</ac:link-body></ac:link>, Synonym property specification cell, type the synonyms for the term.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synonyms.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding terms in the Glossary Table" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227153548 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00051: Adding variables into an output report filename

- page_id: `227153548`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227153548/Adding+variables+into+an+output+report+filename
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:56:11.223+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the following variables and include them in an output report filename.

- Date variable
- Template variable
- Random number
- Project attribute

#### Date variable

A date variable is a variable for the current date and time. (See section **$date** in the previous chapter). The date variable consists of:

- A date variable with the default format.
- A date variable with a customized date format.

##### Date variables with default format

Using a date variable without an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a default format.

The syntax: **${date}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html*”.

##### Date variable with custom format

Using a date variable with an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a specific format.

The syntax: **${date.get('format')}** or **${date.get("format")}**

For example:

```text

```

The output filename would be “*index_20111221-115544.html*”.

#### Template variable

A template variable is a variable for a template that is created in Report Wizard. It allows you to add all template variables into an output report filename (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding variables into an output report filename' space='MD2024xR3']*).

##### Including template variables in an output report filename

When you include template variables created in an output report filename, the output filename which consists of the variables will be converted into value. This section uses the following template variables as examples, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Adding a value of a top-level variable

You can retrieve a top-level variable in an output report filename by using the following syntax.

The syntax: **${Parent}**

For example:

```text

```

The report output filename would be “*index_parent value.html*”.

##### Adding a value of a child variable

You can retrieve any of the following child variable values in an output report filename.

1. Getting a child variable at an index position: The syntax : ${Parent.get(0)}
2. Getting a child variable by child names: The syntax : ${Parent.get('Child')} or ${Parent.get("Child")}
3. Getting a child value by referencing its name (in this case “Child”): The syntax : ${Parent.Child}

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_child value.html*”.

#### Random number

Report Wizard supports only pseudorandom numbers in an output report filename. The random number consists of:

- A random number with the default distributed int value.
- A random number with a distributed int value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive).

##### Random number with the default distributed integer value

You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value.

**The syntax: ${random}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_21547.html*”.

##### Random number with a distributed integer value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive)

You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value between 0 and a specific value.

**The syntax: ${random.nextInt(n)}**

The Parameters:

“n” - the bound on the random number to be returned. The “n” value must be a positive integer.

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_457.html*”.

#### Project attribute

You can add a project attribute or information about a project into an output report filename.

The project attribute consists of:

- Project name
- Project version

##### Project name

You can retrieve the name of a project by using the following syntax.

**The syntax: ${project.name}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_myProject.html*”.

##### Project version

You can retrieve a project version by using the following syntax.

**The syntax: ${project.version}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_1234.html*”.

#### Exception flow

You may accidentally specify an invalid output report filename such as:

- An invalid syntax variable.
- A variable that does not exist in a template variable.
- An invalid parameter.

If this happens, Report Wizard will use this variable as a filename and a warning message will open in a log file (*\.modeling tool\version\md.log*).

For example:

```text

```

A single quote after “yyyyMMdd-hhmmss” was missing. Therefore, the output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss)}.html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax.

For example:

```text

```

“${Parent}” was not in the report template variable. Therefore, the output filename would be "index_${Parent}.html" and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable.

For example:

```text

```

In this example, ‘yyyy-MM-dd qq’ is an invalid parameter. Therefore, output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyy-MM-dd qq').html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter.

- All variables should be able to be combined together. For example: C:\OutputReport\index_${project.name}_${date}.html The output filename would be “ index_myProject_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html ”.
- The template variable is the highest priority of all other variables. For example: C:\OutputReport\index_${random}.html

If you specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the variable value will be used as the filename.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The output filename would be “*index_myRandomNumber.html*”.

If you do not specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the random number with the default distributed integer value will be used as the filename.

The output filename would be “*index_12345.html*”.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can use the following variables and include them in an output report filename.</p><ul><li>Date variable</li><li>Template variable</li><li>Random number</li><li>Project attribute</li></ul><h2>Date variable</h2><p>A date variable is a variable for the current date and time. (See section <strong>$date</strong> in the previous chapter). The date variable consists of:</p><ul><li>A date variable with the default format.</li><li>A date variable with a customized date format.</li></ul><h3>Date variables with default format</h3><p>Using a date variable without an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a default format.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${date}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4218d084-8f1e-410b-9f13-b52437369ab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html</em>”.</p><h3>Date variable with custom format</h3><p>Using a date variable with an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a specific format.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${date.get('format')}</strong> or <strong>${date.get(&quot;format&quot;)}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aecf2d93-fb72-44e3-8f95-783788c2373d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss')}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output filename would be “<em>index_20111221-115544.html</em>”.</p><h2>Template variable</h2><p>A template variable is a variable for a template that is created in Report Wizard. It allows you to add all template variables into an output report filename (see <em><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Including Variables in a Template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding variables into an output report filename" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></em>).</p><h3>Including template variables in an output report filename</h3><p>When you include template variables created in an output report filename, the output filename which consists of the variables will be converted into value. This section uses the following template variables as examples, see figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Sample of Report Variables" ac:alt="Sample of Report Variables"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Variable Sample.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding variables into an output report filename" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Adding a value of a top-level variable</h3><p>You can retrieve a top-level variable in an output report filename by using the following syntax.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${Parent}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30eb8db7-f2a3-4920-814d-2147940b9d15"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${Parent}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The report output filename would be “<em>index_parent value.html</em>”.</p><h3>Adding a value of a child variable</h3><p>You can retrieve any of the following child variable values in an output report filename.</p><ol><li>Getting a child variable at an index position:<br /><strong>The syntax</strong>: <strong>${Parent.get(0)}</strong></li><li>Getting a child variable by child names: <br /><strong>The syntax</strong>: <strong>${Parent.get('Child')} or ${Parent.get(&quot;Child&quot;)}</strong></li><li>Getting a child value by referencing its name (in this case “Child”): <br /><strong>The syntax</strong>: <strong>${Parent.Child} </strong></li></ol><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3da87559-1e46-41b0-b0e2-b94f207df042"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${Parent.get('Child')}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_child value.html</em>”.</p><h2>Random number</h2><p>Report Wizard supports only pseudorandom numbers in an output report filename. The random number consists of:</p><ul><li>A random number with the default distributed int value.</li><li>A random number with a distributed int value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive).</li></ul><h3>Random number with the default distributed integer value</h3><p>You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${random}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed4ac6b9-5e5c-49e6-8e3e-0c5f83ce9ec7"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${random}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_21547.html</em>”.</p><h3>Random number with a distributed integer value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive)</h3><p>You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value between 0 and a specific value.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${random.nextInt(n)}</strong></p><p>The Parameters:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">“n” - the bound on the random number to be returned. The “n” value must be a positive integer.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1aaf3977-17dd-4ccb-86a8-7c7e1d032ff6"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${random.nextInt(1000)}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_457.html</em>”.</p><h2>Project attribute</h2><p>You can add a project attribute or information about a project into an output report filename.</p><p>The project attribute consists of:</p><ul><li>Project name</li><li>Project version</li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Project name</span></h3><p>You can retrieve the name of a project by using the following syntax.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${project.name}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10dd6bfc-808f-42de-ad91-6d6f2165748f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${project.name}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_myProject.html</em>”. </p><h3>Project version</h3><p>You can retrieve a project version by using the following syntax.</p><p><strong>The syntax: ${project.version}</strong></p><p>For example: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e48ef7f-500e-4db7-8925-8d9ab6340600"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${project.version}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_1234.html</em>”.</p><h2>Exception flow</h2><p>You may accidentally specify an invalid output report filename such as:</p><ul><li>An invalid syntax variable.</li><li>A variable that does not exist in a template variable.</li><li>An invalid parameter.</li></ul><p>If this happens, Report Wizard will use this variable as a filename and a warning message will open in a log file (<em>\.modeling tool\version\md.log</em>).</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f339326a-7d57-476c-9f59-1c36c425156f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss)}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A single quote after “yyyyMMdd-hhmmss” was missing. Therefore, the output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss)}.html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax" ac:alt="A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Invalid Variable.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding variables into an output report filename" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax.</h6><p>For example: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="554bea4d-a169-4398-9642-ffb25427a090"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${Parent}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>“${Parent}” was not in the report template variable. Therefore, the output filename would be &quot;index_${Parent}.html&quot; and a warning message would open, see figure below.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable" ac:alt="A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specified Variable Not Existing.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding variables into an output report filename" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable.</h6><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ce18582-9311-47cd-b2bd-363dad75699e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date.get('yyyy-MM-dd qq')}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In this example, ‘yyyy-MM-dd qq’ is an invalid parameter. Therefore, output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyy-MM-dd qq').html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter" ac:alt="A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Invalid Parameter.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding variables into an output report filename" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52cfdfa9-f690-4409-b467-39a9e6cba383"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>All variables should be able to be combined together. For example:<br /><em>C:\OutputReport\index_${<span style="line-height: 1.42857;">project.name</span>}_${date}.html</em><br />The output filename would be “<em>index_myProject_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html</em>”.</li><li>The template variable is the highest priority of all other variables. For example:<br /><em>C:\OutputReport\index_${random}.html</em></li></ul><p>If you specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the variable value will be used as the filename.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Template Variable Name at Random " ac:alt="Template Variable Name at Random "><ri:attachment ri:filename="Random Template Variable Name.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding variables into an output report filename" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The output filename would be “<em>index_myRandomNumber.html</em>”.</p><p>If you do not specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the random number with the default distributed integer value will be used as the filename.</p><p>The output filename would be “<em>index_12345.html</em>”.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151131 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00052: Adding, removing, and changing order of favorites

- page_id: `227151131`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151131/Adding+removing+and+changing+order+of+favorites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:48.246+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Favorites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1539458257

1539458269

1539458259

There are a few ways to add an element to favorites:

- Using the Add Selected to Favorites command from the Favorites menu. This way is especially handy for quick additions to favorites.
- Using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='MD2024xR3'] dialog. Choose this way when you need to add and then perform other manipulations with favorites.

To quickly add an element to favorites

1. Select the element in the Model Browser and click [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] on the toolbar of the open tab. [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_quick_add.png']
2. Click Add Selected to Favorites on the menu.

To add an element to favorites in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='MD2024xR3']** dialog

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='MD2024xR3'] dialog.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='MD2024xR3'] dialog, select the element and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] button, which is between two lists of the dialog. The element appears in the list of favorites.
3. Click OK to confirm the addition and close the dialog.

Once the element is added to favorites, the following changes occur in your project:

- The element becomes marked as a favorite in all trees and lists of the model.
- You can easily navigate to the element in the Model Browser. The element name is added to the Go To Favorites command group on the Favorites menu that opens in the Model Browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_goto_favorites.png']

To remove an element from favorites

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='MD2024xR3'] dialog.
2. In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click the element you need to remove and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove.png'] button.
3. Click OK to confirm the removal and close the dialog.

Once the element is removed from favorites, it is no longer marked. Commands for navigation to the element and for searching in it are no longer available on the Favorites menu.

The following procedure describes how to change the order of favorite elements on the Favorites menu. To change the order of favorites on the Favorites menu, you need to make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='MD2024xR3']** dialog.

To change the order of favorites

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manage favorites' space='MD2024xR3']**Manage Favorites** dialog.
2. In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click an element whose order on the Favorites menu you need to change and then do either of the following:
  - If you need to move the element up, click the Up button. Click again if you need to move the element up by one more position and so on.
  - If you need to move the element down, click the Down button. Click again if you need to move the element down by one more position and so on.
3. Click OK to confirm the reorder and close the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Moving the element up in the Favorites menu.

1539458256

**Related pages**

- Favorites

The element appears in the list of favorites.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the addition and close the dialog.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Once the element is added to favorites, the following changes occur in your project:</p><ul><li>The element becomes marked as a favorite in all trees and lists of the model.</li><li>You can easily navigate to the element in the Model Browser. The element name is added to the <strong>Go To Favorites</strong> command group on the Favorites menu that opens in the Model Browser.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorites_goto_favorites.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding, removing, and changing order of favorites" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></li></ul><p>To remove an element from favorites</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manage favorites" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage Favorites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</li><li>In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click the element you need to remove and then click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding, removing, and changing order of favorites" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the removal and close the dialog.</li></ol><p>Once the element is removed from favorites, it is no longer marked. Commands for navigation to the element and for searching in it are no longer available on the Favorites menu.</p><p>The following procedure describes how to change the order of favorite elements on the Favorites menu. To change the order of favorites on the Favorites menu, you need to make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manage favorites" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage Favorites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the order of favorites</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manage favorites" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong> Manage Favorites</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>dialog.</li><li>In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click an element whose order on the Favorites menu you need to change and then do either of the following:<ul><li>If you need to move the element up, click the <strong>Up</strong> button. Click again if you need to move the element up by one more position and so on.</li><li>If you need to move the element down, click the <strong>Down</strong> button. Click again if you need to move the element down by one more position and so on.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the reorder and close the dialog.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_favorites_order.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding, removing, and changing order of favorites" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Moving the element up in the Favorites menu. </h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc16ea9c-7887-48dd-8eeb-70b574a427d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1539458256</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Favorites">Favorites</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154396 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00053: Additional Content

- page_id: `227154396`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154396/Additional+Content
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.325+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the **Additional Content** property to specify custom content for a desired element in addition to the standard one defined by the UML ownership. The custom content of an element is a collection of elements related to this element by some user-defined rule, such as some type of relationship.

To customize the content of a DSL element

1. Create a customization element for the DSL element or select an existing one.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and specifying derived properties' space='MD2024xR3'] for this customization element.
3. Specify a rule for gathering the additional content of the DSL element by creating an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining expressions' space='MD2024xR3'] for the derived property.
4. Define the derived property as a value of the Additional Content property.
5. Find the property in the general property group of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] of the customization element, under the Content category.

The custom content of the element will then be visible in the following places:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024xR3']**Find** dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- Rows and columns of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024xR3']

****

**Case Study**

Let’s say we need UML Packages to display not only their owned elements, but also the elements related to the Packages by the Element Import relationship. In order to obtain this, we must specify the rule for gathering the custom content of the Package. Then we will analyze the contents of the customized Package in the Containment tree.

To specify the rule for gathering the custom content of the Package

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] and add a customization element name (e.g., PackageCustomization) to it.
2. Define the Package metaclass as the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Target' space='MD2024xR3'] of the customization element.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and specifying derived properties' space='MD2024xR3'] for the customization element, name it importedElements , and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Simple Navigation' space='MD2024xR3'] as shown in the following figure. [ATTACHMENT filename='simple_expr_for_additionalContent.png']
4. In the general property group of the Specification window of the customization element, under the Content category, find the Additional Content property and click the cell of the property value.
5. Define the importedElements derived property as a value of the Additional Content property.

#### NOTE: Property

Property

- The Additional Content property can store a single value which can be only a derived property specification.
- Select the derived property specification from either the same customization element or another one with more abstract customization target. In this case, for example, this could also be the customization element with the Element metaclass as a customization target.

When you are done, the shape of the *PackageCustomization* element should look like the one depicted in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

6. Reopen the project.

To see how the **Additional Content** property affects the contents of the Package

1. Create a new diagram and draw in it a package named, e.g., P .
2. Draw two classes named A and B in the package.
3. Outside the package, draw a class named C and connect it to the package with the Element Import relationship. [ATTACHMENT filename='dgr_example_additionalContent.png']
4. Now take a look at the Containment tree. The package *P* contains both owned classes *A* and *B* as well as the class *C*, which is not owned, but related to the package *P* by the Import Element relationship. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties Description' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Target' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your First Customization' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and specifying derived properties' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining expressions' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="column"><p><span>Use the </span> <strong>Additional Content </strong> <span>property to specify custom content for a desired element in addition to the standard one defined by the UML ownership. The custom content of an element is a collection of elements related to this element by some user-defined rule, such as some type of relationship. </span></p></div><div class="column"><p><br /></p><p>To customize the content of a DSL element</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a customization element for the DSL element or select an existing one.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and specifying derived properties" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a derived property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for this customization element. </li><li>Specify a rule for gathering the additional content of the DSL element by creating an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining expressions" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expression ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>for the derived property. </li><li>Define the derived property as a value of the <strong>Additional Content</strong> property. </li><li>Find the property in the general property group of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link> of the customization element, under the <strong>Content</strong> category.</li></ol><p class="_mce_tagged_br"><span><br /></span></p><p class="_mce_tagged_br"><span>The custom content of the element will then be visible in the following places: </span></p><ul><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Find dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Find</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Selection dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br">Rows and columns of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[dependency matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Case Study</strong></p><p>Let’s say we need UML Packages to display not only their owned elements, but also the elements related to the Packages by the Element Import relationship. In order to obtain this, we must specify the rule for gathering the custom content of the Package. Then we will analyze the contents of the customized Package in the Containment tree. </p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the rule for gathering the custom content of the Package</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Profile diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[profile diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and add a customization element name (e.g., <em>PackageCustomization) </em>to it.</li><li>Define the Package metaclass as the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customization Target" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customization target]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the customization element.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and specifying derived properties" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a derived property specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the customization element, name it <em>importedElements</em>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[define the simple expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as shown in the following figure.<br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simple_expr_for_additionalContent.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Additional Content" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the general property group of the Specification window of the customization element, under the <strong>Content</strong> category, find the <strong>Additional Content</strong> property and click the cell of the property value.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Define the <em>importedElements</em> derived property as a value of the <strong>Additional Content</strong> property.</li></ol></div><div class="column"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29a35273-f0fb-42a7-88c3-b8ed34738ec3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Property</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><ul><li><span>The </span> <strong>Additional Content </strong> <span>property can store a single value which </span>can be only a derived property specification.</li><li>Select the derived property specification from either the same customization element or another one with more abstract customization target. In this case, for example, this could also be the customization element with the Element metaclass as a customization target. </li></ul></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">When you are done, the shape of the <em>PackageCustomization</em> element should look like the one depicted in the following figure. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_customization_with_additionalContent.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Additional Content" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">       6. Reopen the project.</p><div class="layoutArea _mce_tagged_br"><div class="column"><p><br /></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>To see how the </span> <strong>Additional Content </strong> <span>property affects the contents of the Package</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a new diagram and draw in it a package named, e.g., <em>P</em>.</li><li>Draw two classes named <em>A</em> and <em>B</em> in the package.</li><li>Outside the package, draw a class named <em>C</em> and connect it to the package with the Element Import relationship.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dgr_example_additionalContent.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Additional Content" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Now take a look at the Containment tree. The package </span> <em>P </em> <span>contains both owned classes </span> <em>A </em> <span>and </span> <em>B </em> <span>as well as the class </span> <span><em>C</em>, </span> <span>which is not owned, but related to the package <em>P</em> by the Import Element relationship.</span></p></div></div><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment_tree_additionalContent.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Additional Content" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol></div></div></div></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68d6f843-19aa-480d-81e1-df3547d11a1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Customization Data" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Customization Data" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customization Elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customization Element Properties Description" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customization Target" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating your First Customization" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and specifying derived properties" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining expressions" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151052 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00054: Address tab

- page_id: `227151052`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151052/Address+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:46.063+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks > Edit Hyperlink dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Active check box rule

61909249

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Active check box rule

61909251

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Active check box rule

61909250

The**Address** tab is dedicated to adding, editing, or deleteing hyperlinks to webpages and other external resources.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of the Address tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog. The text "units" links to the unit description in the NoMagic documentation.

All boxes and buttons of the **Address** tab are described in the table below.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text to display box | Shows the text that will be displayed as a hyperlink. If you select the text before opening this dialog, you will see that text in this box. If you change the displayed text in this box, it will be changed in the HTML text field too. If this field is empty, you can type a text and it will be added in the HTML text field.It is available only when adding a hyperlink on text. |
| Type the address box | Allows you to type or paste the hyperlink address. |
| Select from list box | Displays all previously used hyperlinks. You can select them to fill in the Type the address box. |
|  | Opens the hyperlink in the web browser specified in the Text to display box. |
| The Clear button | Deletes all previously used hyperlinks from the Select from list box. |
| The Active check box | Activates the hyperlink if selected. It is available only when adding a hyperlink on element or entire diagram.Active check box ruleOnly one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. |

61909248

**Related pages**

3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MD2024xR3']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
