<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154425 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: Advanced cases of properties visibility

- page_id: `227154425`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154425/Advanced+cases+of+properties+visibility
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.725+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Customizing Specification Window > Always Visible Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two methods of displaying stereotype properties that have not yet been applied:

- Profile application . Properties will be visible only when the owning package of an element has a corresponding profile application.
- Another stereotype and / or metaclass . Properties will be visible only when:
  - Another stereotype is already applied to an element.
  - An element’s metaclass is one of the metaclasses extended by the stereotype.

To make the visibility of stereotype properties dependent on a corresponding profile application

1. Open the customization element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] .
2. Click to change the Show Properties When Not Applied property to true .
3. Click to change the Show Properties When Not Applied Limited By Profile Application property to true .

To make the visibility stereotype properties dependent on another stereotype or a metaclass

1. Open the customization element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] .
2. Click to change the Show Properties When Not Applied property value to true .
3. Click the Show Properties When Not Applied Limited By Element Type property and then select a stereotype or a metaclass.

You can select more than one value.

###### Case Study

Let’s analyze the customization element of the «OraUser» stereotype in the*Oracle Customization*profile (*Oracle_Profile.mdzip*). This profile belongs to the*Cameo Data Modeler*plugin and can be used for database modeling.

If the «User» stereotype is selected as the**Show Properties When Not Applied Limited By Element Type**property value, properties of the «OraUser» stereotype will be visible only for elements that have the «User» stereotype applied.

If the**Show Properties When Not Applied Limited By Profile Application**property value is set to*true*, properties of the «OraUser» stereotype will be visible only for elements whose owning package has the*Oracle Customization*profile application, such aselements inside the Oracle schema.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification window of user that is NOT INSIDE Oracle schema

In conclusion, Oracle-specific properties will be visible only for users inside the Oracle schema.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification window of user that is INSIDE Oracle schema

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Specification Window' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Properties will be visible only when the owning package of an element has a corresponding profile application.</li><li><strong>Another stereotype and / or metaclass</strong>. Properties will be visible only when:<ul><li>Another stereotype is already applied to an element.</li><li>An element’s metaclass is one of the metaclasses extended by the stereotype.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To make the visibility of stereotype properties dependent on a corresponding profile application</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the customization element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click to change the <strong>Show Properties When Not Applied</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click to change the <strong>Show Properties When Not Applied Limited By Profile Application</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To make the visibility stereotype properties dependent on another stereotype or a metaclass</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the customization element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click to change the <strong>Show Properties When Not Applied</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Show Properties When Not Applied Limited By Element Type</strong> property and then select a stereotype or a metaclass.</li></ol></div><div class="column"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26562b17-94d8-4c4f-bf27-c1b195fef779"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select more than one value.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Case Study</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Let’s analyze the customization element of the «OraUser» stereotype in the </span><em>Oracle Customization </em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">profile (</span><em>Oracle_Profile.mdzip</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">). stereotype will be visible only for elements that have the «User» stereotype applied.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If the </span><strong>Show Properties When Not Applied Limited By Profile Application </strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">property value is set to </span><em>true</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, properties of the «OraUser» stereotype will be visible only for elements whose owning package has the </span><em>Oracle Customization </em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">profile application, such as </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">elements <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151164 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00056: Advanced HTML Editor dialog

- page_id: `227151164`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151164/Advanced+HTML+Editor+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-28T17:22:07.397+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > HTML editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Advanced HTML Editor allows you to change text style, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inserting images into HTML text' space='MD2024xR3'], insert symbols and tables, and perform other formatting actions.

To open the **Advanced HTML Editor** dialog

- Click the Advanced HTML Editor [ATTACHMENT filename='advanced_html_editor_button.png'] button on the HTML editor toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='html_editor_toolbar.png']

For more information, please see how to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024xR3'] in a dialog.

Click the**HTML source** tab to view the HTML source. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Advanced HTML Editor dialog

To insert an image into HTML text

1. Click the **Insert image** button, as shown in the following picture. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the Picture Properties dialog, specify the image location. Do one of the following:
  - Type the path to the image location.
  - Click **Browse** and select the image you want to insert. If you are working on a server project, make sure that the path to the image location is accessible from any computer that has an Internet connection. Otherwise, the image will not display when the project opens on another computer. To insert an image as an attached file, simply paste its URL to the **Picture Source** box by pressing Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inserting images into HTML text' space='MD2024xR3'].
3. Click OK when you are done.

The image is inserted into HTML text.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image in HTML text.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>The Advanced HTML Editor allows you to change text style, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Inserting images into HTML text" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[insert images]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, insert symbols and tables, and perform other formatting actions.</p><p>To open the <strong>Advanced HTML Editor</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Advanced HTML Editor  <ac:image ac:title="Advanced HTML Editor" ac:alt="Advanced HTML Editor"><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_html_editor_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button on the HTML editor toolbar.<br /><ac:image ac:height="21" ac:width="358"><ri:attachment ri:filename="html_editor_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Advanced HTML Editor dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p>For more information, please see how to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="HTML editor" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[turn on the HTML mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in a dialog. </p><p>Click the<strong> HTML source</strong> tab to view the HTML source. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:title="Advanced HTML Editor dialog" ac:alt="Advanced HTML Editor dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_html_editor_dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Advanced HTML Editor dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span>Advanced HTML Editor dialog</span></h6><p>To insert an image into HTML text</p><hr /><ol><li><span>Click the <strong>Insert image</strong> button, as shown in the following picture.</span><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="insert_image.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Advanced HTML Editor dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li>In the <strong>Picture Properties</strong> dialog, specify the image location. <br />Do one of the following: <ul><li>Type the path to the image location. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Browse</strong> and select the image you want to insert. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f807e2bb-6a56-4d8e-9a72-7062139f3563"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are working on a server project, make sure that the path to the image location is accessible from any computer that has an Internet connection. Otherwise, the image will not display when the project opens on another computer. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">To insert an image as an attached file, simply paste its URL to the <strong>Picture Source</strong> box by pressing Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Inserting images into HTML text" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Inserting into HTML text]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p></li></ul></li><li>Click OK when you are done. </li></ol><p>The image is inserted into HTML text. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Image in HTML text.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Advanced HTML Editor dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span>Image in HTML text.</span></h6><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150355 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00057: Alternative Fragment

- page_id: `227150355`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150355/Alternative+Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-15T14:49:37.667+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Fragment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

The alternative fragment models *if...then...else* constructions.

To draw an Alternative Fragment

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] pallet, select the Alternatives element to draw.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fragment' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.</p><p>The alternative fragment models <em>if...then...else</em> constructions. </p><p><br /></p><p>To draw an Alternative Fragment</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link> pallet, select the Alternatives element to draw. </li></ul><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 5.54.15 PM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Alternative Fragment" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Fragment" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154421 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00058: Always Visible Properties

- page_id: `227154421`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154421/Always+Visible+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-02T05:52:44.747+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Customizing Specification Window
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1371361366

1371361379

1371361369

Generally, stereotype properties become visible in an element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] only after the stereotype is applied to the element. However, you may customize stereotype properties to be visible in the element Specification window even if the stereotype is not yet applied to the element.

This feature allows specifying domain-specific properties for standard UML elements. It is also used to make [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3'] properties (**Type Modifier**, **Active Hyperlink**, and **To Do**, etc.) visible even if appropriate stereotypes are not yet applied to an element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Always visible properties in UML property Specification Window.

To make stereotype properties visible when the stereotype is not yet applied to an element

1. Open the customization element Specification window.
2. Click to change the **Show Properties When Not Applied** property to *true*. All properties of the customized stereotype will become visible in the DSL element Specification window even if the stereotype is not yet applied to the element.

1371361365

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Specification Window' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Advanced cases of properties visibility' space='MD2024xR3']

It is also used to make <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> properties (<strong>Type Modifier</strong>, <strong>Active Hyperlink</strong>, and <strong>To Do</strong>, etc.) visible even if appropriate stereotypes are not yet applied to an element. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="always_visible_props_in_MagicDraw_property_spec.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Always Visible Properties" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Always visible properties in UML property Specification Window.</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To make stereotype properties visible when the stereotype is not yet applied to an element</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Open the customization element Specification window. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Click to change the <strong>Show Properties When Not Applied</strong> property to <em>true</em>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152086 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00062: Analyzing dependencies among elements

- page_id: `227152086`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152086/Analyzing+dependencies+among+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-12T19:35:58.987+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Identifying Package Dependencies
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before exporting or sharing a package as an independent used project, you need to identify and solve package dependencies first. More specifically, you have to make sure that the packagedoes not depend on any external elements (except other used projects). Furthermore, cyclic dependencies between several used projects are not allowed.

There are three types of dependencies:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing dependencies among elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing dependencies among elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing dependencies among elements' space='MD2024xR3']

##### relationshipPackage dependencies by relationship

The used project depends on the external elements which have relationships with the model elements from the used project packages.

external

###### The used project depends on external elements

If a used project's element has a relationship with an external element and that relationship is contained in the used project package, an error message appears when exporting the used package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the used project dependency on an external element

Such dependencies on external elements are displayed in the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Package depends on an external element

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Error in the Package Dependencies panel

In this situation, a modeling tool can suggest moving the relationship into the parent package of this external element. For example, the package *Items* is a parent of a class *Book*, so the relationship can be moved from the *Items* into the package *Library*:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resolved package dependency on an external element

Some movements can be achieved by clicking **Solve** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024xR3']**Package Dependencies** panel.

###### The used project depends on an external element, but can be exported (with warning)

Even though the used project element has a relationship with an external element, that relationship is contained in an external package:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a “legal” used project dependency on an external element

In this case, the dependency on an external element is displayed in the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### “Legal” used project dependency on an external element in the Model Browser

The package can be exported as the used project because the relationship is contained in an external package.

###### The used project does not depend on an external element

If the used project element has a relationship with an external element it is irrelevant in the context of UML (for instance, the external model uses the used project, but not vice versa) and that relationship is contained in an external model, the package can be exported into an independent used project:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a relationship when the used project does not depend on an external element

##### referenceDependencies by reference

The used project depends on the external elements which have references to the model elements from the used project packages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of a dependency by reference

In this case, the package *Items* cannot be exported to an independent project because this package has reference to the *Library* package.

##### dependenciesDiagram dependencies

The diagram depends on all model elements displayed within it.

If the diagram is contained in the package *Items* and depends on external elements, this package cannot be exported to a separate project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the relationship when a diagram depends on an external element

For more information about the package dependencies on external elements, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing dependencies among elements' space='MD2024xR3'].

In this case, if the diagram is not important to the used project, it can be moved from the used project package into any external package by dragging-and-dropping it within the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram is moved from the package *Items* to the package *Library*

**

The package*Items* can now be exported into an independent project.

Teamwork Cloud is an ideal solution for a collaborative work on the same project. For more information about using a modeling tool in the collaborative environment, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborative modeling' space='MD2024xR3'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Unresolved dependencies' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Before exporting or sharing a package as an independent used project, you need to identify and solve package dependencies first. More specifically, you have to make sure that the package</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> does not depend on any external elements (except other used projects). Furthermore, cyclic dependencies between several used projects are not allowed.</span></p><p>There are three types of dependencies:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="relationship"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package dependencies by relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing dependencies among elements" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151590 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00063: Analyzing traceability relations

- page_id: `227151590`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151590/Analyzing+traceability+relations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:53.489+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1977687758

1977687761

1977687760

You can perform the impact and/or gap analysis in your project using the Dependency Matrix feature, which is a powerful way for representing traceability relations between multiple elements from different packages, levels of abstraction, views, or other relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, for example, relations through UML tags.You can create your own dependency matrices or even custom dependency matrix types for visualization of various traceability relations. You only need to define a relevant traceability property as **dependency criteria** for this.

The following figure depicts an example of traceability relations between several model elements.

###### Example of traceability relations

These relations can be represented by the following traceability properties:

- Realizing relations (use cases classes):
  - Realizing Class
  - Realizing Element
  - All Realizing Elements
- Specifying relations (classes use cases):
  - Specifying Use Case
  - Specifying Element
  - All Specifying Elements

You can create a dependency matrix to visualize these traceability relations.The following steps will show you how to create a dependency matrix for the Realizing Class predefined property.

To create a dependency matrix for the Realizing Class predefined property:

1. Create a Dependency Matrix diagram.
2. Define row and column type element types as follows:
  1. For the Row Element Type, select UseCase
  2. For the Column Element Type, select Class
3. Define row and column scopes as follows:
  1. For the Row Scope, click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] > Requirements > MagicTest and select Administration .
  2. For the column scope, click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] > Design > MagicTest and select UI .
4. Define the Realizing Class property as Dependency Criteria.
5. Rebuild the matrix (click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_button.png'] in the diagram toolbar). [IMAGE alt='' src='']Defining criteria for visualization of traceability relationships that can be represented by Realizing Class predefined property

The following figure depicts the created Dependency Matrix showing the traceability relationships between use cases and design classes they realize:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visualization of Realizing Class traceability relations on Dependency Matrix

1977687757

- Traceability

- 
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules
  - Custom traceability rules

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151514 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00064: Analyzing usages and dependencies

- page_id: `227151514`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151514/Analyzing+usages+and+dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:40:33.124+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

525949422

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

525949425

525960400

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

525949424

No Magic modeling tools provide a way to track and analyze element usages and dependencies in models, understand the relationships between used and dependent elements, and find the diagrams where these elements are represented. You can do all of this using the commands **Used By** and **Depends On**, as described below.

##### Used By command

The **Used By** command allows you to find all the elements that use a selected element (e.g., as property, tag etc.). This also includes usage in diagrams.

#### TIP: Example

Example

In the figure below, the *Computer* Propertyhas its type set to the *WorkStation* Class.**In other words, the *Computer* Property uses the *WorkStation* Class as its type. If you execute the **Used By** command for the *WorkStation* Class, you will get the*Computer* Property as one of the results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window showing that the *WorkStation* Class is used by the *Computer* Property.

Note that containing (owning) other elements is not considered usage. For example, when a Package contains a Class, this does not mean that the Package uses the Class. The Package is only a container of the Class.

##### Depends On command

Use the **Depends On** command to find all elements used by a selected element (e.g., properties, tags etc.).

#### TIP: Example

Example

Let's say you have a model with the content tree shown in the figure below. Execute the **Depends On** command for the*Workplace Infrastructure*Package to see how its content depends on the outer elements (not owned by the Package) of the model. One of the search results shows that the*Workplace Infrastructure*Package depends on the *Power Socket* Class, which is the type of several Ports owned by this package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The content tree of a sample model with the *Workplace Infrastructure*Package having dependencies to the outer elements of the model.

Note that****in this particular situation you must select the **Search recursively** check box in the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog. This allows you to search for dependencies of the Package content rather than the Package itself. Also, select the **Ignore inner interrelations** checkbox to eliminate the dependencies between the elements contained by the package from the search results.

##### Searching for usages and dependencies

You can find usages and dependencies of a particular model element by opening its shortcut menu from the model browser or a diagram.

To search for usages/dependencies of a model element

1. In the model browser or a diagram right-click the element, the usages or dependencies of which you want to find.
2. In the shortcut menu of the element click Related Elements , and select Used By or Depends On (depending on what you want to find).
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog' space='MD2024xR3']**Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog , select the search options you want to use.
4. Click OK to start the search.

The search results are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and dependencies search results window' space='MD2024xR3'] at the bottom of the modeling tool. The search results window also provides additional information, such as element type and how a particular element is used by/in the element for which you perform the search.

525949420

**Related pages**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model analysis' space='MD2024xR3']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d31b9b07-9d1f-4173-9d3b-f9e7eb75b0f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949422</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07d951e4-c5ea-4647-b07e-a2f62aee41a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949425</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17e0fa6d-3b88-4943-8f41-b718cbd6f418"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525960400</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a67174c8-0758-4cb1-9217-9866c29b4a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7cace291-24eb-4e5a-99ec-db11ed9663fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949424</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>No Magic modeling tools provide a way to track and analyze element usages and dependencies in models, understand the relationships between used and dependent elements, and find the diagrams where these elements are represented. You can do all of this using the commands <strong>Used By</strong> and <strong>Depends On</strong>, as described below.</p><h3><br />Used By command</h3><p>The <strong>Used By</strong> command allows you to find all the elements that use a selected element (e.g., as property, tag etc.). This also includes usage in diagrams.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27671e0f-2ee2-4192-9030-d88960cb29e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the figure below, the <em>Computer</em> <span>Property </span>has its type set to the <em>WorkStation</em> Class.<em> </em>In other words, the <em>Computer</em> Property uses the <em>WorkStation</em> Class as its type. If you execute the <strong>Used By</strong> command for the <em>WorkStation</em> Class, you will get <span>the </span><em>Computer</em> Property as one of the results.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_by_sample.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: left;">The Specification window showing that the <em>WorkStation</em> Class is used by the <em>Computer</em> Property.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />Note that containing (owning) other elements is not considered usage. For example, when a Package contains a Class, this does not mean that the Package uses the Class. The Package is only a container of the Class.</p><h3><br />Depends On command</h3><p>Use the <strong>Depends On</strong> command to find all elements used by a selected element (e.g., properties, tags etc.).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c71748e-acd0-4f14-aae5-133bad652a62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Let's say you have a model with the content tree shown in the figure below. Execute the <strong>Depends On</strong> command for <span>the </span><em>Workplace Infrastructure</em><span> Package</span> to see how its content depends on the outer elements <span>(not owned by the Package)</span> of the model. One of the search results shows that <span>the </span><em>Workplace Infrastructure</em><span> Package depends on the <em>Power Socket</em> Class, which is the type of several Ports owned by this package.</span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="depends_on_sample.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: left;"><span>The content tree of a sample model with the <em>Workplace Infrastructure</em><span> Package having dependencies to the outer elements of the model.</span></span></h6><p style="text-align: left;"><span>Note that<strong> </strong>in this particular situation you must select the <strong>Search recursively</strong> check box in the <strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong> dialog. This allows you to search for dependencies of the Package content rather than the Package itself. Also, select the <strong>Ignore inner interrelations</strong> checkbox to eliminate the dependencies between the elements contained by the package from the search results.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><br />Searching for usages and dependencies</h3><p>You can find usages and dependencies of a particular model element by opening its shortcut menu from the model browser or a diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for usages/dependencies of a model element</p><hr /><ol><li>In the model browser or a diagram right-click the element, the usages or dependencies of which you want to find.</li><li>In the shortcut menu of the element click <strong>Related Elements</strong>, and select <strong>Used By</strong> or <strong>Depends On</strong> (depending on what you want to find).</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the search options you want to use.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to start the search.</li></ol><p><br />The search results are listed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Usages and dependencies search results window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[usages/dependencies search results window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> at the bottom of the modeling tool. The search results window also provides additional information, such as element type and how a particular element is used by/in the element for which you perform the search.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07e1ba14-ca1f-4b39-ab88-405f62c9ceeb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949420</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong class="confluence-link">Related pages</strong></p><p><strong class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b1f7fc5f-ff6e-420c-939d-92549e7c25e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model analysis" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150188 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00065: Anchor

- page_id: `227150188`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150188/Anchor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-11-14T08:58:08.272+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907355913

907355917

907355915

Use an Anchor to relate an element to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MD2024xR3'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comment' space='MD2024xR3']. By default, a newly created Anchor links an element with a note.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Anchor relating an Action to a Note.

You can format the anchor symbol properties in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR3']** dialog.

To draw an anchor from a symbol

1. On the diagram's pane, select a symbol: class, signal, data type, etc.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar appearing on the side of the symbol, click on the Anchor button.
3. If you drag the shadowed Anchor anywhere on the diagram pane, a new note appears anchored to the symbol.
4. Or you can click the note or comment to which you want to anchor the element .
5. The Anchor is created for the symbol.

To draw an anchor from the diagram pane

1. Click on the diagram's p ane to create a new anchored note.
2. Drag it anywhere on the diagram pane and see a new anchored note appear.
3. Now, a new note is anchored to your diagram pane.

To change an anchor path style

1. On the diagram pane, select an anchor and open its shortcut menu.
2. Select **Symbol Properties**.
3. Select the Path Style property, then choose one of the following path styles: Rectilinear , Oblique , or Bezier . [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.34.24 PM.png']

907355912

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154780 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00066: ANSI Profile Constraints

- page_id: `227154780`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154780/ANSI+Profile+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-12T15:59:42.904+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter describes all constraints added by the ANSI profile.

###### Operation

- isQuery set to true only applies to a member operation (non global).
- virtual tag set to true only applies to a non static member operation.

###### Constructor

- operation with «C++Constructor» stereotype.
- Name should be the same as the owner’s name.

###### Destructor

- operation with «C++Destructor» stereotype.
- Name should be ‘~’ + the same name as the owner’s name.

###### Global

Class with «C++Global» stereotype:

- Name length should be 0.
- All operations and attributes should be public.
- There can be only one global class by namespace.
- Owner of a global class can only be a package

###### Typedef

Class with «C++Typedef» stereotype:

- One and only one «C++BaseType» dependency from a «C++Typedef» class.
- «C++BaseType» supplier dependency can only be a Classifier or Operation.
- «C++Typedef» class cannot contain an attribute and operation.
- Only one inner Class or inner Enumeration is valid.

###### Friend

Dependency with «C++Friend» stereotype:

- «C++Friend» client dependency can only be Class or Operation.
- «C++Friend» supplier dependency can only be Class.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

stereotype.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name should be the same as the owner’s name.</span></li></ul><h5><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Destructor</span></h5><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">operation with «C++Destructor» stereotype.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name should be ‘~’ + the same name as the owner’s name.</span></li></ul><h5><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Global</span></h5><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Class with «C++Global» stereotype:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name length should be 0.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">All operations and attributes should be public. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">There can be only one global class by namespace.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Owner of a global class can only be a package</span></li></ul><h5><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Typedef</span></h5><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Class with «C++Typedef» stereotype:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">One and only one «C++BaseType» dependency from a «C++Typedef» class.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">«C++BaseType» supplier dependency can only be a </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Classifier or <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154075 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00067: Appendix A: report extensions

- page_id: `227154075`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154075/Appendix+A+report+extensions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T10:18:56.274+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352447286

1352447286

1352447286

Report Wizard has been enhanced with an extension that provides additional functionalities (for example, a Custom Tool that encapsulates Java functions in context). The report extensions are Java class and related resources collected into JAR archives, which are merely ZIP files that contain a specially formatted “manifest” file describing the contents of the archive.

To install the extension, copy the *.jar* file into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. The archives stored within subdirectories will not be loaded in Report Wizard, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure.

The extension deployed under the global extensions directory will affect all templates, which means you can use the deployed Custom Tool in all templates. Extensions deployed under a template package will affect only the specific template.

1352447285

**Related pages**

The report extensions are Java class and related resources collected into JAR archives, which are merely ZIP files that contain a specially formatted “manifest” file describing the contents of the archive.</p><p>To install the extension, copy the <em>.jar</em> file into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. The archives stored within subdirectories will not be loaded in Report Wizard, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure" ac:alt="Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extensions Deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Appendix A: report extensions" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure.</h6><p>The extension deployed under the global extensions directory will affect all templates, which means you can use the deployed Custom Tool in all templates. Extensions deployed under a template package will affect only the specific template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d608ca77-9395-42b0-8788-59f20f350bfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352447285</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5e747ab4-111f-402e-a497-b2836dc5f39c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154086 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00068: Appendix B: office open XML format template

- page_id: `227154086`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154086/Appendix+B+office+open+XML+format+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T10:30:28.825+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1353187275

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1353187276

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1353187276

Office Open XML (also referred to as OOXML or Open XML) refers to the standardized file formats of spreadsheets, charts, presentations, and word processing documents for Microsoft Office. These standardized file formats become free and open as ECMA-376 Office Open XML File Formats - 2nd edition (December 2008) and ISO/IEC 29500:2008.

The most common Open XML file formats that Report Wizard supports include:

- DOCX - for word processing (text) documents
- XLSX - for spreadsheets
- PPTX - for presentations

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Support Office Open XML (DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX) and Microsoft Office 2007 and newer versions.

1353187274

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d02f663a-821e-40bb-b8e7-33805fa42eaa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1353187275</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a20f6b3-1ed2-41e1-ab3d-574d13f51009"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1353187276</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f8eefe0-6337-447b-adbf-ec131dd013cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1353187276</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Office Open XML (also referred to as OOXML or Open XML) refers to the standardized file formats of spreadsheets, charts, presentations, and word processing documents for Microsoft Office. These standardized file formats become free and open as ECMA-376 Office Open XML File Formats - 2nd edition (December 2008) and ISO/IEC 29500:2008.</p><p>The most common Open XML file formats that Report Wizard supports include:</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">DOCX - for word processing (text) documents</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">XLSX - for spreadsheets</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">PPTX - for presentations<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5d0a28a-a54d-4169-8fd3-e2b3dd607c4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Support Office Open XML (DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX) and Microsoft Office 2007 and newer versions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fe475f6-b4ef-42e0-a225-4f6d172632c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1353187274</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="41ce9c56-a86e-4974-abb0-6e4b7205c939" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154132 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: Appendix C: OpenDocument format template

- page_id: `227154132`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154132/Appendix+C+OpenDocument+format+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T11:06:48.165+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355361738

1355361739

1355361739

The OpenDocument format (*ODF*) is an open file format for office documents, such as spreadsheets, presentations, and word processing documents. The standard was developed by the Open Office *XML* technical committee of the Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards (OASIS) consortium.

The version 1.0 manifestation was published as an ISO/IEC International Standard, ISO/IEC 26300:2006 Open Document Format for Office Applications (OpenDocument) v1.0.

The most common files supported by Report Wizard are:

- ODT for word processing (text) documents
- ODS for spreadsheets
- ODP for presentations

1355361738

**Related pages**

The standard was developed by the Open Office <em>XML</em> technical committee of the Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards (OASIS) consortium.</p><p>The version 1.0 manifestation was published as an ISO/IEC International Standard, ISO/IEC 26300:2006 Open Document Format for Office Applications (OpenDocument) v1.0.</p><p>The most common files supported by Report Wizard are:</p><ul><li><em>ODT</em> <br />for word processing (text) documents</li><li><em>ODS</em> <br />for spreadsheets</li><li><em>ODP</em> <br />for presentations</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="019e42de-51d9-4046-a350-f1f7095ec904"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355361738</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bdc85593-179d-4d8d-8bc5-7696d304540a" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00070: Appendix D: HTML tag support

- page_id: `227154158`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154158/Appendix+D+HTML+tag+support

- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:38:39.132+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports *HTML* code conversion to *RTF, ODF,* and *OOXML* file formats. The *HTML* code is created from the Element Documentation pane, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation Pane.

The element documentation can also be retrieved by the following *VTL* code:

```text

```

Whenever the report engine encounters *HTML* content, it will automatically convert the content into a valid output format style.

The <em>HTML</em> code is created from the Element Documentation pane, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="MagicDraw Documentation Pane" ac:alt="MagicDraw Documentation Pane"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MD doc panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Appendix D: HTML tag support" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">The Documentation Pane.</h6><p>The element documentation can also be retrieved by the following <em>VTL</em> code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05929382-6822-42a5-943c-1eea4ff279ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($e in $elements)

````html
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Whenever the report engine encounters <em>HTML</em> content, it will automatically convert the content into a valid output format style.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154230 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: Appendix E: DocBook Support

- page_id: `227154230`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154230/Appendix+E+DocBook+Support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T11:39:33.062+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1357340520

1357340520

1357340520

DocBook was built as an application to write technical documents related to computer hardware and software. It is a semantic markup language that was extended from XML language. As an open source application, DocBook has been adopted as a standard to write other types of documentation as well. The current version of DocBook, published by O'Reilly Media and XML Press, is 5.1.

The Report Wizard’s DocBook engine enables you to input the *VTL* codes inside DocBook’s document content. It also allows you to convert HTML into DocBook tags, rearrange tags, and escape special characters. Report Wizard uses “*dbk*” and “*docbook*” file extensions to save DocBook files. The figure below shows the DocBook’s document content.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1357340519

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154313 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: Applying Different Icons for the Same Stereotype

- page_id: `227154313`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154313/Applying+Different+Icons+for+the+Same+Stereotype
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:47.498+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles > Creating Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A modeling tool provides the ability to create a stereotype, which uses different icons depending on a stereotype “kind." For example, PseudoState can be represented by different notations that depend on PseudoStateKind.

To apply different icons on the same stereotype

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='MD2024xR3'] (in our example, *supercarKind*) to define all stereotype “kinds."
2. Apply the «iconHolder» stereotype to the enumeration.
3. Create as many EnumerationLiterals of as many different kinds as you need (in our example, it is “ ferrari ” and “ audi ”).
4. Apply stereotypes with different icons to every EnumerationLiteral (in our example, «ferrari» and «audi» ).
5. Create a Stereotype (in our example, supercar ) with a property (tag definition), with type enumeration ( supercarKind ). You could also select a default value.
6. Assign the created stereotype to an element and select one of the enumeration values in tags. A stereotyped element will change its icon to the enumeration value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of applying icons.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='MD2024xR3']

You could also select a default value.</li><li>Assign the created stereotype to an element and select one of the enumeration values in tags. A stereotyped element will change its icon to the enumeration value. </li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="superkind_stereotype.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Different Icons for the Same Stereotype" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of applying icons.</h6></div></div></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b4bf114-495e-42f4-8c57-7f52bd93aeab"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enumeration" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150982 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00073: Applying image

- page_id: `227150982`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150982/Applying+image
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:44.928+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Symbol display mode

763606666

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Symbol display mode

763606668

764053582

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Symbol display mode

763606667

##### Ways to apply image on a symbol

You can apply an image on a symbol using:

44

###### The Image Library tab

To apply an image on a symbol using the**Image Library** tab

1. From the Window menu, select Image Library. The Image Library tab opens on the right side of the window.
2. Select the image you want to apply from the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Image Library tab' space='MD2024xR3']Image Library tab tree.
3. Drag the image directly on the symbol. The image is applied on a symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Image dialog

To apply an image on a symbol using **Select Image** dialog

1. Open the **Select Image** dialog by doing one of the following: Only mentioned elements support the **Image Library** button on the smart manipulator by default. You can customize the smart manipulator of any other element.
  - Open the Specification window of any element, select **Image**(**Icon**for Stereotype) property cell and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Select Class, Actor, Port, Block, Part, Node, Performer, Concept Role, Stereotype, or Text Box ((HTML Text) on the diagram pane and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
2. Select the image you want to apply.
3. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The image is applied on a symbol.

###### The external resources

To apply an image on a symbol from the external resources

1. Select an image from any external resource (e.g. web browser, email). Image formatsYou can apply images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.
2. Drag it directly on a symbol. 
The image is applied on a symbol. If the the image that you applied on a symbol is large, you can resize that symbol to a preferred size automatically.To resize a symbol to a preferred sizea. Select the symbol with an image applied. 
b. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] smart manipulator that appears at the right bottom side of the shape’s horizontal border.

##### Applying image to Property or its type

When applying image to the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024xR3']Propertyshape (which type is specified) by draggingor selecting an image from the[Image Library](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab) or [other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources), and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting port and part types' space='MD2024xR3']**Type Selection Mode** is switched on,the image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default. For example, if you drag an image on the *workplace* Property shape that type is *Workplace* Class, the image will be applied to the Class but not to the Property.

#### WARNING: Exceptions

Exceptions

- If the Property already has the image applied, the new image replaces it and is set to the Property but not to it's type.
- If the Property type already has an image applied, you cannot replace it with the new one by dragging it on Property shape.

To apply image to Property type

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR3'], switch on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting port and part types' space='MD2024xR3']**Type Selection Mode**.
2. Select an image from the [Image Library](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab) or [other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources).
3. Drag selected image on the Property shape which: 
 - Type is specified. 
 - The Property is without image. 
 - The Property type is without image. 
The image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default. You can specify to apply image on Property by changing the **Apply Image To** property value.

#### NOTE: Symbol display mode

Symbol display mode

You can change the symbol display mode. [How to change the stereotype name and its icon visibility on an element's shape >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Stereotype#Stereotype-Changingthestereotypedisplaymode)

To specify the **Apply Image To** property value

1. On the main menu, select the **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the project Options dialog, find **Apply Image To** property.
3. Select one of the following value: 
 - **Type and Notify** (default) - to apply image to Property type and after that show notification message on the right corner of the screen. 
 - **Type** - to apply image to Property type. 
 - **Property** - to apply image to Property.
4. Click **OK**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can customize the smart manipulator of any other element.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p></li><li><span>Select the image you want to apply.</span></li><li><span><span class="confluence-link">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span><br class="confluence-link" /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Select Image dialog" ac:alt="Select Image dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_image_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying image" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">The image is applied on a symbol.</span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4>The external resources</h4><p>To apply an image on a symbol from the external resources</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select an image from any external resource (e.g. web browser, email).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e934d2a1-e42d-4e81-85de-988abbb68835"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Image formats</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Drag it directly on a symbol.<span> </span><br /><span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">The image is applied on a symbol.</span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e69884e0-1129-415c-ae39-3ec2d1884d5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the the image that you applied on a symbol is large, you can resize that symbol to a preferred size automatically.</p><p>To resize a symbol to a preferred size</p><hr /><p>a. Select the symbol with an image applied.<br />b. Click <ac:image ac:title="Make Preferred Size" ac:alt="Make Preferred Size"><ri:attachment ri:filename="make_preferred_size.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying image" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> smart manipulator that appears at the right bottom side of the shape’s horizontal border.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3><span class="confluence-link">Applying image to Property or its type<br /></span></h3><p><span class="confluence-link">When applying image to <span class="confluence-link"> the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Property</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link>shape (which type is specified) by dragging<span class="confluence-link"> or selecting an image from the<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab"> Image Library</a> or <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources">other resources</a></span>, <span class="confluence-link">and the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting port and part types" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Type Selection Mode</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is switched on, </span>the image is applied to the Property type <span class="confluence-link"> and the notification message is shown by default</span>. <span class="confluence-link"> For example, if you drag an image on the <em>workplace</em> Property shape that type is <em>Workplace</em> Class, the image will be applied to the Class but not to the Property.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c65f7c27-0e83-4d88-a53b-8bb00e7527ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Exceptions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">If the Property already has the image applied, the new image replaces it and is set to the Property but not to it's type</span></span>.</span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">If the <span class="confluence-link">Property type already has an image applied</span>, you cannot replace it with the new one by dragging it on Property shape.<br /></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></span></p><p><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">To apply image to Property type</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, switch on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting port and part types" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Type Selection Mode</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">Select an image from the <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab"> Image Library</a> or <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources">other resources</a></span></span>.</span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">Drag selected image on the Property shape which:<br />    - <span class="confluence-link">Type is specified</span>.<br />    - The Property is without image.<br />    - The Property type <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">is without image</span></span>.</span></span><br /><span class="confluence-link">The image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default.</span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"> You can specify to apply image on Property by changing the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Apply Image To</strong> property</span> value. <br /><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd0300b9-7b48-436c-aa10-bda05dd153f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Symbol display mode</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the symbol display mode. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Stereotype#Stereotype-Changingthestereotypedisplaymode">How to change the stereotype name and its icon visibility on an element's shape &gt;&gt;</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-link">To specify the <strong>Apply Image To</strong> property value<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link">On the main menu, select the <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">In the project Options dialog, find <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Apply Image To</strong></span> property.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Select one of the following value:<br /> - <strong>Type and Notify</strong> (default) - to apply image to Property type and after that show notification message on the right corner of the screen.<br /> - <strong>Type</strong> - <span class="confluence-link">to apply image to Property type</span>.<br /> - <strong>Property</strong> - <span class="confluence-link">to apply image to Property.</span><br class="confluence-link" /></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150061 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: Applying layout template

- page_id: `227150061`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150061/Applying+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:40:17.162+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

59477430

59477432

59477431

If you want to use the layout template in another diagram (usually the Composite Structure diagram), the following conditions must be met beforehand:

- The Part must be typed by the Class or subtype of Class in the Composite Structure diagram.
- The appearance of the Part type Class or subtype of Class must be defined in the Class diagram.
- That Class diagram must be set as the layout template.

You can apply layout templates directly from the diagram toolbar or shape's shortcut menu by using the **Apply Template** command. This command provides different suggestions in the following situations:

- Apply Template without drop-down arrow appears when only one layout template is available for the selected element.
- Apply Template with drop-down arrow appears when multiple layout templates are available for the selected element. The multiple layout templates are listed according to the following rules:

If you set the default layout template, it is always the first on the list, and other templates are listed alphabetically. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting layout template as default' space='MD2024xR3'].If you have not set the default layout template, all layout templates are listed alphabetically.

The procedures below explain how to apply the layout template to:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying layout template' space='MD2024xR3']
- Class and allclassifiers[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying layout template' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying layout template' space='MD2024xR3']

###### Applying a layout template to the Part

There are several different ways you can apply a layout template to a Part. Use one of the workflows below depending on your situation.

To apply a layout template to the Part Property displayed in the Composite Structure Diagram

- Do one of the following:
  - Right click the Part Property shape to which you want to apply the layout template and select Display > Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

To apply a layout template when creating a new Part Property in the Composite Structure diagram

- 
  - Drag the Class that is the type of the Part Property you want to create on the diagram pane and do one of the following:
    - Drop the Class.
    - Hold down the mouse button until the tooltip appears (long drag), then release the mouse button and select the Create Property Symbol and Apply Default Template command.

###### Applying a layout template to the Class and all classifiers

You can apply a layout template for the Class and all classifiers that are set as the layout template, but are represented in other Class diagrams. To do this, follow the procedure below.

To apply a layout template to the Class and all classifiers

1. Select the Class shape or another classifier shape that has the layout template defined.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the shape and select Display > Layout Template > **Apply Template**. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

###### Applying a layout template to the diagram frame

When creating the internal structure for the Class that has a layout template defined, you can keep the same layout and style of its Ports on the Composite Structure Diagram frame.

To apply a layout template to the diagram frame

1. Select the diagram frame.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the diagram and select Display > Layout Template > **Apply Template**. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

The example below illustrates how the appearance of Ports defined in*the Air Transfer System* layout template is applied to the Internal Block Diagram of the *Air Transfer System* Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout template applied on the *Air Transfer System* Internal Block Diagram frame.

59477428

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable** which you can download here: *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*
