<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227149889 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00075: Applying Legend Items

- page_id: `227149889`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227149889/Applying+Legend+Items
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:28.645+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends > Specifying Legend Items
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Adorned Properties

1283264726

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Adorned Properties

1283264728

1283264725

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Adorned Properties

1283264727

When you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024xR3'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='MD2024xR3'] a Legend in a diagram or table, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Item adornment' space='MD2024xR3'] of its Legend Items isappliedto the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements. You can define the elements to which a Legend Item should be applied by specifying one of its properties in the **Apply To** property group. Legend Items can be applied either to**Specific Elements** or to **Elements By Condition**. If you apply a Legend to a table, you can also specify the **Adorned Properties** property, which defines what cells of the relevant table rows should be adorned.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

You can specify either the **Specific Elements**or the **Elements by Condition** property of a Legend Item, but not both.

##### Applying Legend Items to specific elements

You can apply a Legend Item to one or multiple specific elements as described below.

To apply a Legend Item to specific elements

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item you want to apply.
2. Select the property specification cell of the Specific Elements property, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
3. On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the elements to which you want to apply the Legend Item.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_button_long.png'] to add them to the Selected elements area, and click OK .
5. Close the Specification window.

You can also apply one or multiple Legend Items to an element from its symbol.

To apply Legend Items to an element from its symbol

1. In a diagram, right-click the shape of the element to which you want to apply Legend Items, and in the menu, select Legend Item .
2. In the open dialog, select the Legend Items you want to apply, and click the Apply button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example how to apply Legend Items from an element shape.

##### Applying Legend Items to elements by conditionApplying Legend Items to elements by condition

Applying a Legend Item to **Elements by Condition** ensures that it is consistently applied to all the elements that meet the defined condition, even after you change your model.

**Example**

Let's say that you want to apply a Legend Item to all requirements that have a high-risk level. The quickest way to do it is to create a Property Test operation. Follow the steps below to analyze this specific use case and define the **Elements by Condition** property for the Legend Item:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item.
2. Select the specification cell of the **Elements by Condition** property, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. In the **Element by Condition** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Element Type** box.
4. In the Property Test operation specification area of the **Elements by Condition** dialog, select the **risk** property and set its value to **High**. Default optionsBy default, the **Language** is set to **StructuredExpression,** and the Property Test operation is selected in the **Elements by Condition** dialog.
5. Click OK , and close the Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Elements by Condition** dialog with the Property Test operation defined to test if Requirements have a high-risk level.

After following the above steps, the Legend Item is applied to all Requirements that have the **Risk** property set to **high**. If this use case does not meet your needs, you may define advanced Property Test operation properties. You may also[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='MD2024xR3']of a different type, or create several operations for a single condition, as long as it returns a Boolean data type value (*true*or*false*). In addition, you can define a condition using a different**Language**setting.

##### Applying Legend Items to specific cells of a table

When a Legend is used in a table, you can specify not only the elements to which specific Legend Items should apply (the **Specific Elements** or **Elements By Condition** properties) but also the desired properties of these elements. In other words, you can adorn specific cells of a table instead of adorning entire table rows. To do this, use the **Adorned Properties** property of a Legend Item.

#### NOTE: Adorned Properties

Adorned Properties

The **Adorned Properties** property of a Legend Item is only effective if you use the Legend in a table.

**Example**

Let's say you have a Requirements Table with three columns: *Name,* *Text,* and *Risk*., and you want to apply two legends in that table: *Risk* and *Status*. When you create or use a Legend in a table, Legend Item adornments are normally applied to enitre table rows. So in this case, you also have to specify certain element properties (table columns) to which Legend Items of each Legend should be applied. To reproduce this spesific use case, follow these steps:

1. Create a Requirement Table with the Name , Text and Risk columns.
2. In the table, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024xR3'] to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend Risk .
3. Create and define the Legend Items for the Risk Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='MD2024xR3'] indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them High , Medium , and Low .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Risk property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Specify **Adorned Properties** property by selecting the **Risk** property as its value.
  6. Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.
4. In the same table, create a legend intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend Status .
5. Create and define the Legend Items for the Status Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. Create four Legend Items indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them Closed , Approved , Disapproved and In Progress .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. Specify the Elements by Condition property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Status property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Specify the Adorned Properties property by selecting the Name and Text properties as its values.
  6. Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.

After following the above steps, you have a Requirements Table with the*Status*and*Risk*Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict because they are applied to different columns of the table, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure shows a Requirement Table with the *Risk* and *Status* Legends applied to it. 
The Legend Items of each Legend are applied to different columns of a table.

##### Selecting adorned symbols

You can select the adorned symbols in the open diagram directly from the shortcut menu or the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.

To select adorned symbols in the diagram

Do one of the following:

1. 
  - Right-click the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click Select Adorned Symbols . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_adorned_symbols.png']
  - Select the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_adorned_symbols_button.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_adorned_symbols_from_smart_manipulator.png']

If the diagram is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering by Legend items' space='MD2024xR3'], the **Select Adorned Symbols** menu item is greyed out (inactive).

582727279

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR3']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Item adornment' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="635a7c63-3516-489f-b11f-879957f18c38"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1283264726</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4abf311-fa33-4010-9f7c-fa93c4068156"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1283264728</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb251d7a-6adc-497b-b9a7-7f6f29d7383a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1283264725</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49b6edf3-994e-46c7-9a8d-881e073a7b5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="294feb62-250d-4b3f-bf3e-fda47bc82875"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1283264727</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">When you <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a Legend in a diagram or table, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Item adornment" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[adornment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of its L<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">egend Items is </span>applied<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> to the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements. You can define the elements to which a Legend Item should be applied by specifying one of its properties in the <strong>Apply To</strong> property group. Legend Items can be applied either to </span><strong>Specific Elements</strong> or to <strong>Elements By Condition</strong>. If you apply a Legend to a table, you can also specify the <strong>Adorned Properties</strong> property, which defines what cells of the relevant table rows should be adorned.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e783c317-c206-4e3d-b556-79088519d2c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify either the <strong>Specific Elements </strong>or the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> property of a Legend Item, but not both.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Applying Legend Items to specific elements</h3><p>You can apply a Legend Item to one or multiple specific elements as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a Legend Item to specific elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of a Legend Item you want to apply.</li><li>Select the property specification cell of the <strong>Specific Elements</strong> property, and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_short.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select the elements to which you want to apply the Legend Item.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add them to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> area, and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Close the Specification window.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also apply one or multiple Legend Items to an element from its symbol.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply Legend Items to an element from its symbol</p><hr /><ol><li>In a diagram, right-click the shape of the element to which you want to apply Legend Items, and in the menu, select <strong>Legend Item</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, select the Legend Items you want to apply, and click the <strong>Apply</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="applying_legend_items_from_element_symbol.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example how to apply Legend Items from an element shape.</h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a6d3556-ba97-4f64-ab96-5ddaba39082a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Applying Legend Items to elements by condition</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Applying Legend Items to elements by condition</h3><p>Applying a Legend Item to <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> ensures that it is consistently applied to all the elements that meet the defined condition, even after you change your model.</p><p><strong><br />Example</strong></p><p>Let's say that you want to apply a Legend Item to all requirements that have a high-risk level. The quickest way to do it is to create a Property Test operation. Follow the steps below to analyze this specific use case and define the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> property for the Legend Item:<br /><br /></p><ol><li><p>Open the Specification window of a Legend Item.</p></li><li><p>Select the specification cell of the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> property, and  click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_short.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Element by Condition</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Element Type</strong> box.</p></li><li><p>In the Property Test operation specification area of the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> dialog, select the <strong>risk</strong> property and set its value to <strong>High</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b274339-b119-4332-8cf3-0892a0f56e5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Default options</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, the <strong>Language</strong> is set to <strong>StructuredExpression,</strong> and the Property Test operation is selected in the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, and close the Specification window.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="elements_by_condition_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> dialog with the Property Test operation defined to test if Requirements have a high-risk level.</h6><p><span>After following the above steps, the Legend Item is applied to all Requirements that have the <strong>Risk</strong> property set to <strong>high</strong>. If this use case does not meet your needs, you may define advanced Property Test operation properties. You may also </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new operations" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create an operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> of a different type, or create several operations for a single condition, as long as it returns a Boolean data type value (</span><em>true</em><span> or </span><em>false</em><span>). In addition, you can define a condition using a different </span><strong>Language</strong><span> setting.</span></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Applying Legend Items to specific cells of a table</h3><p>When a Legend is used in a table, you can specify not only the elements to which specific Legend Items should apply (the <strong>Specific Elements</strong> or <strong>Elements By Condition</strong> properties) but also the desired properties of these elements. In other words, you can adorn specific cells of a table instead of adorning entire table rows. To do this, use the <strong>Adorned Properties</strong> property of a Legend Item.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="878f6c1e-955f-44d9-acc8-0245c1eb57c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Adorned Properties</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Adorned Properties</strong> property of a Legend Item is only effective if you use the Legend in a table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br />Example</strong></p><p>Let's say you have a Requirements Table with three columns: <em>Name,</em> <em>Text,</em> and <em>Risk</em>., and you want to apply two legends in that table: <em>Risk</em> and <em>Status</em>. When you create or use a Legend in a table, Legend Item adornments are normally applied to enitre table rows. So in this case, you also have to specify certain element properties (table columns) to which Legend Items of each Legend should be applied. To reproduce this spesific use case, follow these steps:</p><ol><li>Create a Requirement Table with the <em>Name</em>, <em>Text</em> and <em>Risk</em> columns.</li><li>In the table, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a Legend]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend <em>Risk</em>.</li><li>Create and define the Legend Items for the <em>Risk</em> Legend by completing the following steps:<br /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and managing Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create three Legend Items]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them <em>High</em>, <em>Medium</em>, and <em>Low</em>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.</li><li>Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired <strong>Fill Color</strong>.</li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Applying+Legend+Items">Specify the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> property</a> by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the <strong>Risk</strong> property value the Legend Item represents.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify <strong>Adorned Properties</strong> property by selecting the <strong>Risk</strong> property as its value.</p></li><li>Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.</li></ol></li><li>In the same table, create a legend intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend <em>Status</em>.</li><li>Create and define the Legend Items for the <em>Status</em> Legend by completing the following steps:<br /><ol><li>Create four Legend Items indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them <em>Closed</em>, <em>Approved</em>, <em>Disapproved</em> and <em>In Progress</em>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.</li><li>Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired <strong>Fill Color</strong>.</li><li>Specify the <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the <strong>Status</strong> property value the Legend Item represents.</li><li>Specify the <strong>Adorned Properties</strong> property by selecting the <strong>Name</strong> and<strong> Text</strong> properties as its values.</li><li>Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.</li></ol></li></ol><p><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After following the above steps, you have a Requirements Table with the </span><em>Status</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and </span><em>Risk</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict because they are applied to different columns of the table, as shown below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="adorning_specific_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The figure shows a Requirement Table with the <em>Risk</em> and <em>Status</em> Legends applied to it.<br />The Legend Items of each Legend are applied to different columns of a table.</h6><h3>Selecting adorned symbols</h3><p>You can select the adorned symbols in the open diagram directly from the shortcut menu or the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.</p><p><br /></p><p>To select adorned symbols in the diagram</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Right-click the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click <strong>Select Adorned Symbols</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_adorned_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_adorned_symbols_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:height="97" ac:width="258"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_adorned_symbols_from_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43fe7115-03e0-463b-9496-f28c6c8d7f46"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the diagram is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Filtering by Legend items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filtered by the selected Legend item(s)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the <strong>Select Adorned Symbols</strong> menu item is greyed out (inactive).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a610a415-f395-41b7-b4fa-1ff7dca197c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">582727279</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Item adornment" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227149906 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: Applying Legends

- page_id: `227149906`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227149906/Applying+Legends
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-28T11:37:03.803+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Keeping Legend adornments

580023290

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Keeping Legend adornments

580023292

580023289

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Keeping Legend adornments

580023291

You can manage the Legends by choosing to either apply, unapply, suspend, or disable them in the diagrams and/or tables of your project. You can also adorn a Legend in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend itself, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Requirement Table displaying only Legend adornments

##### Applying a Legend in an open diagram or table

To apply a Legend in an open diagram or table

Do one of the following:

- Create a Legend in a diagram or table.
- Drag an existing Legend from the model browser to a diagram.
- Display a Legend via the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='Legends_button.png'] button:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the diagram or table toolbar.
  2. In the drop-down menu, select Apply Legends .
  3. Select the Legend(s) you want to display.
  4. Click the OK button.

Completing one of the actions described above displays the Legend in an open diagram or table, and applies its Legend Item adornments to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.

#### NOTE: Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

You can also temporarily suspend or activate a Legend via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button available next to the Legend name in the **Apply Legends**option. This functionality can be useful for demonstration purposes, working with read-only diagrams/tables, or if you want to preview a diagram/table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes. The changes made via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button are only temporary, i.e., they are not saved upon the saving of a project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Unapplying a Legend from an open diagram or table

To unapply a Legend from an open diagram or table

Do one of the following:

- Delete the Legend shape from a diagram.
- Hover the mouse pointer over a legend in a table, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_edit_button.png'] and select Hide .
- Remove a Legend via the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='Legends_button.png'] button:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the diagram or table toolbar.
  2. In the drop-down menu, select Apply Legends .
  3. Clear the check mark of the Legend(s) you want to remove.
  4. Click the OK button.

The actions described above remove a Legend from an open diagram or table, and Legend Item adornments are no longer applied to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.

#### NOTE: Keeping Legend adornments

Keeping Legend adornments

When you delete a Legend shape in a diagram or select the **Hide** command for a Legend in a table, the question message appears, allowing you to keep Legend Item adornments after the Legend is removed.

##### Displaying an applied Legend in an open diagram or table

You can display an applied Legend that was previously removed from a diagram or table.

To display an applied Legend in an open diagram or table

1. In a table or diagram toolbar, click the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] button.
2. In the drop-down menu, select Display Applied Legends .
3. Select the Legend(s) you want to display.
4. Click the Display button.

##### Disabling/enabling Legends

To disable/enable Legends

1. In the main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General options group, select Legends .
3. To disable/enable selected Legends:
  1. For the **Ignored Legends** option, click the three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The **Select Legend** dialog opens.
  2. Click the **Multiple Selection** button and do one of the following:
    1. To disable Legends: on the left side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to disable, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button and click **OK**.
    2. To enable Legends: on the right side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to enable, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_button_3.png'] button and click OK .
4. To disable/enable all Legends:
  1. Set the Use Legends option to true to enable or false to disable the Legends of the project. Click OK .
5. Click OK .

Once the Legend is disabled, even if it is displayed on the diagram, the Legend cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments. Once all the Legends are disabled, even if they are displayed on the diagram, the Legends cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments.

##### display_adornmentsApplying Legends by Condition

You can apply Legend adornments in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend. To achieve this, define the condition that diagrams should meet for the Legend to be applied (the **Diagrams by Condition** property). Legends with the specified **Diagrams by Condition** property are considered Global Legends, as the Legends' adornments are automatically applied to existing and newly created diagrams that meet the defined condition. Such Legends are marked in the **Apply Legends** and **Display Legends** menus with the *(global)* indication:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

Let's say that you want to apply the adornments of a Legend to all Requirement Diagrams and one specific Requirement Table of your model. To do this, follow these steps:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Select the Diagrams by Condition property specification cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
3. In the Diagrams by Condition dialog, select the Condition radio button.
4. In the Property Test operation specification area, select the Type property and enter the Requirement Diagram as its value. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_diagrams_by_condition.png']
5. Click OK .
6. Select the Specific Diagrams property specification cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
7. In the Select Diagram dialog, select the desired Requirement Table.
8. Click OK .

After completing the above steps, the Legend adornments are applied to all Requirement Diagrams and the selected Requirement Table. By specifying the **Diagrams by Condition** property, you also ensure that the Legend adornments will be automatically applied to newly created Requirement Diagrams as well because they meet the defined condition.

580083820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR3']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024xR3']

  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e018f8db-f711-4a2a-91fa-b3d332138916"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023290</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4aad01dc-3880-4863-94f1-4986ccc87f4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023292</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5ce4407-fc22-4e37-8a5e-4c81f6a1542a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023289</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f51355a-0584-41d0-8896-7ad8ccc5658a"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe843ff0-97f3-4464-85a1-8dc5b69d79cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023291</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can manage the Legends by choosing to either apply, unapply, suspend, or disable them in the diagrams and/or tables of your project. You can also adorn a Legend in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend itself, as shown below.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="displaying_adornment_without_legend.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">A Requirement Table displaying only Legend adornments</span></h6><h3>Applying a Legend in an open diagram or table</h3><p>To apply a Legend in an open diagram or table</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Create a Legend in a diagram or table.</li><li>Drag an existing Legend from the model browser to a diagram.</li><li>Display a Legend via the Legend <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button:<ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram or table toolbar.</li><li>In the drop-down menu, select <strong>Apply Legends</strong>.</li><li>Select the Legend(s) you want to display.</li><li>Click the <strong>OK</strong> button.</li></ol></li></ul><p>Completing one of the actions described above displays the Legend in an open diagram or table, and applies its Legend Item adornments to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dae9ada-0e03-4f1d-a06f-2b9e9bcf3fef"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also temporarily suspend or activate a Legend via the <strong>Suspended/Active</strong> toggle button available next to the Legend name in the <strong>Apply Legends </strong>option. This functionality can be useful for demonstration purposes, working with read-only diagrams/tables, or if you want to preview a diagram/table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes. The changes made via the <strong>Suspended/Active</strong> toggle button are only temporary, i.e., they are not saved upon the saving of a project.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="active_suspended_legends.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Unapplying a Legend from an open diagram or table</h3><p>To unapply a Legend from an open diagram or table</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Delete the Legend shape from a diagram.</li><li>Hover the mouse pointer over a legend in a table, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Hide</strong>.</li><li>Remove a Legend via the Legend <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button:<ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram or table toolbar.</li><li>In the drop-down menu, select <strong>Apply Legends</strong>.</li><li>Clear the check mark of the Legend(s) you want to remove.</li><li>Click the <strong>OK</strong> button.</li></ol></li></ul><p>The actions described above remove a Legend from an open diagram or table, and Legend Item adornments are no longer applied to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="230aa9d1-ca9a-4ca2-9a0d-f965eb74de96"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Keeping Legend adornments</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you delete a Legend shape in a diagram or select the <strong>Hide</strong> command for a Legend in a table, the question message appears, allowing you to keep Legend Item adornments after the Legend is removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Displaying an applied Legend in an open diagram or table</span></h3><p><span>You can display an applied Legend that was previously removed from a diagram or table.</span></p><p><span>To display an applied Legend in an open diagram or table</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In a table or diagram toolbar, click the Legend <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>In the drop-down menu, select <strong>Display Applied Legends</strong>.</li><li>Select the Legend(s) you want to display.</li><li>Click the <strong>Display</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Disabling/enabling Legends</h3><p>To disable/enable Legends</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options group, select <strong>Legends</strong>.</li><li>To disable/enable selected Legends:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">For the <strong>Ignored Legends</strong> option, click the three dots <ac:image ac:alt="edit.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/26676999/edit.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1643385379535&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Select Legend</strong> dialog opens.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button and do one of the following:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To disable Legends: on the left side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to disable, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>.</p></li><li>To enable Legends: on the right side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to enable, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_button_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li>To disable/enable all Legends:<ol><li>Set the <strong>Use Legends</strong> option to <em>true</em> to enable or <em>false </em>to disable the Legends of the project. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>Once the Legend is disabled, even if it is displayed on the diagram, the Legend cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments. Once all the Legends are disabled, even if they are displayed on the diagram, the Legends cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments.</p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc75b0b5-766d-4749-9bbb-925046786a4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">display_adornments</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Applying Legends by Condition</h3><p>You can apply Legend adornments in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend. To achieve this, define the condition that diagrams should meet for the Legend to be applied (the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property). Legends with the specified <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property are considered Global Legends, as the Legends' adornments are automatically applied to existing and newly created diagrams that meet the defined condition. Such Legends are marked in the <strong>Apply Legends</strong> and <strong>Display Legends</strong> menus with the <em>(global)</em> indication:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_legends.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong><br />Example</strong></p><p>Let's say that you want to apply the adornments of a Legend to all Requirement Diagrams and one specific Requirement Table of your model. To do this, follow these steps:</p><ol><li>Open the Specification window of a Legend.</li><li>Select the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property specification cell and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Condition</strong> radio button.</li><li>In the Property Test operation specification area, select the <strong>Type</strong> property and enter the <strong>Requirement Diagram</strong> as its value.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_diagrams_by_condition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Specific Diagrams</strong> property specification cell and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Diagram</strong> dialog, select the desired Requirement Table.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, the Legend adornments are applied to all Requirement Diagrams and the selected Requirement Table. By specifying the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property, you also ensure that the Legend adornments will be automatically applied to newly created Requirement Diagrams as well because they meet the defined condition. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a48faba-cae9-40f9-9ea6-2ea302ab9902"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580083820</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Legends" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and managing Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Items" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150102 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00077: Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams

- page_id: `227150102`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150102/Applying+or+removing+aspect+for+preexisting+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:15:31.118+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581426749

581426751

581426750

You can apply custom or predefined aspects for preexisting diagrams. It is possible to apply multiple aspects for one diagram. The aspects can be applied for all diagrams except [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''] diagrams. The procedure below describes how to apply/remove an aspect for a diagram.

To apply/remove an aspect for a diagram

- From the diagram shortcut menu, point to Aspect, and choose an appropriate aspect from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying an aspect for a diagram. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581426748

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff80cc3a-6b61-4f77-828f-cac932104c23"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426749</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2e2d61e-e6d7-44f9-b310-52ed8d346d92"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426751</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c6f2657-bd26-4da7-b19b-883728954a15"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426750</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply custom or predefined aspects for preexisting diagrams. It is possible to apply multiple aspects for one diagram. The aspects can be applied for all diagrams except <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[State Machine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams. The procedure below describes how to apply/remove an aspect for a diagram.</p><p>To apply/remove an aspect for a diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, point to <strong>Aspect,</strong> and choose an appropriate aspect from the list.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aspect_command_group.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying an aspect for a diagram. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="743da1cc-30ee-4816-b7ce-4dd2eedad7d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426748</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample model:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\SysML</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154322 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00078: Applying Stereotypes

- page_id: `227154322`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154322/Applying+Stereotypes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-23T13:45:32.824+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

561938014

561938017

561938016

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3']defines how an existing metaclass may be extended. The stereotype enables the use of the platform or domain-specific terminology or notation in place of, or in addition to, the ones used for the extended metaclass.

When a stereotype is applied to a model element, the values of the properties may be referred to as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024xR3'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3']true

561938013

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying Profile' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with projects' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The stereotype enables the use of the platform or domain-specific terminology or notation in place of, or in addition to, the ones used for the extended metaclass.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When a stereotype is applied to a model element, the values of the properties may be referred to as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tag" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tagged values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1ad10ad-f170-4d98-bdfe-af2ae624b0c7"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></span></p></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d9931ecc-18a7-4571-9bb8-70fc6d84db1f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">561938013</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying Profile" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tag" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152271 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: Applying validationRule stereotype

- page_id: `227152271`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152271/Applying+validationRule+stereotype
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-04-25T12:40:20.292+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

After the Constraint element is created, you must apply:

- «validationRule» stereotype. It is derived from the «invariant » stereotype. It expands the Constraint properties with the ability to specify the following information: severity level (for sorting/filtering), abbreviation string (a short string, for easy recognition) and error message (complete description of error explanation). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the validation rule properties' space='MD2024xR3']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2822077-680f-4896-8bb2-d2630465439e"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After the Constraint element is created, you must apply:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">«validationRule» stereotype. <span class="auto-cursor-target">It is derived from the «invariant » stereotype.</span> It expands the Constraint properties with the ability to specify the following information: severity level (for sorting/filtering), abbreviation string (a short string, for easy recognition) and error message (complete description of error explanation). <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying the validation rule properties" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to specify the validation rules properties >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="776a9247-2826-463c-bff6-db72c9e8b9ba"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227149084 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: Architectural views

- page_id: `227149084`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227149084/Architectural+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-20T14:38:54.591+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML defines 13 diagrams that describe 4+1 architectural views:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Several kinds of diagrams provide a visual notation for the concepts in each view.

##### Use Case view

The use case view represents the functionality and behavior of a system or subsystem as it is perceived by external users. This view is targeted mainly at customers, designers, developers, and testers.

The use case view usually is presented as a number of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR3'] in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR3']. Occasionally it is used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR3'].

The use case view is central because the contents drive the development of the other views. It is also used for project planning. Every single use case unit is deemed as a manageable unit during the project execution.

##### Structural view

The structural view represents structural elements for implementing a solution for defined requirements. It identifies all of the business entities and how these entities are related to each other. Usually entities are represented as classifiers and their instances in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] in multiple abstraction levels. System decomposition to different layers can be displayed using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='MD2024xR3']. A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] can be used to represent the classifier inner structure. The system structural view artifacts are created by software architects and represent the system implementation design solutions.

##### Behavioral view

The dynamic behavior of the system is displayed on the Interaction ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='MD2024xR3']), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space='MD2024xR3'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] diagrams. It focuses mainly on the interactions that occur between objects inside a system, activities and work performed by the various parts of a system, and state changes within a particular object or collaboration. Rather than defining the participants of the system, it defines how particular use cases are executed, which provides value for the external user. The dynamic view is concerned about what is happening inside the system and how those actions impact other participants.

##### Implementation view

The implementation view describes the implementation artifacts of logical subsystems defined in the structural view. It can include the intermediate artifacts used in a system construction (code files, libraries, data files, etc.) This view defines dependencies between the implementation components and their connections by the required and provided interfaces. Components and their relationships are displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MD2024xR3']. Inner parts of the component can be represented with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024xR3']. The implementation view helps analyze system parts and their dependencies in a higher component level.

##### Environment view

The environment view represents the physical arrangement of a system, such as computers and devices (nodes) and how they are connected to each other. In contrast to the component view, the deployment view is concerned with the physical structure of the system and the location of the software modules (components) manifested by artifacts within the system.

The environment view is displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MD2024xR3'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>UML defines 13 diagrams that describe 4+1 <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75fcd8db-4b0f-4bc2-97dc-c72c7c2eb7fc">architectural</ac:inline-comment-marker> views:</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Architectural views" ac:alt="Architectural views"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ArchitecturalViews.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Architectural views" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Several kinds of diagrams provide a visual notation for the concepts in each view.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Use Case view</h3><p>The use case view represents the functionality and behavior of a system or subsystem as it is perceived by external users. This view is targeted mainly at customers, designers, developers, and testers. </p><p>The use case view usually is presented as a number of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[actors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Case diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Occasionally it is used in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p>The use case view is central because the contents drive the development of the other views. It is also used for project planning. Every single use case unit is deemed as a manageable unit during the project execution. </p><h3>Structural view</h3><p>The structural view represents structural elements for implementing a solution for defined requirements. It identifies all of the business entities and how these entities are related to each other. Usually entities are represented as classifiers and their instances in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Object diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Object diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in multiple abstraction levels. System decomposition to different layers can be displayed using <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can be used to represent the classifier inner structure. The system structural view artifacts are created by software architects and represent the system implementation design solutions.</p><h3>Behavioral view</h3><p>The dynamic behavior of the system is displayed on the Interaction (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Communication diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Communication]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[State]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction Overview]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Time diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Time]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams. It focuses mainly on the interactions that occur between objects inside a system, activities and work performed by the various parts of a system, and state changes within a particular object or collaboration. Rather than defining the participants of the system, it defines how particular use cases are executed, which provides value for the external user. The dynamic view is concerned about what is happening inside the system and how those actions impact other participants.</p><h3>Implementation view</h3><p>The implementation view describes the implementation artifacts of logical subsystems defined in the structural view. It can include the intermediate artifacts used in a system construction (code files, libraries, data files, etc.) This view defines dependencies between the implementation components and their connections by the required and provided interfaces. Components and their relationships are displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Component diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Inner parts of the component can be represented with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The implementation view helps analyze system parts and their dependencies in a higher component level.</p><h3>Environment view</h3><p>The environment view represents the physical arrangement of a system, such as computers and devices (nodes) and how they are connected to each other. In contrast to the component view, the deployment view is concerned with the physical structure of the system and the location of the software modules (components) manifested by artifacts within the system. </p><p>The environment view is displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227153547 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00081: Architecture templates

- page_id: `227153547`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227153547/Architecture+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-17T12:20:55.594+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are five architecture report templates that come with the report engine:

#### Behavioral Report

A Behavioral report is a standard report for the Behavioral view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Sequence, Communication, State Machine, and Activity diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of the specification of messages in the Sequence diagram, the specification of lifelines in the Communication diagram, the specification of states in the State Machine diagram, and the actions in the Activity diagram. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

#### Environment Report

An Environment report is a standard report for the Environment view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Deployment diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of nodes, interfaces, components, and artifacts which are ordered by the element’s name.

#### Implementation Report

An Implementation report is a standard report for the Implementation view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Component diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of a list of interfaces, components, and artifacts. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

#### Structural Report

A Structural report is a standard report for the Structural view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of a Class diagram. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of interfaces, classes, and enumerations which are ordered by the element’s name.

#### Use Case Report

A Use Case report is a standard report for the Use Case view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Use Case diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of actors and use case. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are five architecture report templates that come with the report engine:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9e3af78-c96a-412c-9208-449c0d7e292d" /></p><h2>Behavioral Report</h2><p>A Behavioral report is a standard report for the Behavioral view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Sequence, Communication, State Machine, and Activity diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of the specification of messages in the Sequence diagram, the specification of lifelines in the Communication diagram, the specification of states in the State Machine diagram, and the actions in the Activity diagram. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p><h2>Environment Report</h2><p>An Environment report is a standard report for the Environment view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Deployment diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of nodes, interfaces, components, and artifacts which are ordered by the element’s name.</p><h2>Implementation Report</h2><p>An Implementation report is a standard report for the Implementation view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Component diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of a list of interfaces, components, and artifacts. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p><h2>Structural Report</h2><p>A Structural report is a standard report for the Structural view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of a Class diagram. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of interfaces, classes, and enumerations which are ordered by the element’s name.</p><h2>Use Case Report</h2><p>A Use Case report is a standard report for the Use Case view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Use Case diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of actors and use case. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p>
````

## PAGE 00082: Array tool

- page_id: `227153494`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227153494/Array+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-08T04:19:15.972+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050323630

2050323641

2050323628

**On this page**

43

2050323631

Use $array to create an Array or a HashSet instance.

###### **$array.createArray()**

Create an empty ArrayList.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An instance of ArrayList. |

****

###### **$array.createArray(collection)**

Construct an ArrayList containing elements of the specified collection, in the order they are returned by the collection's iterator. Return a zero size ArrayList if the given collection is null.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection of instances whose elements are to be placed into a list. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An ArrayList containing elements of the specified collection. |

****

###### **$array.subList(list, size)**

Create an ArrayList of a portion of the list in the given size.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | java.util.List | An original list. |
|  | size | int | A view size of a given list. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An ArrayList of a specified view size within a given list. |

****

###### **$array.addCollection(parent, child)**

Add a child list into a parent collection. It helps the template handle the case where the child is null.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | parent | java.util.Collection | A parent collection. |
|  | child | java.util.Collection | A child collection. |
| Return | - | void |  |

****

###### **$array.createHashSet()**

Create a HashSet instance.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.Set | An instance of HashSet. |

## PAGE 00083: Artifact

- page_id: `227150193`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150193/Artifact
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-13T14:22:44.229+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907414243

907414245

907414244

An Artifactrepresents a physical piece of information that is used or produced by a software development process. Examples of artifacts include models, source files, scripts, and binary executable files. An artifact may constitute the implementation of a deployable component.

To describe how an artifact is created, the model draws a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manifestations' space='MD2024xR3'] relationship, between the artifact and the elements used in its construction. All artifact manifestations are listed in the Artifact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'], Manifestations pane. In this pane you may add new manifestations.

To be used, an artifact must be deployed, meaning, it must be placed in a location where it may be stored and used. The target of the deployment may be a node, such as an execution environment or a device, a property, or an instance specification. The relationship between the location and the artifact is defined by the deployment relationship.

907414242

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

Examples of artifacts include models, source files, scripts, and binary executable files. An artifact may constitute the implementation of a deployable component. </span></p><p>To describe how an artifact is created, the model draws a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manifestations" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link> relationship, between the artifact and the elements used in its construction.  All artifact manifestations are listed in the <span class="hcp1">Artifact <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></span>, <span class="hcp1">Manifestations</span> pane. In this pane you may add new manifestations.</p><p>To be used, an artifact must be deployed, meaning, it must be placed in a location where it may be stored and used. The target of the deployment may be a node, such as an execution environment or a device, a property, or an instance specification. The relationship between the location and the artifact is defined by the <a>deployment</a> relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44de9bea-20bd-4578-9956-35688578bd88"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907414242</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing property values" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Editing Property Values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manifestations" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li class="hcp3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li class="hcp3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
