<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154915 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro

- page_id: `227154915`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154915/Assigning+a+keyboard+shortcut+to+a+Macro
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-12-03T09:02:47.723+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation > Macro keyboard shortcuts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new keyboard shortcut to a macro

1. Open either the (i) Environment Options or (ii) Macro Information dialog.
2. Type a keyboard shortcut in the [ATTACHMENT filename='press-new-shortcut-key.png'] box.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to assign the keyboard shortcut to a macro.
4. Click OK .

If a keyboard shortcut key entered in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box has already been assigned to another macro, the following message will appear under the **Press new shortcut key**box: **Currently assigned to <the command name>**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To add a new keyboard shortcut to a macro</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open either the (i) <strong>Environment Options</strong> or (ii) <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog.</li><li>Type a keyboard shortcut in the<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> box.</li><li>Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> to assign the keyboard shortcut to a macro.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8dec5360-bd93-40ea-87e2-08069e3accb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a keyboard shortcut key entered in the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> box has already been assigned to another macro, the following message will appear under the <strong>Press new shortcut key </strong>box: <strong>Currently assigned to &lt;the command name&gt;</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150216 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: Assigning Behavior diagrams automatically

- page_id: `227150216`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150216/Assigning+Behavior+diagrams+automatically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-10T00:51:32.514+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When assigning a behavior of an Activity, Interaction, Protocol State machine, or State Machine type for a state or transition, the corresponding behavior diagram is created automatically:

- an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] for an Activity behavior
- a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] for Interaction behavior
- a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] for Protocol State Machine behavior
- a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3'] for State Machine behavior

Double-click the assigned behavior on a state shape to open the diagram. You can also open the behavior diagram from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Browser' space='MD2024xR3'].

To disable an automatic diagram creating

1. On the **Options** menu, click **Environment**. The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In General options, Editing category, set the Create behavior diagram when the behavior type is selected for transition effect or state activities value to false (the default value is true ).

You can specify behavior properties in the Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: More information

More information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behaviors' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR3']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150665 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00086: Assigning behavior to state

- page_id: `227150665`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150665/Assigning+behavior+to+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T22:03:20.954+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['state-behavior']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can define a behavior to be executed correspondingly to the listed events while being in the State whenever the State is entered and exited. 
 
 To assign a behavior to a State by dragging

1. In the Model Browser, select a behavior type element (Activity, Function Behavior, Interaction, Opaque Behavior, State Machine, or Protocol State Machine) or a Signal element.
2. Drag it to the selected State on the diagram. The list of activities appears. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav9d9404b7bf87a7312cc856184577c1b0.png']

- Click a required activity. 
The behavior is assigned to the selected State's activity. - If you drag a behavior type element (Activity, Function Behavior, Interaction, Opaque Behavior, State Machine, or Protocol State Machine), the Activity diagram under the Activity element with a Call Behavior Action element are created automatically.- If you drag a Signal element, the Activity diagram under the Activity element with a Send Signal Action element are created automatically.

To assign a behavior to a State by using the transition's Specification window

1. Open the State's Specification window.
2. In the **Entry,****Do****Activity**, or **Exit** category, click the **Behavior****Type** property value cell. The list of available behavior types opens. Select the behavior type from the list. If you do not see the **Behavior Type** property, click the **+** button near the **Entry**, **Do****Activity**, or **Exit** category to expand its content.
3. According to the selected behavior type, the **Behavior Element** value is defined automatically. Additional properties **Name** and **Owned Diagram** appears. Specify these properties if it is needed.
4. Click the **Close** button after you have defined desired properties. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav4a14ed756a0d12b3f922001d3f22deb4.png']

If a behavior of an Activity, Interaction, Protocol State Machine, or State Machine type is assigned to an Entry, Exit, or Do activity, a diagram for that behavior is created automatically.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

#### PANEL: Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150717 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00087: Assigning Behavior Type

- page_id: `227150717`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150717/Assigning+Behavior+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T23:02:52.586+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Transition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A behavior type is a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024xR3']that is performed when the transition fires.

To assign a behavior type, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a behavior type element and drag it to the selected transition on the diagram.
- In the transition’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] > **Effect** category, click the **Behavior Type** property value cell and select the behavior from the list. According to the selected behavior, additional corresponding properties will appear in the **Effect** category. Specify desired properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- To quickly trace the effect of the transition, from the transition shortcut menu, select Go To and then the effect element.
- If a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024xR3'] of an activity, interaction, protocol state machine, or state machine type is assigned to a transition, a diagram for that behavior is created automatically.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR3']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150213 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00088: Assigning Behaviors

- page_id: `227150213`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150213/Assigning+Behaviors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T00:56:22.532+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See references on how to assign the behavior for the following elements:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024xR3']

See the following procedures, on how to assign the behavior for the rest of the elements,

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR3'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MD2024xR3'], and
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR3'].

To create and assign a behavior

There are two ways to create and assign behaviors, so do one of the following:

- Create a behavior in the element's Specification Window.
  1. Open the element's Specification Window and click the **Behaviors** property group. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  2. Click the **Create** button and then from the menu, select the behavior type. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  3. In the behavior's Specification Window, specify the behavior and click **Close**.
- Right-click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR3'] , on its shortcut menu, click Create Diagram and then click to create Activity , Sequence , State Machine or other behavior diagram.

#### TIP: To create an operation for the behavior

To create an operation for the behavior

1. In the element's Specification Window, click Behaviors property group.
2. Click the behavior, then click the Create Operation button.

If the Specification property is defined already, then the**Create Operation**button is unavailable.

#### PANEL: Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150714 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00089: Assigning Event Type

- page_id: `227150714`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150714/Assigning+Event+Type
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:39.422+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Transition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1769354984

1769354987

1769354986

You need to assign an event to the transition in order to specify an occurrence of an event which can trigger a state transition.

You can assign an event type to a transition in the following ways:

- Using the transition’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] .
- By typing an appropriate command straight on the selected transition path.

To assign an event type in the Transition Specification window

1. Open the transition’s Specification window.
2. In the **Trigger** category, select the **Event Type** property value cell. The list of available event types opens.
3. Select the event type from the list. According to the selected event type, Event Element, and Trigger property values are defined and additional corresponding properties appears in the Trigger category (see the following figure).
4. Click the **Close** button after you have defined desired properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign an event type straight on a transition path

1. Select a transition path on the diagram pane.
2. Type the command of the appropriate event type. The event type is assigned to the transition. In the following figure, see an example of the signal event type assignment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the list with the event type and its command syntax in the following table.

| Name | Command syntax |
| --- | --- |
| Any Receive Event | all |
| Call Event | <operation name ()>NOTE: Before assigning operation straight on the transition path the Call Event together with this operation should be already created in the same State Machine as the transition. |
| Change Event | when (<expression>) |
| Signal Event | <signal name> |
| Time Event | after (<time>) - for an event occurring at a relative timeat (<time>) - for an event occurring at an absolute time |

The event type assigning by typing the command straight on the diagram pane is also available for a transition to self in the State Machine diagram, as well as for a protocol transition and protocol transition to self in the Protocol State Machine diagram.

1769354980

**Related pages**

- Assigning Behavior Type
- Model Elements
- Transition
- State Machine diagram

The event type is assigned to the transition. In the following figure, see an example of the signal event type assignment.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of signal event, that was specified typing text straight on transition" ac:alt="Example of signal event, that was specified typing text straight on transition"><ri:attachment ri:filename="transition2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning Event Type" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>See the list with the event type and its command syntax in the following table.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Command syntax</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Any Receive Event</strong></td><td><em>all</em></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Call Event</strong></td><td><p>&lt;<em>operation name</em> ()&gt;<br /><strong>NOTE</strong>: Before assigning operation straight on the transition path the Call Event together with this operation should be already created in the same State Machine as the transition.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Change Event</strong></td><td><em>when</em> (&lt;<em>expression</em>&gt;)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Signal Event</strong></td><td colspan="1">&lt;<em>signal name</em>&gt;</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Time Event</strong></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li><em>after</em> (&lt;<em>time</em>&gt;) - <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150462 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00090: Assigning Operations to Messages

- page_id: `227150462`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150462/Assigning+Operations+to+Messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:37:06.411+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['signature', 'create-operation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372250885

372250887

372250886

INLINE

You can assign [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024xR3']only to Call (synchronous and asynchronous) Messages. Only one Operation can be assigned to a Message.

To assign an Operation to a Message

- Assign an Operation to a Message in the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3']: 
 
1. Draw a call Message between two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MD2024xR3']or select an existing Message on a diagram pane. 
2. Open the Message Specification window. 
3. Click the **Signature (operation)** property value cell and then select an Operation in the drop-down list. If the **Signature (operation)**drop-down list is empty, check if the Lifeline the current Message is drawn to has a type assigned, and if this type has a specified Operation.In the **Signature (operation)**drop-down list, both Operations and signals of your project are listed. Make sure you have selected an Operation. If you assign a signal to a call Message, the Message will automatically be converted to a send Message.

- Type an Operation name right on the Message on the diagram pane.
- On the diagram, right-click the Message and on the shortcut menu, click Operation . Then click to select the Operation that you want to apply on the element, or click the Create Operation button.

If an Operation you are assigning to a Message has default parameter values, these default parameter values are assigned to the Message arguments. However, if the types of the Operation parameter and the Message argument do not match, the default parameter value is assigned to the Message argument.

Names of operation parameters are displayed in message signature by default. However, you can change this behavior by opening the **Symbol Properties** dialog (right-click a Message and select **Symbol Properties**) and setting the **Show Attribute/Parameter Names** property to **true** to display them or **false** to hide them.

To create a new Operation for a Message

You can create a new Operation only if the Lifeline the Message is drawn to has a type assigned.

1. Draw a Call Message between two Lifelines or select an existing Message on a diagram pane.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Click the Create New Operation According to This Message button (a small green circle at the end of the Message name) and define the Operation property values in the Operation Specification window.
  - On the Message shortcut menu, click Operation and then click the Create Operation button. Select the owner and click OK . In the Operation Specification window, define the Operation and click Close .
  - Type an Operation name right on a Message on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

372250883

Select the owner and click <strong>OK</strong>. In the Operation Specification window, define the Operation and click <strong>Close</strong>.</li><li>Type an Operation name right on a Message on the diagram.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Specification of Operation dialog" ac:alt="Specification of Operation dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operation_request.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning Operations to Messages" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b37923a-058b-431a-824d-de037771b5f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372250883</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00091: Assigning shortcut keys

- page_id: `227148526`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148526/Assigning+shortcut+keys

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686262

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686264

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686263

#### EXCERPT: Restoring shortcut keys

INLINE

Most commonly used commands in a modeling tool have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Most common shortcut keys' space='MD2024xR3'], allowing you to access the commands quicker. However, you can assign custom shortcut keys to modeling tool commands and change or remove already existing shortcut keys.

To assign a new shortcut key to a command

1. From the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. From the option group list on the left side of the dialog, select Keyboard .
3. Search for the command you need to assign a shortcut key to. You can also search by the shortcut keys.
4. In the Press new shortcut key box, press the shortcut you want to assign. Do not type the shortcut, but press the keys as you would when executing the command.
5. Click the Assign button. The assigned shortcut appears in the Current keys box.
6. Click OK .

The shortcut key is then assigned to the selected command and you can start using it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Assigning a shortcut key.

#### TIP: Usage information

Usage information

- You can assign multiple shortcut keys to one command.
- If you enter a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, a notification appears under the Press new shortcut key box.
- If you assign a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, the shortcut key is automatically unassigned from that command and assigned to the new one.

##### Removing shortcut keys

You can remove both default and custom shortcut keys.

To remove a shortcut key

1. From the main menu of the modeling tool, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. From the option group list on the left side of the dialog, select Keyboard .
3. Search for the command you need to assign a shortcut key to. You can also search by the shortcut keys.
4. In the Current keys box, select the shortcut key you want to remove.
5. Click the Remove button. To remove all shortcut keys assigned to the selected command, click the Remove All button.
6. Click OK .

#### NOTE: Restoring shortcut keys

Restoring shortcut keys

To restore all shortcut keys to their default values, open the **Environment Options** dialog, select **Keyboard**, and click **Reset to Defaults**.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150470 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: Assigning Signals to Messages

- page_id: `227150470`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150470/Assigning+Signals+to+Messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:39:45.419+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['signature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372369698

372369700

372369699

INLINE

You can assign a Signal only to Send or Call (synchronous or asynchronous) Messages. Only one Signal can be assigned to a Message.

If a Signal is assigned to a Call Message, the Message is automatically converted to a Send Message.

To assign a Signal to a Message by dragging

1. Select a Signal in the Containment tree.
2. Drag the selected Signal to a desired Message.

To assign a Signal to a Message by typing Message name

- Click the Message to type the Message name on the diagram. A Signal is automatically created with the same name as Message name.

To assign a Signal to a Message from the Message Specification window

1. Draw a Send Message between two Lifelines or select an existing one on a diagram pane.
2. Open the Message Specification window.
3. Click the Signature (signal) property value cell and then select a Signal in the drop-down list.

Names of signal attributes are displayed in the message signature by default. However, you can change this behavior by opening the **Symbol Properties** dialog (right-click a Message and select **Symbol Properties**) and setting the **Show Attribute/Parameter Names** property to **true** to display them or **false** to hide them.

372369697

## PAGE 00093: Assigning value specification as property value

- page_id: `227150906`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150906/Assigning+value+specification+as+property+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-01-04T06:25:20.509+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Cases of advanced editing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some properties whose values can be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MD2024xR3'].

Examples of properties whose values are value specifications: *Default Value*, *Value*.

To assign a value specification

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Show Shortcut Menu button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. From the shortcut menu, select **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MD2024xR3']**.
4. Select a value specification from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change an assigned value specification

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Show Shortcut Menu button (see the highlighted button in the preceding figure).
3. From the shortcut menu, select **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MD2024xR3']**> **Delete**<value specification>.
4. Assign a new value specification.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150196 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: Association

- page_id: `227150196`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150196/Association
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:19:10.011+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

973915845

INLINE

100.00002%

973915847

INLINE

973915846

INLINE

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024xR3'], an Association represents the semantic relationship between two or more classifiers which specifies connections between their instances. An Association relationship is the most general of all relationships and the most semantically weak.

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR3'], an Association represents the participation of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR3']in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR3'], for example, when instances of an Actor and instances of a Use Case communicate with each other. This is the only relationship between Actors and Use Cases. Sometimes an association relationship is called communication Association.

An Association is drawn as a solid path connecting two classifier symbols.

The communication path is a subclass of an Association. It specifies the relationship between nodes by defining the number of nodes that may be connected (multiplicity), and the nature of the connection, via the name of the path or a stereotype.

907535765

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: N-ary Association button

491530473

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: N-ary Association button

491530475

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: N-ary Association button

491530474

An Association Class is a model element with both [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR3']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR3'] properties. You can look at an Association Class as an Association that also has Class properties, or as a Class that also has Association properties. Additionally, it connects a set of classifiers and defines a set of features belonging only to the relationship and not to any of the classifiers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an association class.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR3'] in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'].

To draw an association class

1. Draw two Classes.
2. Click the **Association Class** button on the diagram palette. Association Class buttonIf you do not see the **Association Class** button in the diagram palette, click the black arrow next to the **Association** button to see the list of associations. Select **Association Class** from the list.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. On the diagram pane, click the first Class shape (path source).
4. Drag the path to the second Class (path destination) and drop it there.

If you need to model a relationship among several classes, you should use the N-ary Association. The N-ary association is drawn as a big diamond with all associations attached to its points. Every involved Class can have a role name and multiplicity.

To draw an N-ary association class

1. Draw several Classes on the diagram pane.
2. Draw the N-ary Association connector shape. N-ary Association buttonIf you do not see the **N-ary Association** button in the diagram palette, click the black arrow next to the **Association** button to see the list of Associations. Select **N-ary Association** from that list. See the figure above.
3. Connect all classes to the N-ary Association using an Association relationship.

491530472

## PAGE 00096: Association End

- page_id: `227150200`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150200/Association+End
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:39:07.309+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Association
- labels: ['association-end-type', 'qualifier', 'aggregation', 'association-navigability', 'dot-notation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907695017

907695020

907719011

**On this page**

**3**

907695019

Association Ends are represented by properties each of which is connected to the type of the End. When a property is an Association End, the value or values are related to the instance or instances at the other End(s) of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR3'].

An Association End is the connection between lines depicting an Association and the shape.

The following properties of the association end can be specified: name, association end type, visibility, multiplicity, qualifier, aggregation kind.

##### Association end properties

###### Name

The Association End has other name - role. A role indicates a role played by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR3'] in terms of an Association. The role name is placed at the Association End near the Class playing that role. The role name at the implementation level maps to the reference name of the opposite Class. Roles can have visibility (public, package, protected, and private).

###### Association End type

Changing the Association End type, changes the target of the Association or in other words the classifier to which the Association is connected.

###### Qualifier

A qualifier is an attribute or a list of attributes whose values serve to partition the set of instances associated with an instance across an Association. Qualifiers are attributes of an Association. It is represented as a small rectangle attached to the End of an Association path between the final path segment and the symbol of the classifier that it connects to. The qualifier rectangle is part of the Association path, not part of the classifier. The qualifier rectangle drags with the path segments. The qualifier is attached to the source end of the Association.

##### Editing the Association End properties

To change the Association End type, do one of the following

- On the diagram, select the Association End and move it to the other target. The Association is connected to the other target and the type of the Association End (that is, property) is changed.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR3'] , select the property which represents the Association End and drag it onto the available classifier (the Association target). If the Association is represented on the diagram pane, the Association is redrawn (connected) to the changed target automatically.
- In the property (that is, the Association End) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'] , change the Type property value.

To define the Association End visibility

1. Open the Association End Specification window.
2. From the Visibility list, select one of the visibility type.

The Association End multiplicity describes how many entities are participating at each Association End:

- 0 – zero and only zero.
- 1 – one and only one.
- 0..1 – zero or one.
- 0..* – from zero to any positive integer.
- 1..* – from one to any positive integer.
- * – any positive integer.

To place multiplicity values in Association path ends

- Open the shortcut menu of a selected Association End and click a desired multiplicity.
- Open Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of the desired Association Ends (Role of <class name> ), and then click the desired multiplicity value.
- Open the Association Specification window and, from the Multiplicity list, select or type the multiplicity value for the desired Association End.
- Perform the following steps:

1. 
  1. Open the Association End Specification window.
  2. In the Multiplicity property value cell, type or select from the list a multiplicity value.

- Edit directly on the diagram pane:

1. 
  1. Select multiplicity area and press F2 to switch it to edit mode.
  2. Press Ctrl + Spacebar or Ctrl + Backspace to see available suggestions.

Choose one of the suggestions or type the multiplicity value.

To define an Association End name

- Open the shortcut menu of a selected Association End and click Edit Name . The Association End is marked for editing. Type or edit the name directly on the diagram pane.
- Open the Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of a desired Association End ( Role of *<class name>* ), and then click Edit Name . The Association End is marked for editing. Type or edit the name directly on the diagram pane.
- Perform the following steps:

1. 
  1. Open the selected Association End Specification window.
  2. Type the Association End name in the Name property value cell.

##### Defining Association End qualifiers

A qualifier is an attribute or a list of attributes whose values serve to partition the set of instances associated with an instance across an Association. Qualifiers are attributes of an Association. It is represented as a small rectangle attached to the end of an Association path between the final path segment and the symbol of the classifier that it connects to. The qualifier rectangle is part of the Association path, not part of the classifier. The qualifier rectangle drags with the path segments. The qualifier is attached to the source end of the Association.

To add, edit, or remove a qualifier to/from an Association End

1. Open the Association End Specification window.
2. In the property group list, click the Qualifiers group and do one of the following:
  - To add a new qualifier, click the Create button. In the opened qualifier Specification window, define qualifier properties and click Back to return to the Association End Specification window or click Close to exit the Specification window.
  - To edit a qualifier, select a qualifier and edit its properties in the Association End Specification window or click the Open Specification button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the end of a qualifier row to edit qualifier properties in the qualifier Specification window. Existing qualifiers are also displayed in the property group list under the **Qualifiers** group. Click the selected qualifier to open its Specification window, and define qualifier properties there.
  - To remove a qualifier, select a qualifier and click Delete .
3. After you have finished working with qualifiers, click Close to exit the Specification window.

##### Defining Association End aggregation

The shared or composite aggregation kind can be assigned to the Association End. A composite aggregation is represented as a filled diamond. A shared aggregation is represented as a hollow diamond.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign an aggregation kind to the Association End

- Open Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of the desired Association Ends ( Role of *<class name>* ), and then select one of the following:
  - None . No aggregation is assigned to the selected Association End.
  - Shared .
  - Composite .
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR3'] , click the Composition or Aggregation button and draw an appropriate path on the diagram.
- Right click the Association path end and select Shared or Composite command from the shortcut menu.
- Perform the following steps:

1. 
  1. Open the selected Association or Association End Specification window.
  2. For the selected Association End, click **Aggregation** property value cell to select an aggregation kind (**None**, **Shared**, or **Composite**). By default, the **Aggregation** property is in the **All** properties display mode.

##### Showing Association End properties

If two classes are linked with an Association path, both Classes have an attribute of an opposite Class type. This property can be displayed on the Class shape as well as an Association link.

To show Association Ends as attributes on linked Class shapes

1. From the Class shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties or press Alt+Enter. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR3']**Symbol Properties** dialog opens.
2. In the Attribute category, click the Show Association End as Attributes property value cell.
3. Click one of the following display modes:

- 
  - All . Properties and Association paths will be displayed on the Class shape.
  - Without Association Symbol . If an Association symbol is deleted, the property will be displayed on the Class shape.
  - Do Not Show . Neither property, nor Association path will be displayed on the Class shape.

An Association End is defined as a property. It has attribute properties defined in the Specification window.

To show Association Ends as Ports on the shape

1. Select the shape on which you want to display Association Ends as Ports and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR3']**Symbol Properties** dialog :
  - Right-click a shape to open the shortcut menu, and click Symbol Properties .
  - Press Alt+Enter.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, at the right-top corner, click to expand the menu and select the All mode.
3. Under the **Ports** category, click the **Show Association Ends as Ports** property cell and select *All*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Converting Association End properties

To convert an Association role to an attribute

1. On a diagram pane, select an Association with a role.
2. From its shortcut menu, select the Refactor > Convert To > Attribute(s) .

The Association is converted to the attribute.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can specify Association End properties in the Association End Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

To convert Association Ends to Ports

1. Right- click an Association End(s).
2. On the shortcut menu, click Refactor > Convert To > Port .

##### Association navigability

The Association navigability indicates whether it is possible to traverse an Association within an expression of a classifier to obtain the object or a set of objects associated with instances. The navigability is shown as an arrow that can be attached to the end of the path to indicate that the navigation is supported toward the classifier attached to the arrow.

By default, an Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is not visible.

A role indicates the role played by the Class in terms of an Association. The role name is placed at the Association End, near the Class playing that role. The role name at the implementation level maps to the reference name of the opposite Class. Roles can have visibility (public, package, protected, and private).

To change the Association navigability, do one of the following

- Open the Association End Specification window, turn on the Expert property display mode, and do one of the following:
  - Set the Navigable property value to true to mark the Association End as navigable.
  - Set the Navigable property value to false to mark the Association End as not navigable.
- From the Association shortcut menu, select Role of *<class name>* and then do one of the following:
  - Select Navigable to mark the Association End as navigable.
  - Click to clear Navigable to mark the Association End as not navigable.
- Open the Association End shortcut menu and do one of the following:
  - Select Navigable to mark the Association End as navigable.
  - Click to clear Navigable to mark the Association End as not navigable.

To display the Association navigability

- From both Association Ends shortcut menu, select Show Navigability .

In the following figure, the Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is visible.

##### Advancing actions: navigable owned Association Ends

The navigability describes the need for an object to access another object by navigating across the link. The Association End can be owned by a classifier or an Association. The Association End owned by a classifier can be decorated with the dot. The absence of the dot signifies the ownership by the Association.

The dot notation is not enabled by default. Please pay attention to this before making decisions about the Association End ownership just from the model representation on a diagram.

To enable a dot notation

1. On the Options menu, click Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR3']**Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the General options list, select General and set the Enable Dot Notation for Associations value to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='dot_notation_on_association_end.png']

According to the UML 2.4.1 specification, the Association Ends owned by Classes and Associations is navigable. This improved functionality allows a proper management of the *navigableOwnedEnd* property for Associations:

- The ownership of an Association End can be changed manually.
- The navigability of Association Ends owned by the Associations can be changed keeping the ownership.

To change the ownership of an Association End, do one of the following

- On the Association End shortcut menu, click Owned By and then select the desired owner.
- In the Association End Specification window, click the Owned By property value cell and then select the desired owner from the list.
- From the Association shortcut menu, select the role of the desired Association End, click Owned By and then select the desired owner.
- In the Association Specification window, click the Owned By property value cell of the role with the desired Association End and then select the desired owner from the list.

907695015

The qualifier rectangle is part of the Association path, not part of the classifier. The qualifier rectangle drags with the path segments. The qualifier is attached to the source end of the Association.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p>To add, edit, or remove a qualifier to/from an Association End</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Association End Specification window.</li><li>In the property group list, click the <strong>Qualifiers</strong> group and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>To add a new qualifier, click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened qualifier Specification window, define qualifier properties and click <strong>Back</strong> to return to the Association End Specification window or click <strong>Close</strong> to exit the Specification window.</li><li><p>To edit a qualifier, select a qualifier and edit its properties in the Association End Specification window or click the Open Specification button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the end of a qualifier row to edit qualifier properties in the qualifier Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e93528d3-23f4-43e4-bf8a-c2549ee31bc0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Existing qualifiers are also displayed in the property group list under the <strong>Qualifiers</strong> group. Click the selected qualifier to open its Specification window, and define qualifier properties there.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>To remove a qualifier, select a qualifier and click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>After you have finished working with qualifiers, click <strong>Close</strong> to exit the Specification window.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Defining Association End aggregation</span></h3><p>The shared or composite aggregation kind can be assigned to the Association End. A composite aggregation is represented as a filled diamond. A shared aggregation is represented as a hollow diamond.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Representation of composite and shared aggregations" ac:alt="Representation of composite and shared aggregations"><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite_and_shared_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Association End" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To assign an aggregation kind to the Association End</p><hr /><ul><li>Open Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of the desired Association Ends (<strong>Role of <em>&lt;class name&gt;</em> </strong>), and then select one of the following:<ul><li><strong>None</strong>. No aggregation is assigned to the selected Association End.</li><li><strong>Shared</strong>.</li><li><strong>Composite</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click the <strong>Composition</strong> or <strong>Aggregation</strong> button and draw an appropriate path on the diagram.</li><li>Right click the Association path end and select <strong>Shared</strong> or <strong>Composite</strong> command from the shortcut menu.</li><li>Perform the following steps:</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Open the selected Association or Association End Specification window.</li><li><p>For the selected Association End, click <strong>Aggregation</strong> property value cell to select an aggregation kind (<strong>None</strong>, <strong>Shared</strong>, or <strong>Composite</strong>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99f9e968-b9d2-4145-befb-9f8a039f3803"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, the <strong>Aggregation</strong> property is in the <strong>All</strong> properties display mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol><h3>Showing Association End properties</h3><p>If two classes are linked with an Association path, both Classes have an attribute of an opposite Class type. This property can be displayed on the Class shape as well as an Association link.</p><p><br />To show Association Ends as attributes on linked Class shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>From the Class shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> or press Alt+Enter. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Attribute</strong> category, click the <strong>Show Association End as Attributes</strong> property value cell.</li><li>Click one of the following display modes:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>All</strong>. Properties and Association paths will be displayed on the Class shape.</li><li><strong>Without Association Symbol</strong>. If an Association symbol is deleted, the property will be displayed on the Class shape.</li><li><strong>Do Not Show</strong>. Neither property, nor Association path will be displayed on the Class shape.</li></ul></li></ul><p>An Association End is defined as a property. It has attribute properties defined in the Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show Association Ends as Ports on the shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the shape on which you want to display Association Ends as Ports and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:<br /><ul><li>Right-click a shape to open the shortcut menu, and click <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>Press Alt+Enter.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, at the right-top corner, click to expand the menu and select the <strong>All</strong> mode.</li><li><p>Under the <strong>Ports</strong> category, click the <strong>Show Association Ends as Ports</strong> property cell and select <em>All</em>.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of displaying association end as port on shape" ac:alt="Example of displaying association end as port on shape"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_association_ends_as_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Association End" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Converting Association End properties</h3><p>To convert an Association role to an attribute</p><hr /><ol><li>On a diagram pane, select an Association with a role.</li><li>From its shortcut menu, select the <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Attribute(s)</strong>.</li></ol><p>The Association is converted to the attribute.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of association role (on the left side) conversion to attribute (on the right side)" ac:alt="Example of association role (on the left side) conversion to attribute (on the right side)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_association.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Association End" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You can specify Association End properties in the Association End Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><p>To convert Association Ends to Ports</p><hr /><ol><li>Right- click an Association End(s).</li><li>On the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Port</strong>.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Association navigability</span></h3><p>The Association navigability indicates whether it is possible to traverse an Association within an expression of a classifier to obtain the object or a set of objects associated with instances. The navigability is shown as an arrow that can be attached to the end of the path to indicate that the navigation is supported toward the classifier attached to the arrow.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22f67761-feb4-4b13-96ec-4ed37b04c97c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, an Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is not visible.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A role indicates the role played by the Class in terms of an Association. The role name is placed at the Association End, near the Class playing that role. The role name at the implementation level maps to the reference name of the opposite Class. Roles can have visibility (public, package, protected, and private).</p><p><br />To change the Association navigability, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the Association End Specification window, turn on the Expert property display mode, and do one of the following:<ul><li>Set the <strong>Navigable</strong> property value to <em>true</em> to mark the Association End as navigable.</li><li>Set the <strong>Navigable</strong> property value to <em>false</em> to mark the Association End as not navigable.</li></ul></li><li>From the Association shortcut menu, select <strong>Role of <em>&lt;class name&gt;</em> </strong> and then do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as navigable.</li><li>Click to clear <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as not navigable.</li></ul></li><li>Open the Association End shortcut menu and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as navigable.</li><li>Click to clear <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as not navigable.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="181ec65e-7216-4b58-a116-fba25484f862">To display the Association navigability</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li>From both Association Ends shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Navigability</strong>.</li></ul><p>In the following figure, the Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is visible.</p><h3>Advancing actions: navigable owned Association Ends</h3><p>The navigability describes the need for an object to access another object by navigating across the link. The Association End can be owned by a classifier or an Association. The Association End owned by a classifier can be decorated with the dot. The absence of the dot signifies the ownership by the Association.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ed26ed9-91e3-43c2-9ab1-7bfc7f5db2f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The dot notation is not enabled by default. Please pay attention to this before making decisions about the Association End ownership just from the model representation on a diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To enable a dot notation</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Options</strong> menu, click <strong>Project</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options list, select <strong>General </strong>and set the <strong>Enable Dot Notation for Associations</strong> value to <em>true</em>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Example of dot notation" ac:alt="Example of dot notation"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_on_association_end.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Association End" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>According to the UML 2.4.1 specification, the Association Ends owned by Classes and Associations is navigable. This improved functionality allows a proper management of the <em>navigableOwnedEnd</em> property for Associations:</p><ul><li>The ownership of an Association End can be changed manually.</li><li>The navigability of Association Ends owned by the Associations can be changed keeping the ownership.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the ownership of an Association End, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the Association End shortcut menu, click <strong>Owned By</strong> and then select the desired owner.</li><li>In the Association End Specification window, click the <strong>Owned By</strong> property value cell and then select the desired owner from the list.</li><li>From the Association shortcut menu, select the role of the desired Association End, click <strong>Owned By</strong> and then select the desired owner.</li><li>In the Association Specification window, click the <strong>Owned By</strong> property value cell of the role with the desired Association End and then select the desired owner from the list.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1945caa1-81f7-4505-9cdd-a583015268d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907695015</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150208 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00097: Association in Use Case diagrams

- page_id: `227150208`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150208/Association+in+Use+Case+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-15T14:05:29.518+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Association
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Association shows the participation of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR3']in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR3'], for example, instances where an Actor and a Use Case communicate with each other. This is the only relationship between Actors and Use Cases. The association relationships are also known as communication associations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Association shows the participation of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>, for example, instances where an Actor and a Use Case communicate with each other. This is the only relationship between Actors and Use Cases. The association relationships are also known as communication associations.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="association_in_use_case_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Association in Use Case diagrams" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227148740 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files

- page_id: `227148740`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148740/Attaching+reattaching+and+detaching+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-06T12:30:28.421+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Projects with file attachments
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

304082935

304082937

304093561

**On this page**

304082936

When a file is attached to a project, such file becomes a model element called Attached File.

Attached Files are displayed in the Model Browser. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Attaching files

You can attach files in one of the following ways:

44

###### Dragging the file icon to the diagram pane

To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the diagram pane

1. In the file system, select the icon of the file you need to attach.
2. Drag it to the diagram pane.

The shape of the newly created Attached File element appears on the diagram pane. The diagram owner becomes the owner of the file as well.

###### Dragging the file icon to the Containment tree

To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the Containment tree

1. Drag the file icon onto an element in the Containment tree .
2. In the shortcut menu, select **Create****Attached File**.

A new Attached File element appears in the Containment tree. The element on which you dragged the file icon becomes the owner of the file.

###### Using a command from the shortcut menu of the element selected in the Containment tree

To attach a file by using a command from the shortcut menu of an element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a possible owner for the file to be attached to.
2. In the shortcut menu, s elect Create Element > Attached File .
3. In the Select File to Attach dialog, select the file and click Attach .

A new Attached File element with its name in the edit mode appears in the Containment tree. If you do not need to change the file name, press Enter. Otherwise, type a new name and then press Enter.

Regardless of the way used to create an Attached File element, it has the same default properties, such as **Attached****At**, **Modified****At**, **Size**, **Author**, and **File**. You can see these properties, their descriptions, and values in the **Specification** window of the Attached File element. If you need to replace the attached file, you can do this using the **File** property value editor.

##### Reattaching files

To reattach the file

1. In the Containment tree, select the attached file you need to replace.
2. Press Enter to open its **Specification** window.
3. In the**Quick Filter** box, type *file*.
4. Click the cell of the **File** property value, then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
5. In the **Select File to Attach** dialog, select the new file to attach, and click **Attach**.
6. In the **Specification** window, click **Close**.

The file is reattached, and the **Modified At** property value of the appropriate Attached File element is updated. 
 
If you no longer need the attached file, you can simply remove it from the model just like any other element. Select it in the Containment tree and press Delete. If you have its shape on the diagram, select the shape and press Ctrl+D.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff40ca63-6ef0-4d35-88a8-9adbca174c0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304082935</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df3dd097-79eb-4eae-b26e-48be5bff6eb1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304082937</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13aeb866-a4b8-48e2-9c00-06c3b7af5fc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304093561</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ddd5f86-9958-4b37-91f3-04cc7d5491e8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="858560f3-9563-4d2e-ac70-2f154303f2de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">304082936</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a file is attached to a project, such file becomes a model element called Attached File.</p><p>Attached Files are displayed in the Model Browser.<br /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:title="Attached file in Containment tree" ac:alt="Attached file in Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="attached_file_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Attaching files</h3><p>You can attach files in one of the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbdd2802-e48d-48ec-9e88-664d55b7a3b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4>Dragging the file icon to the diagram pane</h4><p>To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the file system, select the icon of the file you need to attach.</li><li>Drag it to the diagram pane.</li></ol><p>The shape of the newly created Attached File element appears on the diagram pane. The diagram owner becomes the owner of the file as well.</p><h4>Dragging the file icon to the Containment tree</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag the file icon onto an element in the Containment tree</span>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create </strong><strong>Attached File</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">A new Attached File element appears in the Containment tree. The element on which you dragged the file icon becomes the owner of the file.</span></p><h4>Using a command from the shortcut menu of the element selected in the Containment tree</h4><p>To attach a file by using a command from the shortcut menu of an element</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click a possible owner for the file to be attached to.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the shortcut menu, s</span>elect <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt;<strong> Attached File</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select File to Attach</strong> dialog, select the file and click <strong>Attach</strong>.</li></ol><p>A new Attached File element with its name in the edit mode appears in the Containment tree. If you do not need to change the file name, press Enter. Otherwise, type a new name and then press Enter.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b93fe4d-8585-43c3-9bee-3f8e2209d4b7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Regardless of the way used to create an Attached File element, it has the same default properties, such as <strong>Attached </strong><strong>At</strong>, <strong>Modified</strong><strong> At</strong>, <strong>Size</strong>, <strong>Author</strong>, and <strong>File</strong>. You can see these properties, their descriptions, and values in the <strong>Specification</strong> window of the Attached File element. If you need to replace the attached file, you can do this using the <strong>File</strong> property value editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Reattaching files</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To reattach the file</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Containment tree, select the attached file you need to replace.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Press Enter to open its <strong>Specification</strong> window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> In the<strong> Quick Filter</strong> box, type <em>file</em>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Click the cell of the <strong>File</strong> property value, then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Select File to Attach</strong> dialog</span>, select the new file to attach, and click <strong>Attach</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Specification</strong> window</span>, click <strong>Close</strong>. </span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> The file is reattached, and the <strong>Modified At</strong> property value of the appropriate Attached File element is updated.</span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> If you no longer need the attached file, you can simply remove it from the model just like any other element. Select it in the Containment tree and press Delete. If you have its shape on the diagram, select the shape and press Ctrl+D.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150229 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00099: Attribute

- page_id: `227150229`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150229/Attribute
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:32.018+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Class
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Logitics

227180465

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Logitics

227180477

227292795

**On this Page**

- [New Attribute](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-NewAttribute)
- [Attribute Name](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeName)
- [Attribute Type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeType)
- [Additional Information About Attribute Type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-AdditionalInformationAboutAttributeType)
- [Attribute Visibility](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeVisibility)
- [Attribute Multiplicity](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeMultiplicity)
- [Attribute Changeability](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeChangeability)
- [Convert Attribute To Association](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-ConvertAttributeToAssociation)
- [Convert Attribute To Port](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Attribute#Attribute-ConvertAttributeToPort)

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Logitics

227180467

An attribute is a named property of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR3'] that describes a range of values that can be held by the instances of that Class. You can specify attribute properties in the attribute's Specification window. Specifying attribute properties include changing the name, defining the type, converting the attribute to a port, etc. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window at the bottom of the Specification window.

#### TIP: More information

More information

- For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3'].
- For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR3'] .

###### Adding New Attribute

New AttributeTo create a new attribute, do one of the following:

- In the Containment tree, from the class shortcut menu, select Create Element > Property .
- In the class Specification window > Attributes , click the Create button.
- On the diagram pane, select the class and press Ctrl+Alt+A .
- On the diagram pane, select the class and click **Create Property**.
- On the diagram pane, select the class and click **Create Element** > **Property**.

###### Attribute Name

The attribute's name must be unique in the Class scope. Nevertheless, do one of the following instructions to change the attribute's name.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Illegal characters for attribute names are **( ) [ ] { } ! @ # % ; ', ; : ' " ?**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of changing the attribute name from 'country' to 'Country'

Attribute NameTo change an attribute name

- Click the Attribute in the selected Class on the diagram pane or in the Browser tree. Type a new name. [ATTACHMENT filename='Country Renaming.png']
- Click on the Class block, double-click on the Attribute, and on the Attribute Specification window, change the name and click Close . [ATTACHMENT filename='Name Property country.png']

###### Attribute Type

#### NOTE: Attribute Type

Attribute Type

The attribute's type can be any of these: Class, Interface, or Primitive Class, such as *int* or *double*. You are allowed to define the attribute's type.

Attribute TypeTo define the type of an attribute

- Do one of the following:
  - Open the Specification window of an Attribute in your Class block, by right-clicking on the Attribute, and click on the cell of the Type property value. Then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-08 at 10.40.42 PM.png'] button. In the opened window, select the type. Click OK .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Attribute, and then point to Type . In the opened list, select the type or point to New to create a new type.
  - On the Class shape, click the Attribute, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-08 at 10.46.42 PM.png'] button. In the opened list, select the desired type.
  - On the Class shape, click the Attribute, and then click the Attribute the second time. After the Attribute name, type a colon ":" and then type in the name of the new attribute type. Click Enter or click anywhere on the diagram. The type is now created and assigned.

#### TIP: Colon

Colon

Type the colon ":" , press **Ctrl+Space** or **Ctrl+Backspace**, and then in the open list, select the **type**.

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024xR3'] , select an element and drag it to the Attribute on the diagram.

###### Additional Information About Attribute Type

AdditionalTo add additional information about the type of an attribute

1. Open the Attribute Specification window, by right-clicking on the Attribute.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. In the Type Modifier list, select a particular sign in the:
  1. & - one class has a reference to other model elements.
  2. * - one class has a pointer to other model elements.
  3. [] - one class has an array of other model elements.

###### Attribute Visibility

| Visibility Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Public '+' | An attribute can be accessed by any other elements. |
| Package '~' | An attribute can be accessed by elements from the same package. |
| Protected '#' | An attribute can be accessed from the inside of the selected class and classes derived from that class. |
| Private '-' | An attribute can be accessed only from inside of that class. |

#### INFO: Visibility

Visibility

The attribute visibility is shown at the attribute signature.

Attribute VisibilityTo set the attribute visibility

Do one of the following:

- Type '+', '~', '-', or '#' visibility marks just before an Attribute name directly on a diagram.
- Open the Attribute Specification window. From the Visibility drop-down list, select the desired item: public, package, protected, or private.

Attribute Scope

ScopeTo set an attribute scope

1. Open the Attribute Specification window.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. Select the Is Static check box to true .

###### Attribute Multiplicity

MultiplicityTo set the attribute multiplicity

1. Open the Attribute Specification window.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. Select or set the multiplicity value for the Multiplicity field.

###### Attribute Changeability

The attribute changeability controls the access by operations on the class on the opposite end.

| Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Is Read Only | When false - no restrictions on modifications.When true - the value can not be altered after the object is instantiated and its values initialized. No additional values can be added to a set. |

ChangeabilityTo set the attribute changeability

1. Open the Attribute Specification window.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. Set the Is Read Only check box to true .

###### Convert Attribute To Association

#### NOTE: Converting an Attribute

Converting an Attribute

Keep in mind a few things about an attribute when converting it to an association:

- the attribute has to have a type specified, and
- the attribute is not a member end of some association.

AssociationTo convert an attribute to an association

1. On a diagram pane, select a Class with an Attribute, or select an Attribute itself.
2. From the Class's or Attribute's shortcut menu, select the Refactor command, then Convert To , and Association .
3. On the diagram pane, the Attribute is represented as an association with a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Role' space='CBMP2024xR3']**role** .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### TIP: Logitics

Logitics

You can move attributes back to the class by dragging and dropping them on the class shape.

###### Convert Attribute To Port

PortTo convert to a port

1. Right-click an Attribute or multiple Attributes.
2. On their shortcut menu, click Refactor > Convert To > Port .

227180461

**Related Pages**

- UML elements
- Class
- Class diagram
- Editing Property Values
- Specification window
