ac:name="id">227180467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An attribute is a named property of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link> that describes a range of values that can be held by the instances of that Class. You can specify attribute properties in the attribute's Specification window. Specifying attribute properties include changing the name, defining the type, converting the attribute to a port, etc. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window at the bottom of the Specification window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b34b21a3-c6d6-4b97-aca6-15736f4ae9a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about the Specification window usage, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li>For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing property values" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Editing Property Values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding New Attribute</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91667eb7-1c84-455d-a21f-8035f6e62ebe"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New Attribute</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a new attribute, do one of the following:</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, from the class shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; The type is now created and assigned.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05831661-3126-49e0-87ac-636861007ae9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Colon</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Type the colon &quot;:&quot; - one class has a reference to other model elements.</li><li>* - one class has a pointer to other model elements.</li><li>[] - one class has an array of other model elements.</li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Attribute Visibility</h4><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 14.344385%;" /><col style="width: 85.696686%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Visibility Name</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Public '+'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed by any other elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Package '~'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed by elements from the same package.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Protected '#'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed from the inside of the selected class and classes derived from that class.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Private '-'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed only from inside of that class.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a7f8f46-9e86-49c4-9b5c-df80ad95289a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Visibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The attribute visibility is shown at the attribute signature.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f40674c-1c36-4dbe-b149-9ca84b1e3ea0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Attribute Visibility</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set the attribute visibility</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Type '+', '~', '-', or '#' visibility marks just before an Attribute name directly on a diagram.</li><li>Open the Attribute Specification window. From the <strong>Visibility</strong> drop-down list, select the desired item: public, package, protected, or private.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="font-weight: bold;">Attribute Scope</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32ad5469-542d-40f7-aaf9-067d91cf0e52"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Scope</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set an attribute scope</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Attribute Specification window.</li><li>In the top right corner of the window, select <strong>Expert</strong> in the <strong>Properties</strong> drop-down menu.</li><li>Select the <strong>Is Static</strong> check box to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Attribute Multiplicity</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20920863-6e0b-4ec7-bb50-4e792eb0306a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Multiplicity </ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set the attribute multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Attribute Specification window.</li><li>In the top right corner of the window, select <strong>Expert</strong> in the <strong>Properties</strong> drop-down menu.</li><li>Select or set the multiplicity value for the <strong>Multiplicity</strong> field.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Attribute Changeability </h4><p>The attribute changeability controls the access by operations on the class on the opposite end.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Is Read Only</strong></td><td><p>When <em>false</em> - no restrictions on modifications.</p><p>When <em>true</em> - the value can not be altered after the object is instantiated and its values initialized. No additional values can be added to a set.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0380cf81-bd94-4c71-b146-ab19f222a2f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Changeability </ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set the attribute changeability </p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Attribute Specification window.</li><li>In the top right corner of the window, select <strong>Expert</strong> in the <strong>Properties</strong> drop-down menu.</li><li>Set the <strong>Is Read Only</strong> check box to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Convert Attribute To Association </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2655b20d-27e2-4495-a139-92cb3e94b7ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Converting an Attribute</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keep in mind a few things about an attribute when converting it to an association:</p><ul><li>the attribute has to have a <strong>type</strong> specified, and</li><li>the attribute is <strong>not</strong> a member end of some association.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b73cc0b2-ab93-455b-8808-1ffc51048707"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Association </ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To convert an attribute to an association</p><hr /><ol><li>On a diagram pane, select a Class with an Attribute, or select an Attribute itself.</li><li>From the Class's or Attribute's shortcut menu, select the <strong>Refactor</strong> command, then <strong>Convert To</strong>, and <strong>Association</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram pane, the Attribute is represented as an <strong>association</strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0dbbfdc4-0c54-4a85-807e-4a3a03ef5955">with a</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CBMP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Role" /><ac:link-body><strong>role</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Combined Association Role.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Attribute" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dce3c28-a549-477e-84fd-67327ec2b3c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Logitics</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can move attributes back to the class by dragging and dropping them on the class shape.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Convert Attribute To Port</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4d53c02-44d2-4957-a51d-a44c293f34fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Port</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To convert to a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="37dee737-7ee2-44a0-9260-77527fc2ac36">port</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click an Attribute or multiple Attributes.</li><li>On their shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor </strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e4c0c441-a688-4526-9306-ba4df07f00dd">&gt;</ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0abd4243-a172-4c8c-b2cf-66133654fa25"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1407502748</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d5bbb25-7af6-4a80-ae0d-399d7c03c8b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1407502750</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a31b80af-054c-434c-a3c1-0a7c65a4c411"><ac:parameter style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-23_11-43-15.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Attribute in DSL Customization" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227148944 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00101: Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `227148944`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148944/Authentication+with+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:25.622+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

The **3D**EXPERIENCE menu groups all available integrations with the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='IL2024xR3'] supporting the integration with the platform, you can access the related functionalities in the **3D**EXPERIENCE menu.

For some functionalities to work, you need to log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform as described below. Note that you can log in to the platform deployed either on-premises or on the cloud server.

You can manage all secure connections, including self-signed certificates, using the [Server Certificates](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Server+Certificates) tool.

To log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform from the modeling tool

1. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Login .
2. In the Server box of the open dialog, enter the server address. If the 3D EXPERIENCE platform is deployed on the cloud, select the The 3DEXPERIENCE platform is hosted on the cloud check box. [ATTACHMENT filename='login_dialog.png']
3. Click OK .
4. In the respective boxes of the open dialog, enter your 3D EXPERIENCE username and password. [ATTACHMENT filename='log_in_to_platform.png']
5. Click Log in .

Once you log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can use all the functionalities of the installed plugins without canceling the session.

3D

##### Switching credentials

Once you log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can switch to a different platform, the platform's collaborative space, or a different access role without terminating your session.

To switch to different **3D**EXPERIENCE platform credentials

1. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Switch Credentials .
2. In the **Credentials**drop-down list box of the open dialog, select the credentials you want to switch to and click**OK**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Credentials** drop-down list box displays only the platforms, collaborative spaces, and roles that you have access to. They are displayed in the following format: Platform • Collaborative Space• Access Role.

##### Logging out

When you finish working on a project in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can terminate your session by logging out of the platform.

To log out of the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

- In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Logout .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227154084 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00102: Auto importing template tool

- page_id: `227154084`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154084/Auto+importing+template+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T18:01:30.579+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard can automatically import a Template Tool to a template without having to declare the #import statement in a template code manually (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing custom tool to template' space='MD2024xR3']*).

To enable Report Wizard to automatically import a Template Tool, you have to use the existing template variable inside “*template.xml*” and “*report.xml*”.

The current template variable syntax:

```text
Variable Name
  Variable Description
  
  Variable Value
]]>
```

Add "com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool" to a type value where the name is a variable name and the value is a full-qualified template tool class, for example:

To automatically import TextTool

1. Edit the built-in.xml file in the ...\data\reports\Tutorial 24 - Text Tool\reports .
2. Add a variable as shown in the following example.

```text
text
  A text tool
  com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool
  com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool
]]>
```

The result is a template code that can access com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool from $text.

| $text.noLineBreak($diagram.name) |
| --- |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once you have edited a template variable, you have to restart the application prior to generating a report through the modeling tool.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard can automatically import a Template Tool to a template without having to declare the #import statement in a template code manually (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing custom tool to template" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing Custom Tool to Template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>).</p><p>To enable Report Wizard to automatically import a Template Tool, you have to use the existing template variable inside “<em>template.xml</em>” and “<em>report.xml</em>”.</p><p>The current template variable syntax:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e62d12b4-085e-45ac-80ff-df405fa3a4ae"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<variable>
  <name>Variable Name</name>
  <description>Variable Description</description>
  <type/>
  <value xml:space="preserve">Variable Value</value>
</variable>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Add &quot;com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool&quot; to a type value where the name is a variable name and the value is a full-qualified template tool class, for example:</p><p><br /></p><p>To automatically import TextTool</p><hr /><ol><li>Edit the built-in.xml file in the <em>...\data\reports\Tutorial 24 - Text Tool\reports</em>.</li><li>Add a variable as shown in the following example.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c01b07d8-75a2-4127-840a-5fcea178f72b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<variable>
  <name>text</name>
  <description>A text tool</description>
  <type>com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool</type>
  <value xml:space="preserve">com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool</value>
</variable>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The result is a template code that can access com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool from $text.</p><table><tbody><tr><td><pre>$text.noLineBreak($diagram.name)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Auto importing template tool" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94bc0efd-71ea-4d5a-8742-94117ad9f534"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Once you have edited a template variable, you have to restart the application prior to generating a report through the modeling tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151478 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00103: Automated Package Structure Creation

- page_id: `227151478`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151478/Automated+Package+Structure+Creation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:52.223+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of copy-pasting an existing package structure, you can create one automatically. You can do that by creating your own package structure configurations first and then applying them to your model via the **Create Package Structure** action.

##### Creating a package structure configuration

To create a package structure configuration

1. Create a configuration element, which can be any named element (e.g. a Package named 'SubsystemPackages').
2. Right-click the configuration element. In the shortcut menu, click Stereotype > select «package StructureConfiguration » from the menu, and click Apply .
3. Right-click the configuration element and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
5. Under «package StructureConfiguration » select applyToElements and click the Create Value button.
6. In the **Select Elements** dialog, select the element types for which the **Create Package Structure** action will be available. Use the **Multiple Selection**option to specify multiple element types.
7. Under «package StructureConfiguration » select CreateElements and click the Create Value button.
8. In the **Value** box, create alist of packages that will be created. The packages' nesting structure should be defined using spacing as indents (see the image below). If, in addition to spacing, any indent symbols (e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.)are used to define the structure, see **Step 9**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. (Optional) If the structure defined in the **CreateElements** tag is specified using any indent symbols (e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.)that you do not want to include in the created packages' names, select **RemoveIndentSymbols**, click the **Edit Value**button and set it to *true*. By default, the **RemoveIndentSymbols**tag is set to *false*.
10. Click Close .

##### Creating a package structure automatically

To create a package structure automatically

1. Right-click an element for which you want to create the package structure defined in the configuration. In the shortcut menu, click **Tools**> **Create Package Structure**. The **Select Package Structure**dialog opens. The **Create Package Structure**action is only available for elements specified in the **applyToElements**tag of the package structure configuration.
2. In the Select Package Structure dialog, select the package structure configuration element that defines the package structure you want to create. Click OK . The selected package structure is created for the element.

MagicGrid project templates contain two package structure configurations included by default. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using packages' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227148863 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00104: Automated project usages

- page_id: `227148863`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148863/Automated+project+usages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:26:34.688+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418314237

418314240

418314239

**Automated usages of other projects in the main project**are the usages fully managed by our modeling tool. For example, it can create, remove, and rearrange as necessary without user intervention.

##### Version 17.0.3 and later

Version 17.0.3 introduces a new type of project usages, **automated project usages**. Our modeling tool fully manages them. It creates, removes, and rearranges as necessary without user intervention. **Automated project usages** are not shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD2024xR3']**Used Project** dialog as only **user-defined usages** are shown there.

##### Before version 17.0.3

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, there was only one type of project usage. The user created these **user-defined usages** and they carried additional parameters; i.e., whether the used project was used in read-only or read/write mode, the used project loading options, which used project version was required, etc.

When modifying projects with complex composition, always keep the following condition in mind:

*Whenever there are model level references from elements in resource A (either a used project or the main project) to elements in resource B, there is a used project usage A → B.*

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could see this condition in action when working with used projects in read/write mode. Whenever you modified the contents of a used project so that it started to refer to another used project, and then you attempted to save it, our modeling tool would add an additional usage. You would then see the messages: “*Used project B was directly attached to A*” or “*Mounts were updated ...*”.

However, in some cases, this system behavior caused undesirable consequences, as shown in the following pages:

418321539

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project partitioning' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3fd32ca-0dc5-4b7d-8714-4b3f96e6297e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418314237</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="914c53c5-eb35-41a6-9e9c-772e495b2c55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418314240</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="299ed167-6c37-4b4c-9cc5-b38b9dfa127a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418314239</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Automated usages of other projects in the main project </strong>are the usages fully managed by our modeling tool. For example, it can create, remove, and rearrange as necessary without user intervention.</p><h3>Version 17.0.3 and later</h3><p>Version 17.0.3 introduces a new type of project usages, <strong>automated project usages</strong>. Our modeling tool fully manages them. It creates, removes, and rearranges as necessary without user intervention. <strong>Automated project usages</strong> are not shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Project</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> as only <strong>user-defined usages</strong> are shown there.</p><h3>Before version 17.0.3</h3><p>In earlier versions of our modeling tool, there was only one type of project usage. The user created these <strong>user-defined usages</strong> and they carried additional parameters; i.e., whether the used project was used in read-only or read/write mode, the used project loading options, which used project version was required, etc.</p><p>When modifying projects with complex composition, always keep the following condition in mind:</p><p style="text-align: left;margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Whenever there are model level references from elements in resource A (either a used project or the main project) to elements in resource B, there is a used project usage A → B.</em></p><p style="text-align: left;">In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could see this condition in action when working with used projects in read/write mode. Whenever you modified the contents of a used project so that it started to refer to another used project, and then you attempted to save it, our modeling tool would add an additional usage. You would then see the messages: “<em>Used project B was directly attached to A</em>” or “<em>Mounts were updated ...</em>”.</p><p>However, in some cases, this system behavior caused undesirable consequences, as shown in the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d7aea0e8-3a0a-4ea3-abac-46cfb553328d" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48be605c-1e90-4dc8-81d5-c339bc12456a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418321539</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing Used Projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project partitioning" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Partitioning]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151416 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00105: Automatic Instantiation Wizard

- page_id: `227151416`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151416/Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:54:51.300+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64321073

64321075

64321074

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts.

While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypesto the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. Previously, this feature had been supported by the **SysML plugin only**.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** analyzes the structure of the selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). All this information is presented in the first step of the wizard.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** evaluates collected information and provides a suggestion of all possible instances to be created.

If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation wizard,** you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. But if you want to change default values, create several different configurations, see the **Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard** and the **Case study:** sections on how to do that.

The **Automatic Instantiation Wizard** consists of three steps:

- Step #1. Selecting parts to be instantiated
- Step #2. Selecting a package
- Step #3. Creating a diagram (optional)

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the elements for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

64321072

**Related pages**

- Instance Specification
- Constraint Link
- Value Specification
- Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard
- Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study
- Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML Plugin

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151429 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00106: Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study

- page_id: `227151429`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151429/Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard+Case+Study
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T07:54:14.967+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Automatic Instantiation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Consider this example:**

Let’s say we have three classes named **Administrator**, **Librarian**, and **Personnel**. These classes are connected in the following way:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The multiplicity in the association end near the **Librarian** is 4. In this case, the **Automatic Instantiation wizard** suggests four parts.

In the following figure, these values are displayed as **[1]**, **[2]**, **[3]**, and **[4]**. Each of them have a set of properties inherited from the type of the parts. In this case, we cannot add more parts because the multiplicity points to the particular number of available parts. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.

In this case, class **Librarian** has a sub-class **Personnel**, so the type of **part [1]** can be changed to **Personnel** as you can see in the following figure.

Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:

1. Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.
2. Create a new package Instances of the Administrator .
3. Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.
4. After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the Finish button.
5. See the result in the following figure.

These classes are connected in the following way:</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_classes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p>The multiplicity in the association end near the <strong>Librarian</strong> is 4. In this case, the <strong>Automatic Instantiation wizard</strong> suggests four parts.</p><p>In the following figure, these values are displayed as <strong>[1]</strong>, <strong>[2]</strong>, <strong>[3]</strong>, and <strong>[4]</strong>. Each of them have a set of properties inherited from the type of the parts. In this case, we cannot add more parts because the multiplicity points to the particular number of available parts. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.</p><p>In this case, class <strong>Librarian</strong> has a sub-class <strong>Personnel</strong>, so the type of <strong>part [1]</strong> can be changed to <strong>Personnel</strong> as you can see in the following figure.</p><p>Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:</p><ol><li>Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.</li><li>Create a new package <strong>Instances of the Administrator</strong>.</li><li>Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.</li><li>After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the <strong>Finish</strong> button. </li><li>See the result in the following figure. </li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151117 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00107: Automatic numbering

- page_id: `227151117`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151117/Automatic+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-02-20T14:07:36.619+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism > Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space='MD2024xR3'] are already created.

With an automatic numbering feature, you can number the elements of the selected type upon the element creation or modification. You can specify the number display location after the elements are numbered.

To enable automatic element numbering

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR3']**Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the options group list, select or expand the General option and click Numbering either in the options list or in the options group list. The numbering properties opens.
3. Set the Use Element Auto-numbering property value to true .
4. Click OK .

To specify the element number display location

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Symbol Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens.
  - From the main menu, select Options > Project . Then, in the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024xR3']**Project Options** dialog , select the Symbol styles > Default group.
2. Select one of the following **Element Numbering Display Mode** property value: Property valueVisualization on the element shapeAbove the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Before the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Do not display on symbol[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click OK after you have selected.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manual numbering' space='MD2024xR3'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227148602 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00108: Autosave

- page_id: `227148602`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148602/Autosave
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:23:08.442+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you stop working with the modeling tool,an idle time passes and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. 
If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.

To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use

1. Open the Environment Options dialog.
2. In the General pane, select the Save/Load group, and then set the Save Project Recovery Data on Idle option to true .
3. Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the Idle Time to Activate Recovery Save box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After you stop working with the modeling tool,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> an idle time passes</span> and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> pane, select the <strong>Save/Load</strong> group, and then set the <strong>Save Project Recovery Data on Idle </strong>option to <em>true</em>. </li><li>Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the <strong>Idle</strong> <strong>Time</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Activate</strong> <strong>Recovery</strong> <strong>Save</strong> box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00109: Auxiliary diagram symbols

You can create auxiliary diagram symbols from the **Common**category in the diagram palette.

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a65674a-e89a-4cdd-aecf-35432f493aa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490071274</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f6698d7-4d9b-4ed9-a73b-11f5c3e6ead5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490071277</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5749df2f-59f0-472a-b0ac-d031040f8e13"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490071276</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>This section lists auxiliary diagram symbols, including simple text box, image shape, whole diagram overview, and others. These symbols can be used in all diagrams.</span></p><p>You can create auxiliary diagram symbols from the <strong>Common </strong>category in the diagram palette.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="common_elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Auxiliary diagram symbols" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Representation of the Common category in the Diagram pallet</h6><p>Refer to the following symbol descriptions:<br /><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="32951aa4-99c3-4cab-b7cf-59791f76d98e"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="449c73b7-a125-4829-b32e-fd6feef2ec28"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490071273</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagramming" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151350 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00110: Available Transformations

- page_id: `227151350`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151350/Available+Transformations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:23:42.152+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:

- Any to Any . This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.
- Profile Migration . Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).

UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).

Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:</p><ul><li><strong>Any to Any</strong>. This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Profile Migration</strong>. Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d03b38f1-c9ac-4094-9c27-5adee5b78388"><ac:rich-text-body><p>UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83a24d82-c890-4d54-9bba-50ac5c213ddd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227149884 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00111: Avoiding Conflicting Legend items

- page_id: `227149884`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227149884/Avoiding+Conflicting+Legend+items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-06T11:19:47.309+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends > Specifying Legend Items
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you use multiple Legends in one diagram, you should specify a different set of adornment properties for Legend Items of each Legend. This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using **Fill Color** in one Legend and **Icon** in another. If you want to use the **Icon** property in multiple Legends, then specify different **Icon Position on Shape**/**Path** for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the **Pen Color**, **Line Width,** and **Line Style** properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.

#### TIP: Legend Item priority

Legend Item priority

If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the **Priority** property for each Legend Item. It defines which Legend Item should be applied in case several Legend Items conflict.

##### Example

Let's say you want to use two Legends in a Requirements Diagram: the *Status* Legend indicating the status of each Requirement and the *Risk* Legend showing the risk level of Requirements. It is likely that Legend Items from both Legends will be applied to the same Requirement. For example, a single Requirement can have both status and risk values specified. Let's analyze this specific use case by creating the Legends mentioned above and defining their Legend Item adornment properties so that Legend Items would not conflict when applied to the same Requirement:

1. Open the desired Requirements Diagram.
2. In the diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024xR3'] intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend Status .
3. Create and define the Legend Items for the Status Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='MD2024xR3'] indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them Closed , Approved , Disapproved, and In Progress .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR3']Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Status property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.
4. In the same diagram, create a Legend to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend Risk .
5. Create and define the Legend Items for the Risk Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. Create three Legend Items indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them High , Medium , and Low .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired **Icon** and **Icon Position on Shape**. Specifying IconWhen specifying the **Icon** property:Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select an image from the Image Library of your modeling tool.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select a custom image from the local file system.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to specify the URL of an image.
  4. Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the **Risk** property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.

If you followed the above steps, you have a Requirements Diagram with the *Status* and *Risk* Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict when applied to the same shape, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure displays a Requirement shape with two Legend Items applied to it. 
The Legend Items do not conflict due to different adornment properties used to define them.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you use multiple Legends in one diagram, you should specify a different set of adornment properties for Legend Items of each Legend. This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using <strong>Fill Color</strong> in one Legend and <strong>Icon</strong> in another. If you want to use the <strong>Icon</strong> property in multiple Legends, then specify different <strong>Icon Position on Shape</strong>/<strong>Path</strong> for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the <strong>Pen Color</strong>, <strong>Line Width,</strong> and <strong>Line Style</strong> properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ade7b350-1fd3-44cc-9bdc-bf32eb39765d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Legend Item priority</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the <strong>Priority</strong> property for each Legend Item. Condition</strong> property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the <strong>Risk</strong> property value the Legend Item represents.</p></li><li>Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227148550 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00112: Background Task Manager

- page_id: `227148550`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227148550/Background+Task+Manager
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-16T13:27:50.132+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Improving performance
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427371678

1427371681

1427371679

Low-priority or routine tasks are moved to separate threads and are performed as background tasks. These tasks do not stop a modeling tool from running, and you can work with your model as usual. Background tasks are displayed in the Background Task Manager at the bottom right corner of the modeling tool window. For example, when a search process is running, the progress bar is displayed in the Background Task Manager, shown in the following figure.

If a background task runs for too long, you can cancel it as described below.

To end an ongoing process

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='end_process.png'] in the Background Task Manager status bar to cancel the selected process. If you cancel the last of several tasks (or only one existing process), the Background Task Manager is turned off.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Background Task Manager showing the progress of a searching task.

1427371677

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Improving performance' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space='MD2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151609 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00113: Backward traceability – specification

- page_id: `227151609`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151609/Backward+traceability+%E2%80%93+specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-25T09:56:03.885+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Traceability > Predefined traceability rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Property Name | Description | Applied For: | Reference Through: | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested in Artifacts | The property shows which components are manifested in an artifact. | Artifact | Relationships: Manifestation | Component | Example |
| Specifying Class | The property shows the classes representing the component specification from the Design model. | Component | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Specifying Component | The property shows the components that are realized by classifiers through component realization. | Classifier | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Component | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases (from the Requirements model) representing the class specification. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases that specify the given use case in the higher level of abstraction. For example, the Business Use Case specifies the Requirements Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realized Interface | The property shows the interfaces specifying the contract, in which the related classifier conforms to. | Classifier | Relationships: Interface Realization | Interface | Example |
| Specifying Element, All Specifying ElementsAvailable in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Specifying Element property gathers specifying elements from the upper abstraction level. The All Specifying Elements property transitively gathers specifying elements from all upper abstraction levels. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |

ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0f8f0c6-cd5d-4fa6-bef4-eface8400999"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:align="left"><ri:attachment ri:filename="traceability_rules12.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Backward traceability – specification" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Realized Interface</td><td><div class="section"><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>The property shows the interfaces specifying the contract, in which the related classifier conforms to. </span></p></div></div></div></td><td>Classifier</td><td><div class="section"><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Relationships: </span>Interface Realization</p></div></div></div></td><td>Interface</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="683f39f7-e4bc-447e-ba3d-0c67ab7e67f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:align="left"><ri:attachment ri:filename="traceability_rules13.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Backward traceability – specification" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="081cf912-3545-43dc-a473-45eba5faac81">Specifying Element, All Specifying Elements</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1423c7d5-cfea-4c2d-aa21-2894bc583ccd"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Available in Enterprise and Architect editions only.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div></div></td><td><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>The </span> <strong>Specifying Element </strong> <span>property gathers specifying elements from the upper abstraction level. </span></p><p>The <strong>All Specifying Elements </strong>property transitively gathers specifying elements from all upper abstraction levels. </p></div></div></td><td>Element</td><td><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Relationships: </span>Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization.</p></div></div></td><td>Element</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="659d761c-e142-4405-ae78-cfb8d8b2f69b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ## PAGE 00114: Basic DSL Concepts

- page_id: `227154306`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154306/Basic+DSL+Concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-30T09:58:07.570+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| DSL | Domain Specific Language |
| Stereotyped element | An element with no customized stereotype applied. |
| Customization element | An element in which the customization data is specified. Create the customization element by using a Profile diagram. |
| Customization data | A collection of properties and attributes of the Customization element. |
| Customization model | A collection of customization elements. |
| DSL element | An element with the applied customization data. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227149469 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00115: Basic tasks in tables

- page_id: `227149469`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227149469/Basic+tasks+in+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:10:50.435+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

218215203

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

218215208

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

218215207

Tables are diagrams which allow you to manage any amount of model data in a spreadsheet-like form. It is much easier to analyze a group of data in one place, especially when you can create new elements, add existing elements, edit their properties directly from the table, and paste data from the clipboard. This allows you to sort and filter data, show, hide, reorder columns, and clear or edit individual cells. Exporting tables into more commonly used file formats, such as *.html*, *.csv*, or *.xlsx*, makes it simple to share them with stakeholders.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

Each table row represents an element. Each column represents element properties. The column header and row number header are highlighted in grey when selecting single or multiple cells.

The following procedures provide the basic tasks to get you started working with all modeling tool tables:

218215198

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227150211 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00116: Behavior

- page_id: `227150211`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227150211/Behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-11-14T11:40:26.459+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['classifier-behavior', 'owned-behavior', 'behavior-type']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Behavior describes how the states of objects change over time.

Behavior types are as follows:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Behavior' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

Behaviors are used:

- To [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning behavior to state' space='MD2024xR3'] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR3'] .
- To [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behavior Type' space='MD2024xR3'] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR3'] .

The Behaviors are displayed in the compartments of the following elements:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR3']

Behaviors are divided into two groups:

- classifier Behaviors and
- owned Behaviors.

Keep in mind that the **classifier** Behavior group can have only **one** Behavior.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Working With Behavior

Working With Behavior

#### PANEL: Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227153488 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00117: Bookmark tool

- page_id: `227153488`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227153488/Bookmark+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-12T21:48:12.744+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050367121

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050367135

2050367115

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050367125

$bookmark contains utility functions for bookmarking. The functions of this module are accessible from templates through **$bookmark**.

#### INFO: Information

Information

The default style of bookmarks depends on the editor used. For example, Microsoft Word 2003 displays hyperlinks in blue.

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(url, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URL.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | url | java.lang.String | A URL text. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(url, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URL.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | url | java.net.URL | A URL instance. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(uri, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URI.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | uri | java.net.URI | A URI instance. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.open(content)**

Create a hyperlink for a bookmark. The bookmark ID will be automatically generated from the content.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

****

###### **$bookmark.open(bookmarkId, content)**

Create a hyperlink for a bookmark. The bookmark ID value must match the parameter bookmark ID that passes onto the link.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkObject)**

Create a bookmark. The bookmark ID will be automatically generated from the bookmark object.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

****

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkId, bookmarkObject)**

Create a bookmark. The default element type for this function is "label".

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

For example:

```text

```

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkId, bookmarkObject, elementType)**

Create a bookmark by specifying an element type.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
|  | elementType | java.lang.String | An element type name, e.g., html tag. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

****

###### **$bookmark.getBookmarkId(id)**

Return a bookmark ID from a given string.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | id | java.lang.String | An original string value. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The bookmark ID. |

****

###### **$bookmark.createRef(referenceID)**

Create a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

****

###### **$bookmark.openRef(referenceID)**

Create a hyperlink for a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can press F9 to replace the text content shown as **Refer** with the updated numbered item that it refers to.

****

###### **$bookmark.openRef(referenceID, referenceText)**

Create a hyperlink for a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
|  | referenceText | java.lang.String | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can press F9 to replace the text content shown as **Refer referenceText** with the updated numbered item that it refers to.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227152395 space=MD2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00118: Branching projects

- page_id: `227152395`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227152395/Branching+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T11:55:04.511+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to have the Project Merge plugin license key to use the merge functionality.

#### NOTE: Permissions

Permissions

To create, remove, rename, archive, or unarchive a branch, you need to have the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR3']**Administer Resources** permission assigned.

Project branching duplicates versions of a project and enables users to work simultaneously on them. Branching increases productivity for those, who have several development teams working on a single product and have to merge several development branches before releasing the product.

Branches can be created from any version of a Teamwork Cloud project or its branch. The branch version holds the information about the project version it is derived from. Every branch version can be tagged. Two versions of the same of different project branch can be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comparing projects' space='MD2024xR3']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MD2024xR3']into a single one.

Projects that have usages can be branched *with* or *without* these usages. In the later case, the branch version of the project references versions of the projects used in the project, which that branch is derived from (it can be the latest version or some specific version selected by the user).

To create a project branch

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select a project you want to branch.
3. On the toolbar of the Manage Projects dialog, click the Select Branches button [ATTACHMENT filename='select_branches.png'] . The Select Branch dialog opens.
4. In the open dialog, click the Edit Branches button. The Edit Branches dialog opens.
5. Select a project version you want to branch.
6. Click the Create Branch button. The Create Branch dialog opens.
7. In the open dialog, type the branch name and comment. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_a_branch.png']
8. Click OK . The new branch is created, and the dialog closes.
9. The **Edit Branches** dialog displays the new branch of the project. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Search barUse the search bar to filter projects according to:Project Version (together with the commit tags)AuthorComment
10. Click OK . The dialog closes.
11. If you want to open the newly created branch, do the following:
  1. Select it in the Select Branch dialog and click OK . The dialog closes.
  2. In the Manage Projects dialog, see that branch instead of the branched project.
  3. Click Open . The branch opens.

##### Archived branch

The branches that are not being worked with anymore and are only maintained for historical purposes can be hidden from users eyes.

Performance and disk space are not affected because archived branches are hidden only graphically.

To archive a project branch

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select a project you want to archive branch.
3. On the toolbar of the Manage Projects dialog, click the Select Branches button [ATTACHMENT filename='select_branches.png'] . The Select Branch dialog opens.
4. In the open dialog, click the Edit Branches button. The Edit Branches dialog opens.
5. Right-click the branch you want to archive and, on the shortcut menu, click **Mark as Archived.**

The archived branches can be recognized from the grayed out text. Same way you can uncheck archived branch and it will be no more archived.

Project branches are archived globally.

To hide or show archived branches from the dialog

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and deselect Show Archived Branches , to hide archived branches.
- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and select Show Archived Branches , to show archived branches.

This action can be done in **Edit Branches**and **Select branch**dialogs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select a project version you want to branch.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create Branch</strong> button. The <strong>Create Branch</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, type the branch name and comment.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Name and comment of the new branch" ac:alt="Name and comment of the new branch"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_a_branch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Branching projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The new branch is created, and the dialog closes.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog displays the new branch of the project.<br /><ac:image ac:title="New branch in the Edit Branches dialog" ac:alt="New branch in the Edit Branches dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_branch_in_the_edit_branches_dlg.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Branching projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c459004-01a4-49e1-a5cd-e9e03857bb9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Search bar</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the search bar to filter projects according to:</p><ul><li>Project Version (together with the commit tags)</li><li>Author</li><li>Comment</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The dialog closes.</li><li>If you want to open the newly created branch, do the following:<ol><li>Select it in the <strong>Select Branch</strong> dialog and click<strong> OK</strong>. The dialog closes.</li><li>In the<strong> Manage Projects</strong> dialog, see that branch instead of the branched project.</li><li>Click <strong>Open</strong>. The branch opens.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Archived branch</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The branches that are not being worked with anymore and are only maintained for historical purposes can be hidden from users eyes. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce7b0aa8-302b-47fb-b6b7-a79e43a43b2b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Performance and disk space are not affected because archived branches are hidden only graphically.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /></span></p><p>To archive a project branch </p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select a project you want to archive branch.</li><li>On the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog, click the Select Branches button <ac:image ac:title="Select Branches" ac:alt="Select Branches"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_branches.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Branching projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Select Branch</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, click the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> button. The <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Right-click the branch you want to archive and,<span> on the shortcut menu, click <strong>Mark as Archived. </strong></span></li></ol><p>The archived branches can be recognized from the grayed out text. Same way you can uncheck archived branch and it will be no more archived. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fbc89716-c1c5-4c25-8955-3719a435dbcb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Project branches are archived globally.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To hide or show archived branches from the dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Click  <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Branching projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and deselect <strong>Show Archived Branches</strong>, to hide archived branches.</li><li>Click  <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting element types" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Show Archived Branches</strong>, to show archived branches.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d71dbcde-eac8-4d1e-aa50-5a76ea77e95c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This action can be done in <strong>Edit Branches </strong>and <strong>Select branch </strong>dialogs. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_branches_archived_branch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Branching projects" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227151332 space=MD2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00119: Building metric definitions

- page_id: `227151332`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227151332/Building+metric+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-02-19T08:52:25.508+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Metric Suites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1891786832

1891786834

1891786833

Metric definition is the attribute of a Metric Suite with the «MetricDefinition» stereotype applied.

To build a metric definition

1. Select the shape of a Metric Suite.
2. Click the Create Element button on the shape, and then select Metric Definition . An attribute with the «MetricDefinition» stereotype is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_metric_definition.png']
3. Type its name directly on the shape and then click a free space on the diagram.
4. Select the attribute on the shape and click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type_button.png'] .
5. Select the type of the result values that should be calculated according to the formula specified in a metric definition. This step is mandatory. A metric definition must have a result type, e.g., Real or Integer.
6. Press **Enter** to open the Specification window of the metric definition.
7. Click the cell of the Default Value property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] and [ATTACHMENT filename='show_shortcut_menu.png'] appear on the cell.
8. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show_shortcut_menu.png'] and select Value Specification > Opaque Expression .
9. Click the cell of the **Default Value** property value again, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Default Value** dialog opens.
10. From the **Language** drop-down list, select a language. To build a structured expression, select **StructuredExpression**.
11. In the **Body** box, specify the formula for the metric definition. For more information about building structured expressions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR3'].
12. Click OK and then click Close in the Specification window.

The metric definition is specified.

If your Metric Suite is created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, it already has the metric definition for date tracking.

One metric definition can refer to another metric definition of either the same Metric Suite or a more general one (in case there is the Generalization relationship between these Metric Suites).

1891786831

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric Suites' space='MD2024xR3']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='MD2024xR3']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='MD2024xR3']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying parameter definitions' space='MD2024xR3']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specific use cases' space='MD2024xR3']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong>Default Value</strong> dialog opens.</p></li><li><p>From the <strong>Language</strong> drop-down list, select a language.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2541e3c1-ac6a-47c3-8392-6501c7440621"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To build a structured expression, select <strong>StructuredExpression</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>In the <strong>Body</strong> box, specify the formula for the metric definition. For more information about building structured expressions, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> and then click <strong>Close</strong> in the Specification window.</li></ol><p><br />The metric definition is specified.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="778440e4-b945-4559-9b51-a87442dabd48"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your Metric Suite is created using the <em>BaseMetricSuite</em>, it already has the metric definition for date tracking.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>One metric definition can refer to another metric definition of either the same Metric Suite or a more general one (in case there is the Generalization relationship between these Metric Suites).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c0225cd-17d6-4040-bd88-0382ff292deb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1891786831</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metric Suites" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a new Metric Suite" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specify the target for the Metric Suite" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying parameter definitions" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specific use cases" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building validation-based metric definitions" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
