````html
<p><span>This chapter describes the mapping between C++ and UML.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-3-2_13-55-17.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Stereotypes with Tag values</span></h6><p><span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="991b5975-d302-477d-97b8-9e88f2ec8771"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here to see what's on this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95d7a11f-c6e3-47e1-88d0-1684c45b68e8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>C++Class</h3><p>C++ class map to a UML class</p><p>Stereotype <em>«C++Class»</em> is an invisible stereotype used to include language properties for any C++ variable and belongs to Meta class <em>Class</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1be81bb5-b5e1-4f30-8a06-d3c627058e48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96172d50-298c-459e-b538-eee749575200"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class A { 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="left"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-10_11-9-27.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Base Class Definition</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(34,34,34);">A </span><em>base class</em><span style="color: rgb(34,34,34);"> is a </span>Class<span style="color: rgb(34,34,34);"> from which other </span><span style="color: rgb(34,34,34);">Classes<strong> </strong></span><span style="color: rgb(34,34,34);">are derived. It facilitates the creation of other </span>Classes<span style="color: rgb(34,34,34);"> that can reuse the code implicitly inherited from the </span><em>base class</em><span style="color: rgb(34,34,34);"> (except constructors and destructors).</span></p><p><span><em>Base class</em> definition is mapped to UML generalization, a generalization is created between the <em>base class</em> and the <em>super class</em>.</span></p><p>Access visibility (public, protected and private) and virtual properties of the <em>base class</em> are mapped to C++ language properties of the UML generalization. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78b1ba69-e16f-4390-9989-69fe5f0ef2cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="322a294c-043f-43db-997b-94de9e114700"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class BaseClass {}; 
class OtherBaseClass {}; 
class SuperClass : 
 public BaseClass, 
 protected virtual OtherBaseClass { 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="170"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-10_11-19-8.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_15-44-44.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Class relations</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;">Class Member Variable</span></p><p>Class member variables are mapped to UML attributes. See <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Variable"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ac:link></strong> for more info. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cc3c018-0fbb-4b86-bcb9-b697957a69b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5300204-5c9d-4c9f-b577-42e431eff30f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class ClassVariable { 
 int age; 
 char* name; 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="110"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_15-23-33.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Class Member Function</h3><p><span>Class member functions are mapped to UML operations.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37ee7332-d36a-4b2b-9bcf-0db561f9f967"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9c61338-5343-43af-8e8d-d0526a90b10c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class ClassFunction { 
public: 
 void simpleFunc(); 
 float paramFunc(int x,char y); 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="86"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_15-29-7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;">Class Constructor and Destructor</span></p><h4>C++Constructor</h4><p>Stereotype <em>«C++Constructor»</em> is used to define C++ Constructor. This stereotype extends stereotype<em> «C++Operation»</em>. </p><table><colgroup><col style="width: 0.0px;" /><col style="width: 0.0px;" /><col style="width: 0.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Name</p></th><th><p>Meta class</p></th><th><p>Constraints</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>C++Constructor</p></td><td><p>Operation</p></td><td><p>name = <a href="http://owner.name">owner.name</a></p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Tag definition</p></th><th><p>Type and default value</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>explicit</p></td><td><p>boolean[1]=false</p></td><td><pre>Constructor <em>Explicit</em> </pre><pre> explicit a(); </pre></td></tr><tr><td><p>initialization list</p></td><td><p>String[0..1]</p></td><td><p>Constructor initialization:</p><pre> a() : x(1) {} </pre></td></tr></tbody></table><h4>C++Destructor</h4><p>Stereotype <em>«C++Destructor»</em> is used to define C++ destructor. This stereotype extends stereotype <em>«C++Operation»</em>. </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th>Constraints</th></tr><tr><td>C++Destructor</td><td>Operation</td><td><pre>name = “~”+owner.name</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span>C++ class constructor and destructor are mapped to UML operation with stereotypes <em>«C++Constructor»</em> and «C++Destructor»:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f14487e8-6d8d-49cd-a0fc-d4e2c5c9c94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e3b8857-322a-481f-a4ed-f593cd63f3b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class ConstructClass { 
public: 
 ConstructClass(); 
 ~ConstructClass(); 
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-4-40.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Variable</h3><p><span>C++ variable is mapped to UML <em>attribute</em>, the variable type is mapped to the attribute type.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bcc63f0-17d8-45c7-b904-be4c3f040628"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7ba6363-cd90-49da-9ad2-426edd7bb826"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[int age;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model specification:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-10-0.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Pointer and reference <br /></span></h4><p>C++ type <em>pointer</em> and <em>reference</em> is mapped to property <em>Type Modifier</em> of the attribute. Character <em>$ </em>is replaced by the type name. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8b1b28a-eccd-4572-b75f-aa1ebd2841ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d73c980d-21fa-49fb-a93f-4c88f4f45eb2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[int* ptrVar; 
int& refVar]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-11-35.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Pointer mapping with property <em>Type Modifier $*</em></h6><p><em><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-23-47.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></em></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Reference mapping with property </span><em><em style="text-align: center;">Type Modifier $&amp;</em><br /></em></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Array </h4><p><span>C++ array type is mapped to </span><span>array </span><span>tag value of the attribute. If </span><span>array </span><span>is set, then multiplicity property of UML attribute is set to “[0..*]”</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0637788-76c1-47bf-9b68-ee866fa77b70"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67c2932c-f5d0-4691-b6b8-16ebbaea4d70"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[int arrayVar[5];]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-26-56.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">&lt;&lt;C++Attribute&gt;&gt; Tag <em>arrayVar</em> value is 5</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Mutable variable modifiers</h3><p><span>Mutable variable modifiers are mapped to UML attribute’s language property </span><em>Mutable</em><span>. </span></p><p><span>Constraint: only member variable can be <em>Mutable</em> (<em>Global variable</em> cannot be <em>Mutable).</em> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aace1d78-d184-4988-9c72-bc491ae88d77"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="160ad51e-ef28-49a5-bb43-237e6a72d52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mutable int x;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-28-48.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">When variable is mutable, its Tag <em>mutable</em> value is true</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Bit field</h3><p><span>Bit field is mapped to tag value </span><em>Bit field</em><span>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f32feca-078b-4431-b58b-6005ce518f97"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d966d3a3-0331-4912-8ba0-ab65614ebc0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[struct BitStruct { 
 int a:2; 
}; ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="113"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_15-38-45.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>UML model Tag specification:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="bitfield.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag <em>bit field </em>with value 2</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;"><br />Variable Extern</span></p><p><span>C++ extern variable is mapped to stereotype <em>«C++Extern»</em>. Tag <em>Linkage</em> value is used to specify the kind of linkage <em>C</em> or <em>C++</em>, if <em>linkage</em> is not specified (or without value), extern variable without <em>linkage</em> is generated.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9c8d3a7-dd0e-49ae-a921-50c3fe4a86e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c1e42a7-8aa9-4599-a43a-d10af68a38ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[extern int externVar;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="113"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_15-40-31.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span><br />Variable Default Value</span></h3><p><span>Variable initial value is mapped to UML attribute’s default value. Variable initial value set using function style method is mapped to UML attribute’s default value and attribute’s </span>language property Tag <em>Abbreviated Initialization</em> set to true.</p><p>Constraint: only <em>static const</em> member variables can be initialized, and they cannot be initialized using function style method. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e4210e2-0880-4916-8cca-f5806b9839af"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35b6170c-e02e-4d6a-8d3b-a4ba7134d6ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[int var = 5; 
int var2(10);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-38-21.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Variable initial value is mapped to UML attribute’s default value</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-39-25.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag <em>Abbreviated Initialization</em> set to true.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Const Volatile Qualified Type</h3><p><span><em>C++ const</em> and volatile modifiers for attribute/function parameter are mapped to </span><span>Type Modifiers </span><span>properties. </span></p><p>For attribute <em>const</em>, the property <strong>Is Read Only</strong> is set to <em>true</em> during reverse.</p><p>The character <em>$</em> in <strong>Type Modifier</strong> value is replaced by the type name.</p><p>Constraint : If the property <strong>Is Read Only</strong> is set and <strong>Type Modifiers</strong> is not set to <em>const</em> or <em>const volatile - </em>set to const, or an error message will display during syntax check.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de2d99f8-54b8-49cd-8eb3-5fa7d697ec13"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ab6b95e-70b5-466b-b75e-650561106852"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class CVModifiers { 
const int* const constAttribute; 
} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="98"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_15-42-35.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-41-57.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag <em>bit field </em>with value 2</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Function</h3><p><span>C++ function is mapped to UML operation, parameter of function is mapped to UML parameter with property </span><span>direction </span><span>set to <em>inout</em>, return type of function is mapped to UML parameter with property </span><span>direction </span><span>set to <em>return</em>. Type of parameter is mapped to type of UML parameter. </span></p><p>C++ default parameter value is mapped to <em>defaultValue</em> property of UML parameter.</p><p>Pointer, reference and array type of parameter are mapped to property <strong>Type Modifier</strong> of parameter. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd783214-dbf7-493c-8056-1de8a898f074"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87acc749-9b42-4e98-82d4-d699ed9be99d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[float paramFunc(int x, char x);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-44-13.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Return type of function</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span><br />Function Variable-Length Parameter List</span></h3><p><span>C++ function variable-length parameter list is mapped to a UML parameter with name “...” and without type.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="952058a7-47e2-49fc-8b94-82e67caa34bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="482aca64-982e-4774-895c-79c775163047"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Class FunctionClass { 
public: 
 EllipsisFunc(radius); 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_16-53-28.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Void Parameter</h3><p><span>C++ void function parameter is mapped to a UML parameter without name and with type <em>void</em>. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="655079f2-b164-41bf-8825-413c3941a814"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dbcc51ca-503e-409c-bd56-8da0fb089ff8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Class FunctionClass { 
public: 
 void voidParam(void); 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="72"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_15-49-49.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_17-1-7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Operation with type void</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><h3><span>Register Parameter</span></h3><p><span>C++ register parameter is mapped to UML parameter language property <strong>Register Depending</strong> on the compiler, register can be limited on some types (int, char).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5ef2d04-609d-4866-a04e-f9e90473a201"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c90d308-e555-443e-81ab-90167aa2f54d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class RegisterParamClass {       
void registerParam(register int x); 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="93"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_15-55-52.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter x.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag <em>register</em></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span><br />Function Modifiers</span></h3><p><span>C++ function modifiers are mapped to Language properties of Operation. </span></p><p><span>Virtual function is mapped to </span><em>Virtual modifier </em><span>property. </span></p><p><span>Inline function is mapped to </span><em>Inline modifier </em><span>property. </span></p><p><span>Explicit function is mapped to </span><em>Explicit modifier </em><span>property. Constraint: explicit is only valid for constructor. </span></p><p><span>Const function is mapped to UML operation </span><em>Is Query </em><span>property. </span></p><p>Volatile function is mapped to Tag value <em>volatile</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d172c82b-4c3f-4e3e-9565-c8a12c8d8cde"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8369bb3a-ef1a-40c1-8767-ed66b4eafdaa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class FuncModifierClass {
      explicit FuncModifierClass(); 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-6-5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag <em>explicit</em></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Function Pointer</h3><p><span>C++ function pointer type is mapped to attribute/parameter with «C++FunctionPtr» stereotype, a dependency with «C++BaseType» stereotype link from the attribute/parameter to the operation in a «C++FunctionSignature» class, and type modifiers of the dependency is set to <em>*$</em>. </span>Member function pointer use the same mapping, and member class tag of «C++BaseType» stereotype point to a class. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aebc878-b0d5-4212-b62e-947e44fad356"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="488fa1d0-8ad0-416d-97f1-04707efdd412"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[float (A*funcPtr)(int);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-6-5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag <em>explicit</em></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3>Function Operator</h3><p><span>C++ function operator is mapped to normal function with the C++ operator name mapped to UML operation name. See </span><strong>C++Operator </strong><span>for more info.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf0d52dd-fd0d-454f-b75c-507893664c34"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d1f2dac-1216-4288-8f6c-9b5d5f8af47d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Class Op { 
Public: 
 Op operator+(Op x); 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="93"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-8-26.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><h3><span>Exception </span></h3><p><span>C++ exception is mapped to UML operation’s </span><span>raised <em>exception </em></span><span>properties. </span></p><p><span>If </span><em>raisedExpression </em><span>is empty, and </span><em>throw exception </em><span>tag is set to </span><em>none </em><span>a throw without parameter is generated. </span></p><p><span>If </span><em>raisedExpression </em><span>is empty, and </span><span>throw exception </span><span>tag is set to </span><em>any </em><span>throw keyword is not generated. </span></p><p><span>If the tag </span><em>throw exception </em><span>is not set, then generate specific </span><em>raisedExpression</em><span>, or do not generate throw if </span><em>raisedExpression </em><span>is empty. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="260d766c-1445-4154-b792-9e80ea9c0c07"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5216884-5012-4617-8305-c34245076ef7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[void throwFunc() throw (int,char);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="throwFunc.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property<em> throw exception</em></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Visibility<span> </span></h3><p><span>Variables and function visibility are mapped using the UML </span><span>visibility </span><span>property. </span></p><p><span>Members of C++ class without access visibility specified are private. </span></p><p><span>Members of C++ struct or union without access visibility specified are public. </span></p><p><span>Variables and functions outside a class/struct/union are public. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1afdc3e9-0310-49da-aa9e-5dbb8fb117c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="310cfb47-bc80-4630-9a3b-d271d4f79134"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class ClassVisibility { 
 int privateVar; 
protected: 
 int protectedVar; 
public: 
 int publicVar; 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="114"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-11-40.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><h3><span>Static members</span></h3><p><span>Static variables and functions are mapped to UML </span><em>Is Static </em><span>property.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b7a59f1-a4d7-4454-8360-20023977fa59"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f57f85b8-ca6e-4674-9bc9-9fbd888cfa63"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class StaticClass { 
 static int staticVar; 
 static void staticFunc(); 
}; ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="114"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-15-39.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model Tag specification:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="static.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property <em>Is Static</em></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><span>Pure virtual function and abstract class</span></h3><p><span>Pure virtual C++ function is mapped to UML operation with property </span><em>Is Abstract </em><span>set to true. If one or more functions are abstract in a class, the property </span><em>Is Abstract </em><span>of the UML class is set to true. </span>Constraint: if no operation is abstract, the class cannot be abstract. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48448b41-d094-4b7e-a0de-55fd72b0218e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fdf47d2-b45e-4365-b124-00bb678af39a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class AbstractClass { 
 virtual abstractOperation()=0; 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="88"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-15-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span>UML model Tag specification:</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="abstractOperation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Operation property Is Abstract</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span><br />Friend Declaration</span></h3><p><span>C++ friend function is mapped with a «C++Friend» stereotyped dependency relationship between the function (an UML operation) and the friendClass. This relationship grants the friendship to the friendClass. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b69f576b-be30-4697-a43d-363036e5dc57"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f6a165f-55a2-4dc7-880d-9708f6675abf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class ClassB { 
public: 
 friend void friendFunc(); 
}; 
void friendFunc();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-34-29.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>C++ friend member function is mapped with a «C++Friend» stereotyped dependency relationship between the member function and the friend class. This relationship grants the friendship to the friend class.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4252b52c-9f97-46be-9be9-0afb50ad6660"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dbef86b-53c8-4e6f-a50d-8ec6916fba5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class ClassD {
void func(ClassC c);
};
class ClassC {
    friend void ClassD::func(ClassC
c);
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-40-31.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>C++ friend class are mapped with a «C++Friend» stereotyped dependency relationship between the class and the friend class. This relationship grants the friendship to the friend class. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70d59545-cfad-4356-81a6-4f9f64587a28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a39284d8-bdb4-40c9-85a2-4f95e80fe2f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class FriendClass {
public:
 friend class ClassA;
};
class ClassA {
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-10_17-33-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><h3><span>Struct</span></h3><p><span>C++ struct are mapped to a UML class with stereotype «C++Struct». See </span><strong>C++Struct </strong><span>for more info.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9ed3016-f1a2-4d37-ae3f-485ee8909cf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Current Modeling tool </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>The current version of MD use class’s language property “Class Key” </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07634a7d-35d6-4c88-8acf-0c074ee2eff7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c8a9c12-091c-4e02-bfde-31f4bf1399b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[struct MyStruc {
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="107"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-41-58.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><span>Union</span></h3><p><span>C++ union is mapped to a UML class with stereotype «C++Union». See </span><span>C++Union </span><span>for more info.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06dea24d-9737-43e6-bccd-9db9f537903d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Current Modeling tool </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>The current version of MD use class’s language property “Class Key”</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcf39a5f-541c-40f8-9d49-c190fbbb89ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cabc73eb-b5ab-4433-95b8-7bc7c2a0bad8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[union MyUnion { 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="107"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-43-0.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span><br />Enumeration</span></h3><p><span>C++ enum is mapped to UML enumeration. C++ enum fields are mapped to UML enumeration literals. C++ enum field with a specified value is mapped to tag value of «C++LiteralValue» stereotype. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81235274-a559-4a97-9a8f-9c80be6fb600"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8c845a1-f848-47e4-8a23-1b7f96d1ef03"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[enum Day { 
     Mon, 
     Tue=2 
}; ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="96"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_16-56-12.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><h3><span>Typedef</span></h3><p><span>C++ typedef is mapped to a class with «C++Typedef» stereotype. A «C++BaseType» dependency links to the original type. </span>Type modifiers tag of «C++BaseType» dependency is used to define type modifiers. <em>$</em> character is replaced by the type name. A typedef on a function pointer is mapped by linking a «C++BaseType» dependency to an operation and type modifiers tag of «C++BaseType» dependency is set to <em>*$</em>. Operation signature can be stored in a «C++FunctionSignature» class. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="876c4957-55d2-4967-bd3d-392b968ecd6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8a244a1-bd6d-4c05-b231-cc0728eb9c31"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[typedef int UINT32;
typedef int* INT_PTR;
typedef double (*funcPtrType)(int, char);
; ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-23_14-54-15.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Namespace</h3><p><span>C++ namespace is mapped to a UML package with the stereotype «C++Namespace». </span><span> </span>Unnamed namespace is named unnamed+index number of unnamed namespace (start at 1), and unique namespace name tag is set to the source file path+:+index number of unnamed namespace (start at 0). </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbb30c9f-9e9a-4e50-81f3-05462327bf40"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[namespace n {     
     namespace m {
     }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><span>Global Functions And Variables</span></h3><p><span>Global functions and variables are mapped to operations and attributes into an unnamed class with stereotype «C++Global». «C++Global» class resides in its respective namespace, or in a top package. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a355894-d2e8-4ab3-9637-22d414274032"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73915bfc-e3e0-4aad-931f-2149d11cc5f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[int var; 
int func(int x);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_17-3-5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f3bb818-526a-471e-9077-c23eee68c754"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15cad4c2-195b-46e3-b27b-05401d379561"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[namespace std { 
    int err;
    void printf(); 
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_17-1-54.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><span>Class Definition</span></h3><p><span>Variables can be created after a class/struct/union declaration. These variables are mapped to UML attribute, and placed in their respective namespace/global/class container. </span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d329f8e-258f-402d-9b9b-cef258e9b1a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b67a4503-ab2d-4038-921b-868a3d474632"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class VarInitClass { 
} c, d; 
class OuterVarInit {  
     class InnerVarInit { 
     } e; 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_17-12-40.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><span>Class Template Definition</span></h3><p><span>C++ template class is mapped to UML class with template parameters properties added. Type of template parameter is always set to UML Class. To generate/reverse typename keyword, </span><span>type keyword </span>tag is set to typename. </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64cdc613-0185-4ccc-9657-7a7c2118709b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80a884b8-c1ae-4880-a972-8b9d9de44d23"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T> 
class simpleTemplate { 
}; 
template <typename T> 
class TypeNameTemplate { 
}; ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_9-44-14.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_9-57-38.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Template Parameters</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-13-33.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Template Parameter Tag type keyword.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span><br />Function Template Definition</span></h3><p><span>C++ template function is mapped to UML operation with template parameters properties added. </span>C++ template function overload is mapped to a normal function. (the same name with the same number of parameter, but different type of parameter) New style of template function overloading is mapped to a normal function. (the same name with the same number of parameter, but different type of parameter) and a template binding relationship is created between the overload operation and the template operation, with specific template parameter substitutions. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50a65a36-67a4-4623-96c1-3cae9bf46444"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06b489d5-c31a-4487-a0b1-2fff6a4099c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T> 
void simpleFunc(T x); 
// overload old style 
void simpleFunc(int x); 
// overload new style 
template<> 
void simpleFunc<char>(char x);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="114"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-12_10-6-11.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-23_15-10-43.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Template Parameters</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3> Default Template Parameter</h3><p><span>C++ default template parameter is mapped to UML default template parameters. Instantiation using the default template parameter is mapped using a template binding relationship with an </span>empty actual property for the template parameter substitution. </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="123a7dc1-78d2-49de-a379-771745156d04"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T=int> 
class defaultTemplate { 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><span>Template Instantiation</span></h3><p><span>Template instantiation are mapped to template binding relationship between the template class and the instantiate class, the </span><span>template parameter substitution </span><span>of the binding relationship is set using the template argument. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8bcc63-6899-4bc9-9963-362bb5a18e57"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T> 
class simpleTemplate { 
}; 
simpleTemplate<int> 
simpleTemplateInstance; ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">For template argument using template instantiation as argument, an intermediate class is created with the specific binding.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45a5a2c0-d831-4998-a926-2907e32ecbab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T> 
class T1Class { 
}; 
template <class T> 
class T2Class { 
}; 
T1Class<T2Class<int>> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span>For template argument using multiple template instantiations in an inner class (b&lt;int&gt;::c&lt;char&gt;), the intermediate class instance is created in the outer class instance. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7051e251-3e3a-4cd3-b9d9-2bc78f90a0bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T> 
class b { 
 template <class T> 
 class c { 
 }; 
}; 
b<int>::c<char> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span>Example of complex template instantiation. Containment relationship are placed on diagram for information only, these relationships are not created during a reverse process. Containment relationship is modeled by placing a class into a specific class/package. See Containment tree below the diagram. </span></p><h3><span>Partial Template Instantiation</span></h3><p>C++ partial template instantiation use the same mapping as Template Instantiation and the unbinded parameter is binded to the specific template parameter class. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="977d4cac-a9ad-4000-b80e-6f1838178508"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T,class U,class V> 
class PT {}; 
template <class A,class B> 
class PT<B, int, A> {};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Template Specialization </h3><p><span>C++ Template specialization uses the same mapping as Template Instantiation. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ba2a038-89a6-41cf-a343-4d6455b9cb29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[template <class T> 
class TS {}; 
template <> 
class TS<int> {};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Forward Class Declaration</h3><p><span>The example code is declared in A.h file. The file component A.h has the «use» association applied by «C++Include» stereotype with <em>Class Forward</em> tag value. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bf67ee1-156b-43e2-8790-0735b53c9e29"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95a8344a-6a98-4586-a9f2-2d00cd8978ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[class C; 
class A { 
 private: 
 C* c; 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-30-44.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-41-13.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Template Parameters</h6><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-34-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>header include value <em>Class forward</em></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><span>Include Declaration</span></h3><p><span>The example code is declared in A.h file. The «use» association is also applied to C++Include stereotype shown in <strong>Forward class declaration</strong>. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4924d110-06cb-421d-b70e-d6f05d734990"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8e861cb-66ca-4913-882d-58e3b4d3b406"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#include "B.h" 
#include <E.h> 
class A { 
 private: 
 B* b; 
 E* e; 
};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="left"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-39-27.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="white-space: normal;">Specification for #include &quot;B.h&quot;:</span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-41-13.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">User Include</h6><p><span style="white-space: normal;">Specification for #include &lt;E.h&gt;: </span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-42-15.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>System include</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The example code is declared in D.cpp file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a2dd5d6-e3d4-4e0b-a906-7bfc82db3e3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>C++ source code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e321a647-d511-41b9-b3df-c4c774a6574f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#include <E.h> 
class B; 
class D { 
private:B* b; 
E* e; 
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">UML model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-43-52.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="white-space: normal;">Specification for #include &quot;B.h&quot;:</span></p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-45-20.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">User Include</h6><p><span style="white-space: normal;">Specification for #include &lt;E.h&gt;: </span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-11_18-44-44.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C++ Mapping to UML" ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">System include</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><pre><br /></pre>
````

## PAGE 00126: C++ Stereotypes

- page_id: `227154593`
- space_key: `MD2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154593/C+Stereotypes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:18:23.106+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering > C++ ANSI Profile
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Here are the Stereotypes presented throughout the page:

1209660665

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Here are the Stereotypes presented throughout the page:

1209660668

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Here are the Stereotypes presented throughout the page:

1209660667

All C++ stereotypes are derived from «C++Element».

Constraints described in this chapter are for information only; syntax of these constraints must be checked with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object Constraint Language' space='MD2024xR3'] interpreter.

Invisible stereotypes *«C++Class»*, *«C++Operation»*, *«C++Parameter»*, *«C++Attribute»*, *«C++LiteralValue»*, *«C++Include»*, *«C++Generalization»*, and *«C++TemplateParameter»* are used only to store C++ language properties. These stereotypes and their tag definitions are used by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Domain Specific Language customization' space='MD2024xR3'] framework.

#### EXPAND: Here are the Stereotypes presented throughout the page:

Here are the Stereotypes presented throughout the page:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### C++ Stereotypes

##### C++Operation

«C++Operation» is an invisible stereotype used to include language properties for any C++ operation.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Operation | Operation | Function Const void f() const;Constraint: Only valid for member function (member functions are operators that are declared as members of a class) if isQuery then stereotype->select(name=’C++Global’)->isEmpty() |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Descriptions |
| inline | boolean[1]=false | Function Inline inline a(); |
| throw exception | C++ThrowType[1]=any | Exception specification: If operation.raisedExpression is not empty, the expression throw is generated. void f() throw(int); If operation.raisedExpression is empty and expression throw valueis none, then a expression throw without argument is generated. void f() throw () If operation.raisedExpression is empty and expression throw valueis any, it does not generate a Keyword throw. void f(); |
| virtual | boolean[1]=false | Function Virtual virtual a(); Constraint: Only valid for member function and non static stereotype- >select(name=’C++Global’) ->isEmpty() and IsStatic =false |
| volatile | boolean[1]=false | Function Volatile void f() volatile; |
| funtionTryBlock | boolean[1]=false | Function try block void f() try{} |

##### C++Operator

Stereotype *«C++Operator»* is used to define a C++ operator function. This stereotype extends the stereotype «C++Operation».

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Operator | Operation | Function Operator T& operator+(T& a);Constraint: the name start with word operator |

##### C++Constructor

Stereotype *«C++Constructor»* is used to define C++ Constructor. This stereotype extends the stereotype*«C++Operation»*.

| Name | Meta class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Constructor | Operation | name = owner.name |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| explicit | boolean[1]=false | Constructor Explicit explicit a(); |
| initialization list | String[0..1] | Constructor initialization: a() : x(1) {} |

##### C++Destructor

Stereotype *«C++Destructor»* is used to define C++ destructor. This stereotype extends Stereotype *«C++Operation»*.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Destructor | Operation | name = “~”+owner.name |

##### C++Parameter

«C++Parameter» is an invisible stereotype used to include language properties for any C++ function parameter.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Parameter | Parameter |  |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| C Type declaration | boolean[1]=false | Declare parameter’s type in C style: void a(enum Day x); C++ style: void a(Day x); |
| Register | boolean[1]=false | Paramer Register void a(register int x); |
| Array | String[0..1] | C++ Array definition void a(int x[2][2]); |

##### C++ Attribute

An invisible stereotype*«C++Attribute»* is used to include language properties for any C++ variable.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Attribute | Property | Constraint for code generation: it is valid to have a default value for any kind of attribute, but it is illegal to initialize a member variable within its definition. class A { int x = 1; }; if defaultValue.size() > 0 then owner.stereotype- >exists(name='C++Global') or (isStatic = true and typeModifiers.contains(“const”)) |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Descriptions |
| abbreviated initialization | boolean[1]=false | Initialize the attribute with the abbreviated form: int x(5); Constraint owner.stereotype->exists(name='C++Global') |
| bit field | String[0..1] | Bit field int x:2; Constraint: Only valid for member function stereotype->select(name=’C++Global’) ->isEmpty() |
| c type declaration | boolean[1]=false | Declare attribute’s type in C style C style: enum Day x; C++ style: Day x; |
| container | String[0..1] | container of the attribute. $ character is replaced by the attribute type. vector<$> x; |
| mutable | boolean[1]=false | Attribute mutable modifier. mutable int x; Constraint: Only valid for member function stereotype->select(name=’C++Global’) ->isEmpty() |
| array | String[0..1] | C++ array definition int x[2][2]; |

##### C++LiteralValue

An invisible stereotype *«C++LiteralValue»* is used to include language properties for any C++ enum field.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++LiteralValue | EnumerationLiteral |  |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Descriptions |
| value | String[0..1] | Value definition of an enum field:(A valid C++ expression)enum Day {Mon = 2}; |

##### C++Friend

Stereotype *«C++Friend»* is used to define C++ friend relationship.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Friend | Dependency | Client is Class or Operation and supplier is Class (client.oclIsTypeOf(Class) or client.oclIsTypeOf(Operation)) andsupplier.oclIsTypeOf(Class) |

##### C++Struct

Stereotype *«C++Struct»* is used to define C++ struct.

| Name | Meta Class |
| --- | --- |
| C++Struct | Class |

##### C++Typedef

Stereotype *«C++Typedef»* is used to define C++ typedef.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Typedef | Class | A typedef does not contain an operation and attribute feature->isEmpty() A «C++BaseType» dependency is defined |

##### C++Union

Stereotype *«C++Union»* is used to define C++ union.

| Name | Meta Class |
| --- | --- |
| C++Union | Class |

##### C++Global

Stereotype *«C++Global»* is used to define global functions and variables (functions and variables outside a class/struct/union declaration).

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Global | Class | Only one «C++Global» class into a package owner.ownedElement->select( stereotype->select(name=’C++Global’)).size()=1 All operations and attributes are public feature->forAll(visibility = #public) |

##### C++Namespace

Stereotype *«C++Namespace»* is used to define a C++ namespace.

| Name | Meta class |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Namespace | Package |  |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| unique namespace name | String[0..1] | Unnamed namespace namespace {} |

##### C++Extern

Stereotype *«C++Extern»* is used to define C++ extern variable.

| Name | Meta class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Extern | Operation, Property | owner.stereotype->exists(name=’C++Global’) |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| linkage | String[0..1] | Specification Linkage: extern “C” |

##### C++FunctionPtr

Stereotype *«C++FunctionPtr»* is used to define C++ function pointer.

| Name | Meta class |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++FunctionPtr | Parameter, Property |  |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| signature | Operation | The signature of the function (C++ function pointer definition without the operation name) |
| member class | Class | The class used for pointer to member function. |

##### C++FunctionSignature

Stereotype*«C++FunctionSignature»* is used as a container to model C++ function pointer.

| Name | Meta class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++FunctionSignature | Class | The class cannot have properties. properties->isEmpty() |

##### C++Class

Stereotype «C++Class» is an invisible stereotype used to include language properties for any C++ variable.

| Name | Meta class |
| --- | --- |
| C++Class | Class |

##### C++BaseType

Stereotype «C++BaseType» is used to link base type of a typedef.

| Name | Meta class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++BaseType | Dependency | Client is type of Class with stereotype «C++Typedef». |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| type modifiers | String[0..1] | Type modifiers of the typedef or function pointer. |
| member class | Class[0..1] | Memberclass of typedef or function pointer. |
| array | String[0..1] | Array definition. |

##### C++Include

Stereotype «C++Include» is used to keep information about the include type, for generating include and forward class declaration.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Include | Association, Dependency, Generalization, Parameter, Property, TemplateBinding, TemplateParameter | Client is type of Component |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| header include | String | The value of the tag can be one of the followingNoneUser IncludeSystem IncludeClass Forward |
| implementation include | String | The value of the tag can be one of the followingNone User IncludeSystem IncludeClass Forward |

The*header include*tag is used when the client component has header file extension, “*.h”; otherwise, the tag*implementation include*will be used.

The *User Include* tag value is used for generating user include, such as #include “test.h”.

The *System Include* tag value is used for generating system include, such as #include <string.h>

The *Class Forward* tag value is used for generating forward class declaration.

##### C++TemplateParameter

Stereotype*«C++Template Parameter»* is used to keep type keyword between class and typename for template parameter declaration.

| Name | Meta Class |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++TemplateParameter | TemplateParameter |  |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| type keyword | C++TemplateTypeKeyword=class | The value of tag is one of the following classtypename |

##### C++Generalization

Stereotype *«C++Generalization»* is used for information related to generalization and interface realization.

| Name | Meta Class |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++Generalization | Generalization, InterfaceRealization |  |
| Tag definition | Type and default value | Description |
| Inheritance Visibility | C++GeneralizationVisibility[1] = none | The value of tag can be one of the following Nonepublicprotected privateExample: class A : private B {}; |
| Virtual Inheritance | boolean[1]=false |  |

class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 39.386974%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 23.976608%;" /><col style="width: 31.578947%;" /><col style="width: 44.444443%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th>Constraints</th></tr><tr><td><p>C++LiteralValue</p></td><td><p>EnumerationLiteral</p></td><td><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th>Descriptions</th></tr><tr><td>value</td><td>String[0..1]</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Value definition of an enum field:</p><p>(A valid C++ expression)</p><pre>enum Day {Mon = 2};</pre></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Friend</h3><p><span> Stereotype <em>«C++Friend»</em> is used to define C++ friend relationship. </span><span> </span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th>Constraints</th></tr><tr><td>C++Friend</td><td>Dependency</td><td><p><span>Client is Class or Operation and supplier is Class </span></p><pre>(client.oclIsTypeOf(Class) or </pre><pre>client.oclIsTypeOf(Operation)) and</pre><pre>supplier.oclIsTypeOf(Class)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Struct</h3><p><span>Stereotype <em>«C++Struct»</em> is used to define C++ struct. </span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 22.758621%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 32.432434%;" /><col style="width: 33.445946%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th></tr><tr><td><p>C++Struct</p></td><td><p>Class</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">C++Typedef</h3><p><span>Stereotype <em>«C++Typedef»</em> is used to define C++ typedef. </span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 40.38314%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 18.494534%;" /><col style="width: 18.051926%;" /><col style="width: 63.456512%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th>Constraints</th></tr><tr><td><p>C++Typedef</p></td><td><p>Class</p></td><td><p><span>A typedef does not contain an operation and attribute </span></p><pre><span> feature-&gt;isEmpty() </span></pre><p><span>A «C++BaseType» dependency is defined</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Union</h3><p><span>Stereotype <em>«C++Union»</em> is used to define C++ union. </span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 22.222221%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 30.795847%;" /><col style="width: 33.564014%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th></tr><tr><td><p>C++Union</p></td><td><p>Class</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Global</h3><p>Stereotype <em>«C++Global»</em> is used to define global functions and variables (functions and variables outside a class/struct/union declaration). </p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 46.0779%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 15.3754%;" /><col style="width: 18.236%;" /><col style="width: 66.3886%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th>Constraints</th></tr><tr><td><p>C++Global</p></td><td><p>Class</p></td><td><p>Only one «C++Global» class into a package</p><pre>     owner.ownedElement-&gt;select(</pre><pre>         stereotype-&gt;select(name=’C++Global’)).size()=1 </pre><p>All operations and attributes are public</p><pre>     feature-&gt;forAll(visibility = #public)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Namespace</h3><p><span>Stereotype <em>«C++Namespace»</em> is used to define a C++ namespace. </span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 21.52195%;" /><col style="width: 24.810787%;" /><col style="width: 53.667263%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Name </span></p></th><th><p><span>Meta class </span></p></th><th><p><br /></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>C++Namespace </span></p></td><td><p><span>Package </span></p></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th><p><span>Description </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>unique namespace name </span></p></td><td><p><span>String[0..1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Unnamed namespace </span></p><pre>namespace {} </pre></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Extern</h3><p><span>Stereotype <em>«C++Extern»</em> is used to define C++ extern variable. </span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 21.503448%;" /><col style="width: 24.813793%;" /><col style="width: 53.68276%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Name </span></p></th><th><p><span>Meta class </span></p></th><th><p><span>Constraints </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>C++Extern </span></p></td><td><p><span>Operation, Property </span></p></td><td><pre>owner.stereotype-&gt;exists(name=’C++Global’) </pre></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th><p><span>Description </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>linkage </span></p></td><td><p><span>String[0..1] </span></p></td><td><p><span><span>Specification </span><em>Linkage:</em> </span></p><pre>extern “C” </pre></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++FunctionPtr</h3><p><span>Stereotype <em>«C++FunctionPtr»</em> is used to define C++ function pointer. </span></p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 54.1415%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 23.428%;" /><col style="width: 18.357%;" /><col style="width: 58.215%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Name </span></p></th><th><p><span>Meta class </span></p></th><th><p><br /></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>C++FunctionPtr </span></p></td><td><p><span>Parameter, Property </span></p></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th><p><span>Description </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>signature</span></p></td><td><p><span>Operation</span></p></td><td><p><span>The signature of the function (C++ function pointer definition without the operation name) </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>member class</span></p></td><td><p><span>Class</span></p></td><td><p><span>The class used for pointer to member function. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++FunctionSignature</h3><p><span>Stereotype<em> «C++FunctionSignature»</em> is used as a container to model C++ function pointer. </span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 23.539946%;" /><col style="width: 18.608816%;" /><col style="width: 57.85124%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Name </span></p></th><th><p><span>Meta class </span></p></th><th><p><span>Constraints </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>C++FunctionSignature </span></p></td><td><p><span>Class</span></p></td><td><p><span>The class cannot have properties. </span></p><pre><span> properties-&gt;isEmpty() </span></pre></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Class</h3><p><span>Stereotype «C++Class» is an invisible stereotype used to include language properties for any C++ variable. </span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 23.579582%;" /><col style="width: 18.599531%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Name </span></p></th><th><p><span>Meta class </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>C++Class </span></p></td><td><p><span>Class </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++BaseType</h3><p><span>Stereotype «C++BaseType» is used to link base type of a typedef. </span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 23.612068%;" /><col style="width: 18.5425%;" /><col style="width: 57.845432%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Name </span></p></th><th><p><span>Meta class </span></p></th><th><p><span>Constraints </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>C++BaseType </span></p></td><td><p><span>Dependency </span></p></td><td><p><span>Client is type of Class with stereotype <em>«C++Typedef»</em>. </span></p></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th><p><span>Description </span></p></th></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>type modifiers</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>String[0..1]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Type modifiers of the typedef or function pointer.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>member class</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Class[0..1]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Memberclass of typedef or function pointer.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>array</p></td><td><p>String[0..1]</p></td><td><p>Array definition.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Include</h3><p><span>Stereotype «C++Include» is used to keep information about the include type, for generating include and forward class declaration. </span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 77.35849%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 25.777779%;" /><col style="width: 44.666668%;" /><col style="width: 29.555555%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th>Constraints</th></tr><tr><td><p><span>C++Include </span></p></td><td><p><span>Association, Dependency, Generalization, Parameter, Property, TemplateBinding, TemplateParameter</span></p></td><td><p><span>Client is type of Component </span></p></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><span>header include</span></p></td><td>String</td><td><p><span>The value of the tag can be one of the following</span></p><ul><li>None</li><li>User Include</li><li>System Include</li><li>Class Forward</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>implementation include</span></p></td><td>String</td><td><p><span>The value of the tag can be one of the following</span></p><ul><li><span>None </span></li><li>User Include</li><li>System Include</li><li>Class Forward</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span>The </span><em>header include </em><span>tag is used when the client component has header file extension, “*.h”; otherwise, the tag </span><em>implementation include </em><span>will be used.</span></p><p>The <em>User Include</em> tag value is used for generating user include, such as #include “test.h”.</p><p>The <em>System Include</em> tag value is used for generating system include, such as #include &lt;string.h&gt;</p><p>The <em>Class Forward</em> tag value is used for generating forward class declaration. </p><h3>C++TemplateParameter</h3><p><span>Stereotype<em> «C++Template Parameter»</em> is used to keep type keyword between class and typename for template parameter declaration. </span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th><br /></th></tr><tr><td>C++TemplateParameter</td><td>TemplateParameter</td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>type keyword</td><td><p><span>C++TemplateTypeKeyword=class</span></p></td><td><p><span>The value of tag is one of the following </span></p><ul><li><span>class</span></li><li><span>typename</span></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>C++Generalization</h3><p><span>Stereotype <em>«C++Generalization»</em> is used for information related to generalization and interface realization.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Meta Class</th><th><br /></th></tr><tr><td>C++Generalization</td><td><p><span>Generalization, InterfaceRealization</span></p></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><th>Tag definition</th><th>Type and default value</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Inheritance Visibility</td><td><p><span>C++GeneralizationVisibility[1] = none </span></p></td><td><p><span>The value of tag can be one of the following </span></p><ul><li><span>None</span></li><li><span>public</span></li><li><span>protected </span></li><li>private</li></ul><p>Example: class A : private B {};</p></td></tr><tr><td>Virtual Inheritance</td><td><p><span>boolean[1]=false</span></p></td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5353adf-b54f-4faf-b936-90c4c315a45e"><ac:parameter
